Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing Data Store Memory, Selector, and Assignment blocks
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Data Store Memory block, a Selector block, and an Assignment block. A Data Store Read block is connected to the index port of the Selector block. The same Data Store Read block is connected to the index port of the Assignment block. The output of the Selector block is connected to the data port (U ) of the Assignment block.

Summary: ImportOptions setvaropts function errors with string array inputs
Description: opts = detectImportOptions(filename);  opts = setvaropts(opts,["Var1","Var2"],"TreatAsMissing","NA");  Will error with "Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values."
Workaround: Use numeric inputs, or cell arrays of character vectors for setting multiple variables.opts = detectImportOptions(filename);opts = setvaropts(opts,cellstr(["Var1","Var2"]),"TreatAsMissing","NA");?or?opts = setvaropts(opts,[1 2],"TreatAsMissing","NA");?

Summary: Creating an anonymous function might result in a memory leak
Description: When the handle to an anonymous function is no longer in use, MATLAB might not free up all the memory allocated to the function handle.

Summary: Incorrect documentation for configuration parameter?Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout?(UseRowMajorAlgorithm)
Description: The documentation for configuration parameter?Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout ?(UseRowMajorAlgorithm ) contains this incorrect statement: "You can enable the Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout  configuration parameter to enable efficient algorithms, which might result in numeric differences between simulation and generated code."
Workaround: Refer the updated documentation link -?https://www.mathworks.com/help/simulink/gui/use-algorithms-optimized-for-row-major-array-layout.html

Summary: Stateflow charts that perform floating point operations might assert during HDL code generation in Native Floating Point mode
Description: HDL code generation for a Stateflow chart might generate an assertion when the following conditions are met: MATLAB  is set as the Action Language  in the Chart Properties dialog box The chart contains floating point operations The Floating Point IP Library  in the HDL Code Generation > Floating Point  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box is set to Native Floating Point??   The assertion looks like the following:In cast: Assertion failed: isa(aValue)   [? 0] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/builtins/sl_main/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcg_ir.so+13427972 _ZN2CG11DoubleValue4castEPKNS_5ValueE+00000086 [? 1] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+25912710  [? 2] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+25919655  [? 3] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+25921796  [? 4] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+20326342  [? 5] ../matlab/bin/glnxa64/simulink_plugins/sl_pir_cpp/../../../../bin/glnxa64/libmwcgir_hdl.so+20590166?
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: The Capacity vector and Head vector?parameters?in the Centrifugal Pump (TL) block cannot be set to Run-time
Description: In the Centrifugal Pump (TL) block, the?Capacity vector ?and?Head vector? parameters?do not currently support run-time capabilities. However, the block dialog incorrectly provides the option to choose between the?Compile-time ?or?Run-time ?settings. Selecting?Run-time ?and simulating the model results in a compilation error: Function 'fluids_functions. polynomial_fit' must be an unprotected MATLAB function. ?In fluids. thermal_liquid. pumps_motors. centrifugal_pump
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Compilation error when Dynamic compressibility is set to Off?for heat exchanger sample variants in the?thermal liquid domain
Description: This issue occurs if Dynamic compressibility ?is set to Off  in: Heat Exchanger (TL), Simple Variant Heat Exchanger (TL-TL), Simple Variant Heat Exchanger (TL-G), Simple Variant? Simple Heat Exchanger Interface (TL)  In such situations, simulating the model generates this error message: Number of equations exceeds number of variables .?
Workaround: Set Dynamic compressibility?to On.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Assertion thrown during HDL code generation from a Simulink model containing Gain blocks that are operating in "u*K" mode
Description: When performing HDL code generation from a Simulink model containing a Gain block, we throw the following assertion if the gain mode is "u*K", the input vector is unordered and the gain factor is a square matrix: Error: Evaluation of elaborate function on class hdldefaults. Gain failed with the error message: pir_core:pirudd:assertionFailed: Assertion failed: dom_pir_udd/ paramset. cpp:65:orientation. size() == 1 ?

Summary: Error when creating link with IBM DOORS Next (DNG)
Description: When you create a link from an item in MATLAB or Simulink to an item in IBM DOORS Next (formerly known as IBM DOORS Next Generation, and often abbreviated as DNG), a dialog box might appear with an error that says "Failed to parse ProjectID ". This error might occur if the IBM DOORS Next service is hosted on Jazz server 6.0.6.1 or later version. As a result of this error, you cannot create links between items in MATLAB or Simulink and items in IBM DOORS Next.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: If you set the Vehicle Body 3DOF Dual Track block Axle force?parameter to?External forces, the block incorrectly implements the lateral force signal input to the front right tire
Description: If you set the Vehicle Body 3DOF Dual Track block?Axle force? parameter to?External forces , the block incorrectly implements the signal array connected to the input port?FwF . Specifically, the block does not correctly use the array element for lateral force on the right front tire. The issue does not affect the Vehicle Body 3DOF Single Track block.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier might fail to complete normally when performing Out of bound array access detection on models containing matrix-typed data with an InitialValue
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might fail to complete normally when all of the following conditions are true: Design Error Detection is performed with?Out of bound array access   enabled A data object of matrix type has a non-empty InitialValue   specification (such as through the Model Workspace).  In some cases, analysis might fail with an "Error detection produced errors" message. In all cases, the return status of sldvrun will be 0 when this issue is applicable.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Project does not automatically rename usages of data dictionaries inside internal test harnesses
Description: Project automatic renaming does not update data dictionary names  inside internal test harnesses saved in a model. When renaming a data dictionary, in the Rename  dialog box, Project offers to automatically update all links to the data dictionary. However, the automatic renaming does not complete the expected actions :?  When the user expands the impacted model and double-clicks the Data Dictionary  line in the Rename  dialog box, Project highlights the data dictionary of the containing model instead. When the user confirms renaming the data dictionary in the Rename  dialog box, Project does not update the link in the internal test harness.
Workaround: Manually update the data dictionary name in the internal test harness.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for the cast?function might return a real matrix instead of a complex matrix with zero imaginary part
Description: If you use the 'like' ?argument of the?cast ?function in the generated code , the result might have incorrect complexity if all ?these conditions are true: The first input A  (the input array that is to be cast) is sparse, real, and numeric (not logical) The last input p  (the prototype) is complex
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Autorename fails for a Simulink bus object containing multiple bus elements.
Description: The autorename fails for a Simulink bus object that meets the following conditions: The bus object contains two or more bus elements. One of the bus elements is based on the type that is being renamed.  MATLAB stack trace: Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BaseWorkspaceFileHandler/modifyObject  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler/refactor  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.VariableRefactoringHandler/refactor  Error in dependencies.internal.action.refactor  Error in dependencies.internal.action.refactor  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Dependency.refactor?
Workaround: Manually modify the remaining instances of the bus usages.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Simulink might crash when multiple models simulate simultaneously, but the models finishing last contain string signals
Description: Simulink allows multiple unrelated models to simulate at the same time. Simulink might crash if one model finishes simulation and the remaining models contain string signals.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: MAAB check?Stateflow transition appearance incorrectly flags transitions crossing junctions
Description: The Model Advisor check?Stateflow transition appearance ? (Check ID:mathworks.maab.db_0129) incorrectly flags?transitions crossing junctions when transitions and junctions are grouped into sub-charts within the states.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: sum(x,dim) might return incorrect answers for?single?and?half?inputs?in generated code that uses row-major layout
Description: Code generated for the function  sum(x,dim) , where?dim ?is the dimension to operate along,?might return incorrect answers if all  the following conditions are true: class(x)  is 'single ' or 'half ' The generated code stores  x ?in row-major order x  is not a vector x  is not empty The dim  argument is supplied size(x,dim)  is greater than 1024  size(x,k)~=1 ?for some?k < dim   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB functions that recursively call themselves might produce incorrect code
Description: C/C++ code generation from MATLAB might produce incorrect code if all  of these conditions are true: The source MATLAB code contains a function that (directly or indirectly) calls itself? The first call to the recursive function (or the function that starts the recursion) passes a constant input to that function During recursion, the subsequent calls to that function pass different values for that input parameter?  For example, define a MATLAB function simple2 ?that calls the recursive function? recursive_fcn ? with a constant input?5 : function out = simple2 %#codegenout = recursive_fcn(5);end The function recursive_fcn ? recursively calls itself with a different input value for each call:function out = recursive_fcn(n)if n > 0    m = n - 1;    out = 0;    out = recursive_fcn(m);    out = out + do_something(n);else    out = 0;endend function out = do_something(n)out = cumsum(1,n);end The generated C code contains an infinite recursion and does not terminate. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Drive Cycle Source block produces an incompatible file error
Description: The Drive Cycle Source block produces an incompatible file error dialogue message when importing files with non-uniformly sampled time data.
Workaround: Click?OK?to accept the error. You will then see the correct error message regarding the issue with non-uniformly sampled data.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Multiple CAN FD  channel connections cannot run on the same MathWorks virtual channel
Description: You cannot simultaneously run more than one CAN FD channel connected to the same MathWorks virtual device channel.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table(n-D) block with fixed point inputs containing negative fraction length generates incorrect HDL code
Description: The generated HDL code for a Simulink model might be incorrect when the following settings are applied: The model contains a Lookup Table(n-D) block with Flat? Interpolation method The inputs are of fixed-point type with negative fractional length?, such as  sfix6_E3 numerictype(0, 6, -3)?   The incorrect HDL code can result in simulation failures in ModelSim when verifying the generated code.
Workaround: Avoid negative fraction lengths as input type to the Lookup Table(n-D) block.

Summary: Persistent lateral drift for vehicle models with solid axle suspensions and non-zero initial roll angles
Description: If your vehicle model has a non-zero initial roll angle and a solid axle suspension, the vehicle can have a persistent lateral drift during simulation.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Memory allocated for a dynamic array in the generated code might be?freed before last use of the array
Description: In the generated code, memory allocated for a dynamic array might be?freed before the last use of the array if all  these conditions are true: Your MATLAB code contains an array for which the code generator allocates memory dynamically on the heap. (Such arrays must be variable-size and unbounded, or variable-size with bound that is greater than a threshold.) In your MATLAB code, the last reference of the dynamic array is used inside a function call. This function call appears in the condition of an if  or while ?statement.  For example, consider this MATLAB function: function y = example(n1,n2) assert(isa(n1, 'double'));assert(n1 <= 100);assert(isa(n2, 'double')); % Create a 1x100 upper bounded struct array that contains an inner unbounded arrays1 = repmat(struct('x', zeros(1,n2)), 1, n1);% Create an unbounded struct array that contains an inner unbounded arrays2 = repmat(struct('x', zeros(1,n2)), 1, n2);for i = 1:numel(s1)    s1(i).x(10) = n1;end a2 = subfcn(s2);if subfcn(s1) == 42    y = 123 + s2(10).x(2);else    y = 456 + a2;end function y = subfcn(s)coder.inline('never');y = 0;for i = 1:numel(s)    y = y + s(i).x(1);end Generate MEX code for this function by running:? codegen example?  The condition "if  subfcn(s1) == 42 " ?contains the last reference to s1 . This last reference ?occurs inside a call to the function subfcn . In the generated code,?s1  contains dynamic matrices that are heap allocated and need to be freed after the last use of s1 . (The reason for the prior call subfcn(s2)  is to make sure that the code generated for the function subfcn ? can handle arbitrary array sizes. The second call subfcn(s1) ?reuses this generated code.)  The generated code looks like: b_s1.data = &s1.data[0];b_s1.size = &s1.size[0];b_s1.allocatedSize = 100;b_s1.numDimensions = 2;b_s1.canFreeData = false;emxFree_struct_T_1x100(&s1); /* The free happens too early because s1 is indirectly used below via b_s1 */st.site = &d_emlrtRSI;if (subfcn(&st, &b_s1) == 42.0) { Calling the generated MEX function example_mex  might result in a MATLAB crash and/or an incorrect answer. For example, example_mex(10,10)?  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Create a new temporary variable that holds the last reference to the dynamic array instead of putting that reference directly in the condition of the if or while statement. For example, in the previous MATLAB function example, change:?if subfcn(s1) == 42?to:t = subfcn(s1);if t == 42
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table block with linear interpolation and output word length less than or equal to table word length causes HDL mismatch
Description: When calculating the interval of two consecutive points in a Lookup Table block for linear interpolation, overflow might occur if the result cannot be represented by the output type set on the block. This can cause a numeric mismatch in the generated HDL code.
Workaround: Increase the output data type to be 1 bit larger in word length than the data type of the table data.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Discrete-Time Integrator block logs incorrect minimum and maximum values for fixed-point data types with slope and bias scaling
Description: The Discrete-Time Integrator block logs incorrect minimum and maximum values during simulation when its output data type is fixed-point with slope and bias scaling.  To observe this issue, perform?Range Analysis ?with the Fixed-Point Designer.  Alternatively, search for "Range Analysis using Fixed Point Tool" in the documentation to find instructions on how to do this.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Shared utility code folder error for modified Vehicle Dynamics Blockset and Powertrain Blockset example
Description: Simulating or updating the diagram of a Powertrain Blockset or Vehicle Dynamics Blockset example model might produce an unexpected error if you have edited a model in the model hierarchy. The error might look like: ? Error while bringing model references of model SiCiPtReferenceApplication up to date: Error????building model 'SiEngineController'.  The current model configuration differs from the model????configuration used to generate the shared utility code folder, ????'<myPath>\ slprj\ sim\ _sharedutils'.
Workaround: Delete the slprj folder and .slxc files, then try again.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing logged signals from Stateflow charts when Simulink.fileGenControl sets the CacheFolder to a directory other than pwd
Description: Signals inside Stateflow charts that are inside a model block (set to Accelerator mode simulation) can be logged using the Data Import/Export  pane. When Simulink.fileGenControl  is set to generate code and cache files in a directory other than pwd  using the following command:? Simulink.fileGenControl('set','CacheFolder','C:\otherDirectory')?  Such signals may not appear in the final logged data.?
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Index outputs of union, setdiff, and setxor might refer to the last instances of repeated elements instead of the first instances
Description: The generated code for setxor  and union  might return the indices corresponding to the last occurrence of repeated elements in its second and third outputs rather than the first occurrences. For example, with?[y,ia,ib] = x_union([2,2],1) ,  we should have?ia = 1 , but code generation returns?ia = 2 .?  Similarly, the generated code for setdiff ?might return the last occurrence of repeated elements in its second output rather than the first occurrence. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Streaming and sharing of resources that are clock-rate pipelined can cause HDL mismatch
Description: If streaming and sharing are both requested when Clock Rate Pipelining  is enabled, the generated HDL code might have a numeric mismatch when compared to the original model. The mismatch occurs because the scheduling logic inside the clock-rate pipelined region does not correctly handle resources participating in multiple optimizations that require scheduling such as streaming and sharing. This can happen if streaming is requested on a subsystem which contains another subsystem requesting sharing.
Workaround: Remove the sharing request from the lower subsystem, or avoid using Clock Rate Pipelining when requesting the streaming and sharing optimizations on the same resources.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in value of parameter Scalarize vector ports?between protected model and model in which it is used generates incorrect HDL code
Description: If the value of the Configuration parameter Scalarize vector ports  does not match between the model underneath a protected subsystem and the model in which the protected subsystem is being used, then HDL Coder can generate incorrect code. If the design contains a protected subsystem for HDL code generation, then all Configuration Parameters must match between the top model and the protected model as mentioned in this HDL Coder documentation page:?Protected Model Restrictions for HDL Code Generation .
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for PC Worx 6.0?target when using models with?Shift Arithmetic?blocks
Description: Behaviour of?Shift Arithmetic ?instructions on target?PC Worx 6.0? is different from that in Simulink. This could lead to incorrect code generation for Out-Of-Range bit shift operations.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL coder might generate incorrect code for 1-bit HDL Counter with step value of -1
Description: The generated code from the HDL Counter might be incorrect when the?step value  is -1  and the difference between the?Word length  and Fraction length  is 1 .
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 7, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calling discretize with repeated bin edges and the 'IncludedEdge' option set to 'right' might produce incorrect answer
Description: In the generated code, calling  discretize  might produce incorrect answer if all  these conditions are true:?  The bin edges provided to discretize   include repeated values, implying that at least one bin has right and left edges that are the same.  The 'IncludedEdge'  option is set to 'right'  and the bin with repeated edges is not the first (i.e. left-most) bin. This indicates that the bin with repeated edges must be empty because the conditions for an element to be placed in it contradict each other.   MATLAB correctly leaves such a bin empty, but the generated code might place elements in it.? Here is an example that illustrates this issue: discretize([0 1 2],[0 0 1 1 2],'IncludedEdge','right')?  MATLAB execution produces this result:?   [1? ? ?2? ? ?4]?  Generated code produces this result:? [1? ? ?3? ? ?4]?  Note that if the call to discretize ?does not use the ?'IncludedEdge' ?option or if the?'IncludedEdge' ?option is set to?'left' , the generated code ?is not affected by this issue.? This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant global variable passed to external C/C++ function by using coder.ceval might not be initialized correctly in the generated code
Description: This issue affects code generation if all of these conditions are true: Your MATLAB code passes a global variable to an external C/C++ function by using coder.ceval  The global variable is passed by reference by using?coder.rref  During code generation, you initialize the global variable to a constant value by using coder.Constant?    In such cases, the global variable might not be initialized correctly in the generated code.?
Workaround: In your MATLAB code, assign the global variable to a temporary local variable. Pass the temporary variable to the external C/C++ function by using coder.ceval.For example, suppose that your MATLAB code looks like this:function usingConstantGlobalglobal G;coder.ceval("DoSomething",coder.rref(G));end To work around this issue, change the code to: function usingConstantGlobalglobal G;tmp = G;coder.ceval("DoSomething",coder.rref(tmp));end
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Potentially incorrect VHDL test bench code generated for multirate design with a vector port named phase?at DUT interface and ScalarizePorts parameter set to on
Description: The generated VHDL test bench code from HDL coder might fail to compile and generates an error message similar to: # ** Error: s_tb.vhd(160): No feasible entries for infix operator "=".? # ** Error: s_tb.vhd(160): Type error resolving infix expression "=" as type std.STANDARD.BOOLEAN.?  # ** Error: s_tb.vhd(474): VHDL Compiler exiting?  This error occurs when the following conditions are met:  HDL test bench is generated with VHDL  as the TargetLanguage  The design has multiple rates with a vector port named phase  at DUT interface ScalarizePorts  property is set to on.
Workaround: Either set ScalarizePorts to off or use a different name other than phase for the vector port.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: The instfreq function returns a silent wrong answer when called with complex-valued input
Description: Between R2018a and R2020a, the instfreq  function returned a silent wrong answer when called to apply the 'tfmoment'  method to complex-valued input. As of R2020a, the function supports complex-valued inputs correctly. instfreq  versions fixed with release updates prior to R2020a throw an error if the input is complex-valued and the 'tfmoment'  method is used.

Summary: blfread does not recognize CAN FD Rx messages
Description: When reading CAN FD messages from a BLF-file using blfread , messages denoted as "Rx" direction are not imported as expected. This issue also occurs when parsing metadata with blfinfo . Messages with "Tx" direction are properly imported. Messages with "TxRq" are ignored by design.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Error when creating a comparison report for a signal comparison performed programmatically
Description: An error occurs when you create a comparison report if the comparison results were created by using the Simulink.sdi.compareSignals  function. When you compare signals using the Simulation Data Inspector UI, you can create a comparison report without error.
Workaround: Unzip the attachment and savethe?workaroundForBugReport2209852.p file to a folder on your MATLAB path.Run this function immediately after using the Simulink.sdi.compareSignals APIin your MATLAB script/function.

Summary: Requirements report displays unrelated text with requirement description
Description: When you generate a Requirements Report for a Simulink model with the "Include details from linked documents"?option enabled?in Report Settings,?the generated page will?display HTML style noise like this: "p, li { white-space: pre-wrap; }" for every reported link destination in a Simulink Requirements Set (links that point to entries?in?.slreqx files).
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating models with non-complementary motion actuation and at least one implicit 6-DOF tree joint
Description: If a Simscape Multibody model has non-complementary motion actuation and at least one implicit 6-DOF tree joint, MATLAB might crash during a normal simulation. The crash occurs when an insufficient computed torque error occurs, and the joint with the largest magnitude error is an implicit 6-DOF joint.  When generating code for such a model (rapid accelerator, etc), MATLAB will always crash.
Workaround: Simscape Statistics Viewer displays the number of implicit 6-DOF joints in a model; try to eliminate all implicit 6-DOF tree joints by adding explicit joint blocks, such as the 6-DOF Joint block, from the Simscape Multibody library.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Simulating a model hierarchy with model arguments that span multiple levels might produce incorrect results
Description: Simulation results for a model hierarchy will be incorrect when all of these conditions apply: A model argument is promoted up through multiple levels of the model hierarchy. The referenced model that defines the model argument in the model workspace is a function-call model or has a virtual bus that crosses the model boundary. A Model block that receives the model argument from a lower level specifies a value for it.  Simulation will use a model argument value that is specified at a lower level of the model hierarchy instead of the value specified at the highest level Model block that receives the model argument.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink might not rebuild rapid accelerator target when?accelerator target is up to date
Description: Simulink might not rebuild the rapid accelerator target for the top model of a model hierarchy when all of the following conditions apply: You have a model hierarchy with at least one referenced model.  You make a change to a model in the hierarchy that requires a rebuild of the model reference simulation target during simulation in accelerator mode. You simulate the top model of the hierarchy in accelerator mode. You simulate the top model of the hierarchy in rapid accelerator mode.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unit Conversion block returns incorrect generated code for conversions with offsets and fixed-point data types with bias
Description: The Unit Conversion block might return incorrect answers for accelerator mode, rapid accelerator mode simulation, or code generation when the following conditions are true:The Output data type  parameter is set to Inherit: Inherit via back propagation , The input or output data type is a fixed-point data type with a bias, The unit conversion has an offset, for example, ˇăC  to ˇăF  or ˇăC  to K .
Workaround: Set the Output data type parameter of the Unit Conversion block to Inherit: Inherit via internal rule and insert a Data Type Conversion block after the Unit Conversion block.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector fails to inspect compatible shared utilities
Description: Compatible shared utilities pass compatibility checks, but Code Inspectorfails to inspect those successfully.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector fails code inspection when alookup table object is passed as an argument to a referenced model

Summary: int(besselj(a, b*r)^2*r, r, 0, Inf) produces incorrect result

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect images when SimBiology app generates multiple reports in the same folder
Description: The Generate Report task in the SimBiology app lets you create HTML or XML reports containing model information, diagrams, analysis tasks, and plots. If you generate multiple reports and save them in the same folder, the app overwrites images from the previous reports. As a result, the first report might show embedded images from a second report.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unit Conversion block gives incorrect answer when converting data types during Normal mode simulation

Workaround: Set the Output data type parameter of the Unit Conversion block to?Inherit: Inherit via internal rule and?insert a Data Type Conversion block after the Unit Conversion block.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a

Summary: getgeodata fails due to changes in the FTP structure by NCBI
Description: NCBI reorganized the Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) data structure in February of 2020. Consequently, getgeodata  fails when retrieving series matrix files or SOFT data files.
Workaround: To work around this issue in R2018b or earlier, follow these steps.For accessing series (GSE) accession numbers:1. Connect to NCBI FTP server.ftpConnection = ftp('ftp.ncbi.nih.gov'); 2. The series matrix file is in the directory: /geo/series/GSE<X>nnn/<accessionnum>/matrix/, where <X> is the numeric portion of the accession number divided by 1000.gseAccessnum = 'GSE11287';cd(ftpConnection, '/geo/series/GSE11nnn/GSE11287/matrix/');seriesFileGz = sprintf('%s_series_matrix.txt.gz', gseAccessnum);seriesFile = sprintf('%s_series_matrix.txt', gseAccessnum);3. Download the file and unzip it.mget(ftpConnection, seriesFileGz);gunzip(seriesFileGz); 4. Read the unzipped file with geoseriesread.gsmGeoData = geoseriesread(seriesFile); For accessing DataSet (GDS) accession numbers:1. Connect to NCBI FTP server.ftpConnection = ftp('ftp.ncbi.nih.gov'); 2. The SOFT file is in the directory: /geo/datasets/GDS<X>nnn/<accessionnum>/soft/, where <X> is the numeric portion of the accession number divided by 1000. gdsAccessnum = 'GDS2602';cd(ftpConnection, '/geo/datasets/GDS2nnn/GDS2602/soft/');softFileGz = sprintf('%s.soft.gz', gdsAccessnum);softFile = sprintf('%s.soft', gdsAccessnum);3. Download the file and unzip it.mget(ftpConnection, softFileGz);gunzip(softFileGz);4. Read the unzipped file.gdsGeoData = geosoftread(softFile);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect HDL Code might be generated when sharing or streaming optimizations are requested in the presence of clock-rate pipelining
Description: If sharing or streaming optimization is requested on a design with an Oversampling factor  set to? greater than 1, an unnecessary output delay might be added which generates incorrect code. This occurs when the number of delays that are allocated by the clock-rate pipelining optimization for the sharing or streaming region exactly matches the number of delays needed to perform the streaming or sharing calculation.
Workaround: If sharing or streaming is requested, always run the validation model to insure there is no mismatch between the original model and the generated model. If a mismatch occurs increase the oversampling factor by 1.?
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB crashes with "mps_text_to_parse_tree" or "issue_error" at the top of the crash dump
Description: MATLAB might crash when all of the following conditions are met: Running or saving a classdef file The classdef file contains a method with a dotted name such as "function set.Prop " or "function get.Prop " The classdef file contains a method that is missing an end  keyword   There are two common crash logs for this issue. Both will contain?mps_text_to_parse_tree ?near the top.  1. Trace has strlen  at top Stack Trace (from fault):?[? 0] 0x00007f6f639dd646 strlen+00000038[? 1] 0x00007f6f5017c5b2 std::char_traits<char>::length(char const*)[? 2] 0x00007f6f500d5562 _M_parse_tree_T<char>* mps_text_to_parse_tree[? 3] 0x00007f6f5017536c in_parse_M_function_for_lxe  2. Trace has? issue_error  at top Stack Trace (from fault):[? 0] 0x00000000fb8413a3 MPARSER_T<char>::issue_error[? 1] 0x00000000fb6e1198 mps_text_to_parse_tree[? 2] 0x00000000fb7a80d0 in_parse_M_function_for_lxe
Workaround: This crash can be avoided by editing the class file causing the crash and ensuring that every block statement (for, if, function, methods, classdef, etc.) has an associated end keyword. Using?Smart Indent?can help to figure out where missing ends are located.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: mod?and rem functions might return nonzero values when there is overflow
Description: For standalone targets, the code generated for?mod(a,b) ?and?rem(a,b)  might return nonzero values if the inputs a  and b  are of floating-point type and the quotient a/b ?overflows to Inf ?or -Inf . Summary: ?Height and Width properties not honored for a DOM Image object with a ScaleToFit format object
Description: An mlreportgen.dom.Image  object does not honor the Width  and Height  properties if the Style property contains an mlreportgen.dom.ScaleToFi t format object.? Instead, the image is always scaled to fit the page in the generated report.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Evaluating a fistree object silently produces incorrect results when multiple inputs use same values
Description: Evaluating a fistree   object?can silently produce incorrect results when both of the following conditions are true: The fistree  object contains more than two FISs One fistree  input is connected to the inputs of multiple FISs within the fistree   This issue can produce incorrect results when evaluating a?fistree  object using the?evalfis ? function. Also, the issue can produce incorrect parameter values when tuning a fistree  object using the tunefis  function.
Workaround: To workaround the problem, first remove any input-to-input connections from the fistree?object by modifying the Connections?property of the fistree object. Then, specify the same input data for all previously connected inputs when evaluating the fistree?object.For example, suppose your?fistree?object?fisT?contains four FISs with the following configuration.The first input of each of the first three FISs are connected.The second input of each of the first three FISs are connected.The outputs of the first three FISs are connected to the three inputs of the fourth FIS.fisT?has the following Connections property value.fisT.Connections = ["fis1/input1" "fis2/input1";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis1/input1" "fis3/input1";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis1/input2" "fis2/input2";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis1/input2" "fis3/input2";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis1/output1" "fis4/input1";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis2/output1" "fis4/input2";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis3/output1" "fis4/input3"];Evaluating fisT as follows can silently produce incorrect results. Here, input is a two-column array of input data.?output = evalfis(fisT,input);To remove the input-to-input connections, set?Connections?as follows.fisT.Connections = ["fis1/output1" "fis4/input1";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis2/output1" "fis4/input2";...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? "fis3/output1" "fis4/input3"];When evaluating fisT, use the same input data for the previously connected inputs. In this case, you repeat the same input data columns three times.output = evalfis(fisT,[input input input]);

Summary: Compiling MATLAB Data API code with VS2019 Community Edition with Conformance Mode enabled generates an error with the message Error C2760 syntax error: unexpected token 'identifier', expected 'expression'
Description: In Microsoft Visual Studio 2019 Community Edition, the Conformance Mode option is set to /permissive-  (enabled) by default.? This causes the compiler to generate errors not generated by older versions of VS, or by other versions of Visual Studio 2019. In particular, it generates an error when a typedef  statement contains a dependent type name followed by an asterisk and parameter name, and the sequence is not preceded by the keyword typename . There are several such instances in the file ArrayFactory.hpp .
Workaround: You can use any one of these possible workarounds: Use mex or mbuild instead of Visual Studio to buildapplications. Under the Visual Studio project properties, under C/C++ -> Language, set Conformance Mode to No. Use a version of Visual Studio other than Visual Studio 2019 Community Edition.Edit extern/include/MatlabDataArray/ArrayFactory.hpp to insert the keyword typename before the dependent type names impl::ArrayFactoryImpl and impl::ArrayImpl in each of the four lines of source code for which the compiler generates errors.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for CODESYS target when using models with Shift Arithmetic blocks
Description: Behaviour of Shift Arithmetic  instructions on target CODESYS  is different from that in Simulink. There are two arguments to shift functions,(1) in : The number to be shifted, and(2) n :? The number of bits it should be shifted by. In Simulink, if n  is greater than word length of in , the answer will be zero. However, for such a case in CODESYS , the IDE will shift in  by n? MOD word_length(in )" bits?CODESYS SHL Operator  . This could lead to incorrect code generation.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated CUDA code produces incorrect results when greater than 3-D input image passed to imresize?function
Description: When generating CUDA? code for the imresize  function, the generated?code produces incorrect results when the input image to be resized is specified as? N-dimensional (N > 3). For example, the code generated for the following MATLAB entry-point function,?foo,? produces incorrect results: function output = foo()?  ? input = rand(640,480,3,4);? output = imresize(input,[224 224]); end
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Discrete-Time Integrator block?might produce incorrect results for unit gain and differing input and output data types
Description: The Discrete-Time Integrator?block might produce incorrect results when all of the following conditions are true:? The?Integrator method ?parameter is set to Accumulation: Forward Euler  or? Accumulation: Backward Euler .   The Gain value ?parameter is set to 1.0 .   The input data type is double or single. The output data type is different from the input data type.  Simulink might produce incorrect results in Accelerator, Rapid Accelerator, and?standalone code generation?modes under these conditions.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Function calls without trailing semicolons inside parfor loops might be discarded in the generated code
Description: If your MATLAB code has statements without trailing semicolons inside parfor  loops, these statements might be discarded in the generated C/C++ code.  For example, consider this MATLAB function: function exampleparfor k = 1:10    fprintf('Hello\n')end The code generator produces this warning:? Warning: Printing of expressions without semicolons is suppressed in PARFOR loops.?  In this case, the code generator deletes the call to fprintf  inside the  parfor  loop . ?This behavior causes any side effects from calling the function, such as printing or updating persistent variables, to not occur. As a result, the generated code behaves incorrectly.
Workaround: If you see the above warning, terminate the reported lines with semicolons. For example, in the previous example, change the code to:?function exampleparfor k = 1:10    fprintf('Hello\n');end
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: datetime time zones do not reflect recent change to Brazil's time zone
Description: In April 2019, the government of Brazil announced that the country no longer observes Daylight Saving Time. The time zone data in MATLAB does not reflect this recent change. In MATLAB, this change manifests itself as datetime  arrays in what used to be the DST period (for example, 17 November 2019 to 16 February 2020) with Brazilian time zones (for example, 'America/Sao_Paulo' ) being treated as UTC-2 rather than UTC-3. The Brazilian time zones impacted by this change are listed here. 'America/Sao_Paulo'  'America/Campo_Grande'  'America/Cuiaba'
Workaround: If working only with recent datetime values (since 17 February 2019), use the fixed time zone offset. For each time zone listed below, change the time zone to the corresponding fixed time zone offset.?'America/Sao_Paulo' -> 'Etc/GMT+3''America/Campo_Grande' -> 'Etc/GMT+4''America/Cuiaba' -> 'Etc/GMT+4'For example, for datetime values associated with S?o Paulo, change the TimeZone property to 'Etc/GMT+3'.if all(d > datetime(2019,2,17))    d.TimeZone = 'Etc/GMT+3'end For datetime values associated with Campo Grande or Cuiab¨˘, use 'Etc/GMT+4'.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator might produce incorrect C/C++ code for subscripted assignment of cell arrays containing upper bounded arrays
Description: Suppose that you generate C/C++ code for MATLAB code that contains subscripted assignments to a cell array that contains upper bounded arrays. When all  the following conditions are true, the generated C/C++ code might read uninitialized memory: You create a cell array by using the cell  function You perform a series of subscripted assignments to this cell array The right hand sides of these subscripted assignments are also cell arrays The right hand sides of these subscripted assignments contain arrays with different and explicitly specified upper bounds  For such MATLAB code patterns, the generated code assigns the same upper bound to all the elements of the left-hand side array, taking the upper bound from first assignment.? For example, suppose that you define this MATLAB function: function out = main(in1, in2)out = cell(1,2);out{1} = in1;out{2} = in2;end During code generation, define the types of in1  and in2 ?as: in1_element = coder.typeof(1,[1 5], [false true]);in1 = repmat({in1_element}, 1, 2);in2_element = coder.typeof(1,[1 6], [false true]);in2 = repmat({in2_element}, 1, 2); codegen -config:lib main -args {in1,int2}? Both in1  and in2  are cell arrays that contain variable-sized, upper bounded arrays?in1_element  and in2_element ?with different upper bounds. So, this issue might affect the generated code.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect overflow in Simulink.LookupTable and Simulink.Breakpoint objects when?number of breakpoints exceed the limit for derived Tunable Size data type
Description: Incorrect simulation results are generated for models that contain an n-D Lookup Table or Prelookup block with these settings: Model contains an n-D Lookup Table block: In the n-D lookup table block, Data Specification  is set to Lookup table object  Name  field is set to the name of an existing or newly added  Simulink.LookupTable object   In the Simulink.LookupTable object,   The Support tunable size  property is set Number of breakpoints exceeds the limit for the derived tunable size data type   Model contains a Prelookup block In the Prelookup block, Breakpoints data Specification  parameter is set to Breakpoint object  Name  field is set to the name of an existing or newly added  Simulink.Breakpoint object   In the Simulink.Breakpoint object ,  The Support tunable size  property is set Number of breakpoints exceeds the limit for the derived tunable size data type
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Code generator might throw an error if a data replacement type for 'char' type is specified
Description: The code generator might trigger an error when all the following conditions are  met: Model configuration parameter?Replace data type names in the generated code  is selected. A name is specified for model configuration parameter Replacement Name: char . A name is specified for model configuration parameter Replacement Name: uint64  OR Replacement Name: int64 .  The error message might be similar to this message:? 'CHAR' is an invalid duplicate replacement type for 'char'.?

Summary: Evaluation of assessments using enumeration constants with same underlying value might product wrong answer
Description: In Simulink Test Manager, if several symbols in an assessment are mapped to different enumerated values with the same underlying value, the evaluation of the assessment might produce a wrong answer. You might see an error like this, even if c1  and c2  are mapped to the same enumerated type: Error during assessment evaluation.  Caused by:  ? ? Error during evaluation of expression '...'  ? ? Type error for expression '...': type of 'c1' is 'enum1' and type of 'c2' is 'enum2'.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Conversion of logical values to fi objects with non-zero bias might be incorrect
Description: Conversion of logical values (true, false) to a fi object with non-zero bias might be incorrect. For example:? >> fi(true,0,8,0.1,-2)  ans =?  ? ? 0.8000  ? ? ? ? ? DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: slope and bias scaling  ? ? ? ? ? ? Signedness: Unsigned  ? ? ? ? ? ? WordLength: 8  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?Slope: 0.1  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Bias: -2?
Workaround: Convert logical values to uint8 before converting to fi.For example:?>>? fi(uint8(true),0,8,0.1,-2)ans =?? ? ?1? ? ? ? ? DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: slope and bias scaling? ? ? ? ? ? Signedness: Unsigned? ? ? ? ? ? WordLength: 8? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?Slope: 0.1? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Bias: -2
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 4, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating code for a model containing C action language Stateflow Chart with shift operation applied to custom storage class
Description: MATLAB might crash when generating code for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains C action language Stateflow chart. The Stateflow chart applies a shift operation (<< or >>) to a signal or parameter that resolves to a custom storage class. ?The Casting modes  model configuration parameter is set to Standards Compliant .  For an example of this issue, please see the attached issue_example_2133775.slx within the attached file issue_2133775.zip

Summary: Figure reporter incorrectly scales the snapshot
Description: The Report API Figure  reporter might incorrectly scale the figure snapshot to fit a PDF report page in either of the following cases: The report output type is not specified. The report output type is specified in upper case as PDF .?   The?Figure  reporter might also cause the caption to overflow the page boundaries.
Workaround: Specify the report output type in lower case as pdf.If the figure caption overflows the page boundaries, set the Scaling property to? 'custom' and set the Height and Width properties.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model with clock-rate pipelining and streaming optimization might generate incorrect HDL code
Description: The generated HDL code for a model with clock-rate pipelining and streaming or sharing optimizations might be incorrect in certain cases. For example, this issue might occur when a model contains multiple subsystems connected in serial all specified with a non-zero streaming factor. Serial subsystems with streaming may not be scheduled correctly as a part of clock-rate pipelining if a downstream subsystem with streaming is at a lower level of hierarchy than an upstream system with streaming. In this case, the validation model might give assertions during simulation.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 5, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Unexpected warning or error message in the Receiver Accumulator (2P) block
Description: In the Receiver Accumulator (2P) block, when the?Liquid volume fraction out of range check  parameter is set to Warning  or Error , the block may erroneously generate the following message: "Liquid volume fraction must be less than or equal to Minimum liquid volume fraction."
Workaround: Set the?Liquid volume fraction out of range check?parameter to None to avoid receiving the warning or error message. Alternatively,?you can disregard the message?"Liquid volume fraction must be less than or equal to Minimum liquid volume fraction" when?Liquid volume fraction out of range check?is set to Warning.Warning messages that read "Liquid volume fraction must be greater than or equal to Minimum liquid volume fraction"? are still valid.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation might ignore property set method if class is in a package and uses property validation
Description: The code generator might product incorrect code if all of the following conditions are true:?Your MATLAB code uses a MATLAB class that is contained inside a package. Your MATLAB code defines and uses a property of this class that has a set method. This property uses property validation.  In such cases, the generated code might not include a call to the property's set method, and instead assign the property directly. The generated code still honors the property validation rules.? For example, define the class sampleStates  in a package mypackage :  classdef sampleStates    properties        Samples(1,:) double;    end    methods        function obj = set.Samples(obj,val)            obj.Samples = val + 1;        end    endend Define a MATLAB function that creates an instance of this class and sets the property Samples .? function out = mainx = mypackage.sampleStates;x.Samples = 1;out = x.Samples;end When you call main , MATLAB returns an output 2 . The generated code does not execute the set  method and returns 1 . This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: This issue has two possible workarounds. Use any one of these:?Modify your MATLAB code so that the property does not have a custom-written?set?method.Modify your MATLAB code so that the property does not use property validation.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 4, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code and MATLAB might behave differently when using addition or subtraction operation that mixes?double?and?int64?or?uint64?inputs
Description: The generated code might produce a wrong answer if your MATLAB code contains an an addition or subtraction that mixes?double ?and?int64 ?or?uint64 ?inputs. This issue occurs?when all of the following conditions are true:? One input is?double ?and the other is?either?int64 ?or?uint64.  If adding, both the  double  and  int  inputs are negative. If subtracting, the first operand is negative and the second is positive.  The?double ?input has absolute value greater than?2^63 .   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks and MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data Store Read block feeding Stateflow chart with Simulink events might generate incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when all the following conditions are met:? Data Store Read block is feeding a Stateflow chart. The Stateflow chart invokes an event that calls a Simulink subsystem external to the chart. This external subsystem reads and write to the same Data Store Memory block.
Workaround: Use any one of these workarounds:Add a Signal Conversion block between the Data Store Read block and Stateflow chart and select?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?parameter. This is a preferred workaround because this only excludes the Signal Conversion block from buffer reuse optimization.Disable buffer reuse by clearing?Reuse local block outputs?(BufferReuse) model configuration parameter. This workaround excludes other blocks from buffer reuse optimization.

Summary: S-Function or FMU blocks that do not support multithreading might run on a separate thread if they are in a referenced model
Description: Referenced models might run on separate threads even when they contain an S-Function or FMU block that does not support running on a separate thread. For example, a referenced model containing an S-function might run on a separate thread when you have not explicitly allowed the S-function to be run on a separate thread with this command: ssRuntimeThreadSafetyCompliance(S,?RUNTIME_THREAD_SAFETY_COMPLIANCE_TRUE);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multirate sharing with adaptive pipelining generates incorrect HDL code
Description: The generated HDL code might be incorrect for a model that has multirate sharing and adaptive pipelining optimizations enabled for product, multiply add, and multiply accumulate blocks. In these cases, the validation model produces assertions due to the mismatch between the HDL code and Simulink simulation behavior.
Workaround: Either disable adaptive pipelining or use single-rate sharing when adaptive pipelining is enabled.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table n-D block or prelookup table block with non-power of 2 spacing generates incorrect HDL code
Description: Lookup Table n-D block or prelookup block might generate incorrect HDL code when the following conditions are true: The breakpoint is spaced with non-power of 2 values Inputs to the block are signed fixed point or integer type Saturation lower limit is a negative number.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 7, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assignment block with initialization signal within Iterator subsystem might generate incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when all the following conditions are met:? Simulink model has an Assignment block. The Assignment block has the Initialize output (Y)  set to Initialize using input port <Y0> . The Assignment block is within a For Iterator or While Iterator subsystem. The Assignment block is used in a feedback loop. In the generated code, the buffer of the Assignment block is reused with the buffer of another block within this loop.
Workaround: Use any one of these workarounds:Add a Signal Conversion block at the output of the Assignment block (before a Outport block) and select Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter. This is a preferred workaround because this only excludes the Signal Conversion block from buffer reuse optimization.Disable buffer reuse by clearing Reuse local block outputs (BufferReuse) model configuration parameter. This workaround excludes other blocks from buffer reuse optimization.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code might be generated for a model referencing another model?containing Stateflow charts with similar graphical functions
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model references another model. The referenced model contains a Stateflow chart containing two graphical functions that have the same operations but reference different variables. At least one of the variables referenced within the graphical function is not used as the input or the output of the graphical function and is not defined within the local scope of the function.  When the above conditions are met, the generated code might have a graphical function call incorrectly replaced by a call to another graphical function. For the attached example g2121509.slx , the code generator generates the incorrect code in the function g2121509Ref  in g2121509Ref.c : *rty_Out1 = g2121509Ref_Fun1(tmp, rtu_Input1) + g2121509Ref_Fun1(tmp, rtu_Input1); ? ?The expected correct code is:? *rty_Out1 = g2121509Ref_Fun1(tmp, rtu_Input1) + g2121509Ref_Fun2(tmp, rtu_Input2);??
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Indexed deletion might cause unwanted changes to an array or cause MATLAB to crash
Description: During an indexed deletion operation of an array, if the remaining number of elements in the array is greater than intmax , then MATLAB might either crash or incorrectly change the value of the array elements. Consider the following example where A  is an array such that numel(A) > intmax : A = true(1,2^31+10); A([1 2^30]) = []; After deletion, the size of the remaining array is 2^31+8 . In this case, MATLAB either crashes or incorrectly changes the values of A .
Fixed In: R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 5, R2020a, R2020b

Summary: C/C++ compiler throws an overflow warning when compiling an S-function
Description: When using GCC 9 or newer, the C/C++ compiler might throw an overflow error when compiling an S-function because the error message is too large for the buffer. The error message is similar to: ?simulink.c:101:5: warning: '</arg></arguments></diag></d...' directive writing 37 bytes into a region of size 14 [-Wformat-overflow=]
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: For Each subsystem passes same memory to all instances of child model for partitioned input or parameter
Description: The code generator generates incorrect code for a model hierarchy when all of these conditions are true: The top model contains a For Each subsystem which contains a Model block. The For Each subsystem passes an input or parameter, that is partitioned using the Input Partition or Parameter Partition tab, to the Model block.  The generated incorrect code passes the same memory for the partitioned input or parameter to each instance of the child model, instead of separate memory for each instance. For example, a top model has For Each subsystem which contains MyReferenceModel as a referenced model. In the For Each subsystem, you partition the input signal across 3 iteration of this For Each subsystem. The generated code looks like this: for (ForEach_itr = 0; ForEach_itr < 3; ForEach_itr++) {     int32_T rtb_sig1 = rtb_VectorConcatenate[ForEach_itr];     MyReferenceModel_step(&MyReferenceModel_Input, rtb_sig1,  &rtb_d_Sig3);      ForEachSubsysTopModel_Y.Out1[ForEach_itr] = rtb_d_Sig3; }   whereas the expected code should be: for (ForEach_itr = 0; ForEach_itr < 3; ForEach_itr++) {     int32_T rtb_sig1 = rtb_VectorConcatenate[ForEach_itr];     MyReferenceModel_step((&self_ForEachSubsys[ForEach_itr].MyReferenceModel_Input), rtb_sig1,  &rtb_d_Sig3);      ForEachSubsysTopModel_Y.Out1[ForEach_itr] = rtb_d_Sig3; }   Note that in the expected code, we are passing different memory for partitioned input to each instance of the child model.

Summary: Assessments results corrupted when dynamic enumerated data type definition cannot be found
Description: In Simulink Test Manager, if a symbol is mapped to?a dynamic enumerated data type, the graphical explanation in assessment results is corrupted if the enumerated data type definition cannot be found.

Summary: webcamlist and webcam cannot detect USB webcams in certain Linux operating systems
Description: USB webcams connected to your system might not be detected by the webcamlist  and webcam  functions in the following Linux operating systems: Ubuntu 19.04 Ubuntu 18.04 LTS Debian 10 Debian 9
Workaround: For the following MATLAB releases, a fix is provided as an update to the "MATLAB Support Package for USB Webcams".To install the updated version, start Add-On Explorer:On the MATLAB Home tab, in the Resources section, click Add-Ons > Get Hardware Support Packages , orExecute supportPackageInstaller command in MATLAB command window.MATLAB R2019b,Open Support Package Installer and update the "MATLAB Support Package for USB Webcams" to version 19.2.2 by uninstalling the support package and reinstalling it.MATLAB R2019a,Open Support Package Installer and update the "MATLAB Support Package for USB Webcams" to version 19.1.3 by uninstalling the support package and reinstalling it.MATLAB R2018b,Open Support Package Installer and update the "MATLAB Support Package for USB Webcams" to version 18.2.2 by uninstalling the support package and reinstalling it.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When?input exceeds?last breakpoint, under certain conditions, Prelookup or n-D Lookup table blocks?might fail to return warning or error message
Description: Prelookup or n-D Lookup table blocks?might not return warning or error message under these conditions: Prelookup or n-D Lookup Table block is present in the model Diagnostic for out-of-range input? parameter is set to Warning  or  Error  Index search method? parameter is set to?Evenly spaced points ?or?Breakpoints specification? parameter is set to?Even spacing  Data type of Input or Breakpoints are integer or fixed-point data types Input value exceeds the last breakpoint value The difference between the input value and the last breakpoint value is small.  Note: The issue only occurs if the difference between the input and the last breakpoint is small. For larger differences, the warning or error is correctly returned.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Code generation error for AUTOSAR model in which Simulink Function sends message to root outport
Description: Code generation fails for an AUTOSAR model if both  of the following conditions are true: The model contains a root outport for which the AUTOSAR data access mode is set to QueuedExplicitSend . The model contains a Simulink Function that sends queued data messages to the root outport.  Under these conditions, AUTOSAR code generation fails with an error.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing a Power block or for a MATLAB code with a power function and Standard Math Library as C99 (ISO)
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for MATLAB code containing a power  function or for a model with a Power block if the following conditions are met:The input datatype to the power? function or Power block is single . If generating C code from a model, in the Configuration Parameters dialog box the Standard math library ?parameter is set to C99 (ISO).  If generating C code from MATLAB code using the MATLAB Coder app, on the? Custom Code tab,? ?the Standard math library ?parameter is set to?C99 (ISO).  The input to the Power block or power  function is either 1  and NaN  or NaN  and 0 . The generated code contains powf  functions.
Workaround: Select C89/C90(ANSI) as the Standard Math Library when using power function in MATLAB or when using Power block in Simulink
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Crash while exiting MATLAB after simulating a model
Description: After simulating a model, and while exiting MATLAB, the program might exit with a nonzero exit code and users might notice an additional crash log generated in MATLAB's temp directory. If the stack trace from the crash log resembles the following, please see this?Bug Report ?for more information and possible workaround: [? 0] 0x000000010b09815c? ? bin\win64\libmwsl_services.dll+00622940 SlrtwFltToAscii::~SlrtwFltToAscii+00000796 [? 1] 0x000000010b090d62? ? bin\win64\libmwsl_services.dll+00593250 SlTracer_Simple::getCurrentTime+00003682 [? 2] 0x0000000011c78801? ? bin\win64\libmwfoundation_usm.dll+00100353 foundation::usm::Detail<foundation::usm::scope::Process>::erase+00000161 [? 3] 0x000000010020b3bb? ? bin\win64\libmwservices.dll+02208699 services::graphics::MCRContext::SetMCRHooks+00015835 [? 4] 0x0000000015d7956f? ? bin\win64\mcr.dll+01086831 QueryMLFcnTable_mcr+00016767

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Function block or MATLAB Function in Stateflow might read stale data from a MAT-file when using coder.load()
Description: After the initial build of a Simulink model or a Stateflow chart, any changes made to a MAT-file that is loaded by using coder.load  might not be used in subsequent simulations. Data from the updated MAT-file might not be used during simulation if all the following conditions are true: The MAT-file is located in a folder on the MATLAB path which is different from the folder that contains the Simulink model or the Stateflow chart. The model or the chart loads data from the MAT-file by using coder.load . After the first build, you update or replace the MAT-file by another MAT-file with the same name.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:Do not use coder.load to load MAT-files that are not located in the folder that contains the model or the chart.After updating the MAT-file and prior to simulation, either modify the block contents or force a full rebuild of the block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink might not throw run-time error during simulation of model hierarchy
Description: During simulation, Simulink might fail to report run-time errors and fail to terminate the simulation when all of the following conditions apply: A model contains at least one Model block set to simulate in normal mode. The model hierarchy uses function-call subsystems, Stateflow charts, or MATLAB Function blocks.  The failure to report run-time errors and terminate the simulation might result in incorrect answers. Any blocks downstream of the source of the run-time error will continue to execute with the invalid upstream signal data. Visual debugging tools, like scopes, display blocks, and the Simulation Data Inspector will show incorrect data.  Therefore, this bug causes simulation to behave differently than code generated from the model.
Workaround: Run the attached script after you simulate any potentially impacted model. The script will output true or false, indicating whether a run-time error occurred without being reported.If the script returns true:Resolve the error returned by the script.Restart MATLAB. The script cannot clear previously captured diagnostics within a MATLAB session.

Summary: createComponentAsModel?import fails when component and runnable have the same name
Description: When using the AUTOSAR function createComponentAsModel  to import an ARXML component description and create a Simulink model, if the component and one of its runnables have the same name, the import fails with this error: Error using arxml.importer/createComponentAsModel (line 120) Error at element 1 of first input to get_param: block_diagram does not have a parameter named 'BlockType'
Workaround: Before importing, check whether the ARXML component name is distinct from the names of its runnables. If necessary, you can temporarily rename a runnable that has the same name as its component for import and then revert the runnable name within the created Simulink model.

Summary: wvd?function produces incorrect time-vector output when 'NumTimePoints' is specified
Description: When the wvd  function computes the smoothed pseudo Wigner-Ville distribution with the 'NumTimePoints'  name value-pair, the time vector output contains a time range that depends on the value specified in 'NumTimePoints' . The time vector should span the whole signal duration.  For example,  wvd(sin(2*pi*(0:100)/4),100,'SmoothedPseudo','NumTimePoints',100)??% Displays half the correct signal duration of 1 second
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 4, R2020a

Summary: Unsupported data types in custom C code triggers assertion
Description: If there are any unsupported data types in the C code to be imported into Simulink to use in C Caller, MATLAB Function, MATLAB System blocks and in Stateflow chart, it will trigger an internal assertion.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results for coding pattern involving While loop with empty body
Description: For MATLAB code that satisfies the following conditions: There exists a while  loop which does not contain any statements inside its body.?? The condition of the while  loop is a compound logical expression involving logical and '&& ' At least one of the logical subexpressions is a function-call with a side effect.  Simulation using MATLAB Function block, MATLAB System block, or MATLAB Function in Stateflow might produce incorrect results. In this situation, the simulation will behave like the while loop never executes or executes once. For an example please see 'issue_model_2093701.slx' in attached issue_2093701.zip. See also Bug Report ID 2067108 ?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Edit-time checking dialog not appearing for custom checks that verify block parameter constraints
Description: When edit-time checking is enabled for custom checks that verify user-defined block parameter constraints, the Model Advisor correctly indicates, in the model editor, blocks that violate the check. However, when you select the warning icon, the edit-time dialog does not appear and the following error occurs: Error using edittime.util.getViolationsViolation class not defined for BlockConstraintViolationError in edittime.util.getViolationsError in slSingleSelectionActionConfigError in slSingleSelectionActionConfig - Show complete stack trace?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Solve method of simscape.multibody.KinematicsSolver class returns incorrect results when frame variables are oppositely aligned
Description: If a KinematicsSolver object contains two oppositely aligned frame variable groups, i.e., ?Group 1 has base frame F1 and follower frame F2?Group 2 has base frame F2 and follower frame F1? and variables in both groups are added to the set of outputs, then the values of the variables in Group 2 returned by the solve function are incorrect.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: GPU Coder might generate incorrect code if a function has loops with variable unsigned bounds
Description: GPU Coder might generate incorrect code for a function which satisfies all of the following conditions: ?1) The function should have one or more unbounded loops.? ?2) The index value of the above loop evaluates to an unsigned type.? The following function illustrates this:? function y = foo(u,v) y= uint8(ones(255,1)); a = uint8(v);  b = uint8(u); for i=b:a % loop with unsigned integer variable bounds    y(i,1) = uint8(i*i); end? end?  In the above example, if b > a, generated code can produce incorrect value.?

Summary: Root Inport Mapper fails to map models with Inport blocks?whose data types are assigned as MATLAB expressions
Description: Root Inport Mapper, available through Simulink or Simulink Test, might fail to map models with Inport blocks?whose data types are assigned as MATLAB expressions.

Summary: Code view is stuck loading generated code
Description: After code generation, the Code view shows the loading icon and does not display the generated code. This happens when the code uses special indentation format, for example, if there are multiple { ?symbols on the same line.
Workaround: Open Code Generation Report to view code.

Summary: Reapplying a stereotype after it has been removed preserves property values
Description: When a stereotype is removed from an?architecture element (component, port, or connector), property values defined in that stereotype persist. Consequently, reapplying the same stereotype or a stereotype that extends a common stereotype, whether by using the Property Inspector or the "applyStereotype" function, retrieves the property values that were previously set on the applied stereotype.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multidimensional root Inport associated to signal object and connected to S-Function might generate incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when all the following conditions are met:? Root Inport is connected to S-Function block. The output signal of that root Inport is associated to a signal object (for example, Simulink.Signal ) The signal object has any storage class except "Auto " and "Model default ". Preserve array dimensions  parameter is selected for the signal object.
Workaround: Disable dimension preservation for the root Inports feeding to S-Function blocks.

Summary: Setting the scope of a port to 'Parameter' in a C Caller block causes MATLAB to crash
Description: If the scope of a Caller block port is changed to 'Parameter', MATLAB crashes when 'OK' or 'Apply' button on the Block Parameters dialog is clicked.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Fixed-point numeric type bias resets to 0 on update diagram if used in an evenly spaced breakpoint of lookup table object
Description: Simulink resets the bias value of a Simulink.NumericType object to zero on update diagram when the following conditions are true: The Simulink.NumericType  object or a Simulink.AliasType  object referencing the Simulink.NumericType  object is used as the data type for the breakpoints of a Simulink.LookupTable  object The Simulink.LookupTable  object?Breakpoints specification ?is set to?Even spacing  The? Simulink.NumericType  object has a bias value that is nonzero  This bug impacts anything in the model that references the Simulink.NumericType  object, since the object itself is changed.
Workaround: Replace the Simulink.NumericType object with a Simulink.AliasType object where the data type is an explicitly specified fixed-point type instead of a reference to a numeric type. For example:>>mytype = Simulink.AliasType('fixdt(1,16,2^0,1)');?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 7, R2019b Update 3, R2020a

Summary: MATLAB sporadically crashes when generating code for a Simulink model containing MATLAB Function or Stateflow blocks
Description: MATLAB might crash when you generate code for a Simulink model containing MATLAB Function or Stateflow blocks. The crash report refers to sf_cg_ctx_destroy. On Windows, the call stack is similar to: [? 1] 0x... cg_ctx (in cg_ir.dll)[? 2] 0x... SF::sf_cg_ctx_destroy (in stateflow.dll)[? 3] 0x... SF::cdr_construct_context (in stateflow.dll)[? 4] 0x... SF::Cg (in stateflow.dll)

Summary: An exception is thrown when changing options in the Publish settings pane
Description: When changing any publish setting by using the Publish setting  pane (such as the Outputfile format  setting), an exception is thrown in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0": java.lang.ClassCastException. The Publish setting  pane stops working. It is still possible to publish, but any settings change throws another exception.

Summary: DOM? API HTML and HTMLFile objects ignore the CSS height property for table rows
Description:
The DOM API HTML and HTMLFile objects ignore the CSS height property for table rows. For example, the height property in the following script is ignored and generates a warning at the MATLAB command line: import mlreportgen.dom.*htmlTable = '
hello
';domTable = HTML(htmlTable);
Workaround:
Use row entry to specify row height, for example:import mlreportgen.dom.*htmlTable = '
hello
';domTable = HTML(htmlTable);
Fixed In: R2018b Update 6, R2019a Update 6, R2019b Update 1, R2020a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The median?function returns incorrect results for some 3-element and 4-element complex vectors
Description: The?median ?function returns incorrect results for some 3-element and 4-element complex vectors.
