Summary: MATLAB crash or assertion error when generating code for data with more than 10 dimensions
Description: MATLAB might crash or display an assertion error when generating code for data with more than 10 dimension. The assertion diagnostic contains the following text: Assertion at ...\matlab\src\cgir_support\dom_core\analysis\range\RangeValueFactory.cpp line 459:In getMatrixSizeRange: Assertion failed: numElements > 0 && numElements <= MAX_MATRIX_ELEMENTS??   In release R2018b of MATLAB the message might instead be:Assertion at dom_core/analysis/range/RangeValueFactory.cpp line 551:aNumDims >= 0

Summary: Hilbert spectrum output of the Hilbert-Huang transform (HHT) is incorrect
Description: The Hilbert spectrum output, P ,  of hht ?is incorrect. The plot of the Hilbert spectrum obtained when hht ?is called with no output arguments is correct, but returning the Hilbert spectrum results in the incorrect output. ?

Summary: MATLAB crash might occur during code generation from MATLAB code with nested for-loops where the inner loop might be conditionally executed
Description: In presence of nested for-loops where the inner loop has a variable sized bound, and is conditionally executed, MATLAB might crash during code generation. For example, consider the following MATLAB code that contains a copy to matrix B ?from matrix A ?within the inner?for ?loop as follows: function [B] = example_1991581(A, in)? ?B = A + 1;? ?global g? ?if in > 5? ? ? n = 5;? ?else? ? ? n = 8;? ?end? ?for i = 1:15? ? ? ?if in? == 3? ? ? ? ? ?B( 1:1:n, 1:1:1 ) = A( 1:1:n, 1:1:1 );? ? ? ?else? ? ? ? ? ?g = g+1;? ? ? ?end? ? endend  This might result in an assertion similar to the following:? CGIR assertion 'In forLoopOfVariableSizedCopy: Assertion failed: compatibleLoop(loop, ?forEntry)' failed in '../ ../ src/ cgir_xform/ dom_core/ lowering/ memFcn/ ReplaceWithMemCall.hpp:330'? To reproduce this bug, run the?run_memcpy_example_1991581.m ?file for the attached example memcpy_example_1991581.m  in??'example_1991581.zip' .
Workaround: Set?Use memcpy for vector assignment?to?Off, or, set the?Memcpy threshold (bytes)?parameter to a very high value such as?int32(intmax).

Summary: Model cannot set code generation folder structure to reuse library code
Description: When you try to reuse library code in a model, the model uses the value specified in Simulink.filegen.CodeGenFolderStructure.TargetEnvironmentSubfolder . In certain cases, despite setting the correct Code generation folder  structure  via the Simulink preferences or via the Simulink.fileGenControl  API, building the model generates a warning or error depending on the value of Behavior when pregenerated library subsystem code is missing ?(PregeneratedLibrarySubsystemCodeDiagnostic ) model configuration parameter. The error is as follows: The subsystem 'SubsystemA'?cannot reuse library code because it is using 'ModelSpecific' code generation folder structure. Choose 'TargetEnvironmentSubfolder' code generation folder structure to enable the subsystem to reuse library code.?

Summary: Models with Pipe (2P) or 3-Zone Pipe (2P) block might cause simulation to stall or fail
Description: Simulation might stall or fail for models with a Pipe (2P) block from the Simscape Foundation library or a 3-Zone Pipe (2P) block from the Simscape Fluids library. This occurs because?the Gnielinski correlation  equation, which is?used to calculate the heat transfer, may become singular under some conditions, such as low flow rate.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model with a?Bus Assignment block?or an?Assignment block
Description: The results of an Accelerator mode simulation might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a?Bus Assignment block?or?an?Assignment?block.  The input to the assignment input port of the Bus Assignment block is one or more signals from a bus.? The signal that connects to the assignment input ports of the?Bus Assignment block  or the U-input data port of the?Assignment block? drives a root-level output port.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, deselect the Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks parameter.

Summary: Folder deletion error during PDF report generation
Description: PDF report generation randomly fails with a folder deletion error. This error generally occurs when the report contains a diagram snapshot whose elements include hyperlinks to enable diagram-based navigation. The error message is similar to: "Cannot remove directory: ?"  followed by the path of the intermediate folder.?

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify non-inlined graphical function call inside Stateflow transition condition
Description: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify non-inlined graphical function calls inside Stateflow transition conditions, for example: [ foo(u1) ]{? ˇ­? }  where:? function out = foo(in) ? ? out = boolean(in);?end?    ? ? ? ?

Summary: U suffix on integer literals might be missing from the generated code causing a??MISRA 10.6?violation
Description: The generated code might contain a MISRA 10.6 violation for a model that contains a signal with an unsigned integer data type that is smaller than int16  and contains a custom storage class.For this modeling pattern, MISRA compliant code should contain a U  suffix in the assignment to the signal variable with the custom storage class. The MISRA violation occurs because the integer literal in the assignment statement is missing the U  suffix.

Summary: MATLAB might crash on Windows machines without Microsoft Visual C++ 2010 Redistributable installed
Description: On machines that do not already have the Microsoft Visual C++ 2010 Redistributable installed, MATLAB's supporting licensing and installer applications may fail, causing MATLAB to crash.

Summary: MATLAB crashes after interpreted mode simulation with a MATLAB System block that accesses a Data Store Memory block
Description: A System object in a MATLAB System block accesses Data Store Memories by using global variables within System object methods. If the System object via a MATLAB System block accesses a Data Store Memory block in interpreted mode and the Data Store Memory contains a fixed-point type whose characteristics (signedness, word length, fraction length, etc.) are different than any fixed-point type used at the inputs or outputs of the MATLAB System block, MATLAB will crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Passing?bus or structure type data to Simulink functions in event actions of SimEvents blocks may produce incorrect results
Description: Calling Simulink functions from event actions of SimEvents blocks might produce incorrect results when the following conditions are met: A data of bus or structure type is passed to the Simulink function The bus or structure type contains fields which are non-double

Summary: MATLAB does not honor the MATLAB proxy settings for a connection to dws.mathworks.com during startup
Description: MATLAB does not honor the MATLAB proxy settings for a connection to dws.mathworks.com . This connection occurs on MATLAB startup to perform a check for product updates.  This bug does not affect system proxy settings outside of MATLAB that are configured.

Summary: Uncompilable code when Parameter Writer access Model block inside For-Each subsystem
Description: Simulink generates uncompilable code when the following conditions are true: You have a Model block inside a For-Each subsystem. A Parameter Writer block accesses an argument of that Model block. The accessed parameter has the Model Default storage class. The Parameter Writer block is not inside the same For-Each subsystem as the Model block.  Compiling the generated code leads to errors like the following:? error C2039: 'InstP_Model': is not a member of 'InstP_top_T'  or? error: "InstP_Model" undeclared (first use in this function).?

Summary: Resizing the Volume Viewer app generates a warning
Description: Resizing the Volume Viewer app before it has completely initialized, generates warnings. These warnings cause no issues with the usage of the app: Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event SizeChanged defined for class matlab.ui.Figure: Output argument "pos" (and maybe others) not assigned during call to "images.internal.app.volview.View/computeViewportPosition". Error in images.internal.app.volview.View/manageVolumeFigureResize Error in images.internal.app.volview.View>@(hobj,evt)self.manageVolumeFigureResize() ...
Workaround: To avoid this issue, do not resize the app until it is completely initialized.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening Simulink on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 6
Description: If the user environment does not contain the LD_LIBRARY_PATH environment variable, MATLAB might crash when opening Simulink.
Workaround: Set your LD_LIBRARY_PATH environment variable by entering these commands in a command terminal:cd (matlabroot)LD_LIBRARY_PATH=export LD_LIBRARY_PATHbin/matlabIf setting the LD_LIBRARY_PATH variable resolves the crash, you can make the fix more permanent by entering these commands in a command terminal: cd ~touch .bash_profileprintf 'LD_LIBRARY_PATH=\nexport LD_LIBRARY_PATH' >> .bash_profile. ~/.bash_profile If you are using non-standard terminal settings, you might need to modify a different start-up script, such as ~/.profile.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Find Nonzero Elements?block?generates incorrect code with fixed-point data types that cannot represent zero values
Description: In the generated code, the?Find Nonzero Elements?block incorrectly excludes some non-zero elements when the following conditions are true: The input to the Find Nonzero Elements block is of fixed-point type.? The fixed-point type cannot represent the value zero exactly, e.g. fixdt(0,8,1,1) . The input signal contains?nearest to zero values that the fixed-point type can represent, e.g. fi(1,0,8,1,1).   The incorrect code causes?a mismatch in behavior between normal mode simulation and all code generation targets (accelerator mode, rapid accelerator mode, standalone code generation, and so forth).?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initialization of state for Unit Delay block that reuses the same variable for its state and input signal in generated code
Description: The generated code contains an incorrect initialization of a Unit Delay block state for a model that meets all of these conditions: The input to the Unit Delay block is not a conditionally-executed subsystem.? The input to the Unit Delay block is a constant value which does not equal the value of the Initial condition  parameter of the Unit Delay block. In the generated code, reuse occurs between the Unit Delay block state and its input signal.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with a?Bus Assignment block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a?Bus Assignment block.?  The input to the assignment input port of the Bus Assignment block is one or more signals from a bus. An Assignment  block also connects to the?assignment input ports?of the Bus Assignment  block. The Assignment  block has a Y0-input initialization?signal .  The Assignment  block and Bus Assignment block are inside  the same For-Iterator  or While-Iterator  subsystem.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, deselect the?Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment?blocks parameter.

Summary: Code generation error when model has Model block in SIL/PIL simulation mode connected to?Merge block
Description: A code generation error occurs under these conditions:? A top model or a model in a reference hierarchy contains a Model block with?Simulation mode ?set to?Software-in-the-loop (SIL) ?or?Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) ?mode. the Model block is connected to a Merge block.  The error is:  You cannot connect the signal from Model Reference block?'<model>/ <block>' output port <port number> to a Merge block when the Model block is in Software-in-the-Loop (SIL) or Processor-in-the-Loop (PIL) simulation?mode. To workaround this limitation, ?first make sure that no errors occur when?the Model block is in Accelerator simulation mode. Next, ?restore the Model?block simulation mode to its previous setting. Then, ?if the Model block is?inside a conditionally executed subsystem that is connected to the Merge block, add a Signal Conversion block (Signal copy) between the Model block and the?Outport of the conditionally executed subsystem. Alternatively, ?if the Model?block is connected directly to the Merge block, ?add an Enabled Subsystem?between the Model block and the Merge block that passes the signal straight?through. Configure the Enable Port of the new Enabled Subsystem so that only?one Merge block input is active at a time.

Summary: MATLAB cannot access Amazon S3 when Federated Login Authentication is required
Description: Accessing an Amazon S3 bucket using credentials based on AWS Federated Authentication issues the error: Cannot find files or folders matching: 's3://mybucket/path/to/folder' . This affects all functions that can read from cloud storage, including datastore , readtable  and imread .

Summary: Import Tool returns an error when importing a file with duplicate column headers
Description: When you use the Import Tool to import a file with duplicate column headers as a Numeric Matrix, Cell Array, or String Array, MATLAB?? returns the following error: Two or more variable names are the same. Rename them so that they are unique.
Workaround: Change the Output Type to Table, and convert to matrix or cell array afterwards at the command line, using table2cell or table2array.

Summary: Indirect assignments for Variant Subsystem choice output connected to ground block
Description: A Variant Subsystem output connected to a constant parameter in one choice?and to a ground block in another choice, may generate the below code with indirect assignments.    For example :    void In1(void)?  {?  #if a == 1?      ? mdl1_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut1 = 1.0;?      ? mdl 1_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut2 = 1.0;?      ? mdl 1_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut3 = 1.0;?    #endif?      ? mdl1_Y.Out1 = test1_18b_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut1;?    ? mdl1_Y.Out2 = test1_18b_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut3;?    ? mdl1_Y.Out3 = test1_18b_B.VariantMergeForOutportOut2;?    }    Where, the expected code is :  ?  void In1(void)?  {?    #if a == 1?    ? mdl1_B.Out1 = 1.0;?    ? mdl1_B.Out3 = 1.0;?    ? mdl1_B.Out2 = 1.0;?    #endif? ??  }

Summary: Simulink Coder build process fails on Linux for models containing UDP Send or UDP Receive?blocks when certain GCC C/C++ version compilers are used.
Description: When Simulink Coder is used with models containing UDP Send or UDP Receive blocks, and compiled using some of the recent versions of GCC C/C++ compilers such as GCC 4.9.x on Ubuntu 16.04.6 LTS, the build process fails. These versions of GCC C/C++ compiler require the link flags -lm -ldl to appear last in the build command line. The gcc command line did not have these link flags listed last, and the link phase of the build fails. Errors will indicate undefined references to functions such as 'dlsym' and 'dlcose', and that the build fails to generate all binary outputs.
Workaround: Manually call the gcc build command in a Linux shell with the link flags?-lm -ldl only appearing at the very end of the gcc build command line.

Summary: Incorrect port name displayed on Property Inspector after undo/redo
Description: Changing the name of a port on a component in the Composition Editor and performing an undo or redo causes a mismatch between the name of the port in the Composition Editor and the name displayed on the Property Inspector.?

Summary: Connector error on MATLAB startup
Description: If you have Wireshark installed, MATLAB might throw the following error when starting up: Error in Connector: Did not start the server. Desired port was: 31515. Last error was: Error while starting socket: Net Exception: Address already in use:  127.0.0.1:31714, ?10048?
Workaround: Uninstall or disable Wireshark.?

Summary: Build with template makefile fails when using rtwoptions to customize DOWNLOAD or MAKECMD macros
Description: When you build code using a makefile generated from a customized template makefile, you might see one of these errors:?  syntax error near unexpected token `|'  | was unexpected at this time   The build process failure occurs under these conditions:  The system target file has a custom rtwoptions  structure that defines  makevariable  Your template makefile uses?  makevariable  to modify one of the DOWNLOAD  or MAKECMD  macros.

Summary: Potential incorrect results for models containing Data Store Memory block resolving to Simulink.Signal
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may provide incorrect results for a model when the following conditions are met:  The model contains model references One of these referenced models contains a data store which resolves to a Simulink.Signal object (call this data store 'A').  In addition to the above condition one of the following 2 conditions should be met:  There is a separate Data Store Memory block in a parent model with the same name, which does not resolve to a Simulink.Signal object  'A' is used inside of a MATLAB Function Block or Stateflow chart   If the above mentioned conditions are satisfied for a model, the replacement model that Simulink Design Verifier creates may produce incorrect results.?

Summary: Slow simulation of Frequency Response Estimator block with Bode plot display
Description: In the Frequency Response Estimator block, selecting Display Bode plot  creates a plot of estimated frequency response that updates during simulation. For faster sample times, frequent refreshing of the Bode plot can cause significant simulation slowdown. The block ignores the Refresh plot every N*Ts seconds  parameter, which is intended to limit the refresh rate.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, clear Display Bode plot. To view estimated frequency responses during simulation, connect a Display block to the frd output port of the Frequency Response Estimator block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: C Caller blocks with Mux blocks as inputs generate incorrect code
Description: C Caller block receiving an input signal from a Mux block generates incorrect code. The expected generated code should combine the input signals to the Mux block into one vector signal and call the C Caller block's function once with the combined vector signal. The incorrect generated code creates a call to the C Caller block's function for each input signal to the Mux block.
Workaround: Replace Mux blocks with Vector Concatenate blocks as inputs to C Caller blocks.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 5, R2019a Update 4, R2019b, R2020a

Summary: Generating code for a model that contains a Stateflow Chart produces an error that mentions tlc
Description: Generating code for a model that meets the following conditions results in a  tlc  error:  The model contains a Stateflow Chart that uses the C action language with string inputs.  The model configuration parameter Import Custom Code  is selected.    Generating code results in a build failure and a message similar to the following:  Main program:==> matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:FcnGenRateGroupedCodeFromModule(5237)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:FcnGenerateCodeFromModule(5119)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodySysFcn(256)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodyFcnCache(501)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc:SLibFcnGenBodySysFcnEntry(608)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc:FcnGenBodySysCache(1594)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\commonbodlib.tlc:SLibGenBodyCache(5940)    matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\formatwide.tlc:<NONE>(55) ?

Summary: Polyspace Access users cannot view results
Description: Polyspace Access might not load all the LDAP users you specify in the Cluster Operactor (COP) settings. The affected users can log in to Polyspace Access but are unable to view any results. You cannot manage or change the project permissions of these users. This issue occurs when the number of users that Polyspace Access attempts to retrieve from the LDAP server exceeds the maximum results size that the LDAP server returns. For instance, if you configure the?LDAP Base DN ?in the COP setting to include 150 users, but the maximum results size of the LDAP server is 100 users, Polyspace Access loads only 100 out of 150 users.
Workaround: Use a more restrictive?LDAP Base DN?in the Cluster Operator settings. A more restrictive Base DN improves performance and reduces the likelihood that a Polyspace Access request to load users will exceed the LDAP server's maximum results size.?For example, this base DN only lists a domain component (DC)  DC=MyCompany,DC=comTo make the base DN more restrictive, add an organizational unit (OU):  OU=Users,OU=Development,DC=MyCompany,DC=comPolyspace Access will retrieve only users from the Development organization, as opposed to all users under MyCompany.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when simulating a referenced export-function model with signal logging
Description: MATLAB might crash when you simulate a model that meets the following conditions: The top model contains a Model block that is referencing an export-function model. The export function model contains at least one function-call root?Inport block with inherited sample time (-1). Signal logging is enabled on a signal inside the function-call subsystem driven by the above root Inport block.

Summary: SQO metric progress shows 0% instead of 100% when there are 0 remaining findings to review
Description: In the Quality Objectives dashboard, the progress to achieve a SQO threshold is displayed as 0% instead of 100% when there are 0 remaining findings to review and the SQO threshold is achieved.

Summary: Discrete FIR Filter HDL Optimized block with programmable coefficients produces wrong output datatype
Description: If you use the Discrete FIR Filter HDL Optimized block with programmable coefficients, a mismatch might occur between the Simulink and HDL filter output. This mismatch is due to precision loss from the HDL output data type, which might be too small to represent filter results for all possible values of the input coefficients.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when multiple runtimes in a deployed application set a property that uses validation functions
Description: MATLAB crashes when multiple MATLAB runtimes using the same deployed application attempt to set the value of the same property, and that property uses a validation function.? For example, consider the following class: classdef MyClass  ? ? properties  ? ? ? ? prop {mustBeReal} = 5  ? ? end? ??  end  If two instances of this class are created in two different MATLAB runtimes in a single deployed application, and, these MATLAB runtimes try to assign a value to this property, the application will crash.
Workaround: To work around this issue for a user-defined class, remove the validation function from the property declaration block and add a set?method to do the validation. That is:classdef MyClass? ? properties? ? ? ? prop = 5? ? end? ? methods?? ? ? ? function obj = set.prop(obj, value)?? ? ? ? ? ? mustBeReal(value);?? ? ? ? ? ? obj.prop = value;?? ? ? ? end?? ? end?end?There are no known workarounds if you are using a class that ships with MATLAB and has a property which defines a validation function.

Summary: Opening Logic Analyzer or Simulation Data Inspector during a long simulation might crash Simulink
Description: Simulink might crash at the end of a simulation if you opened the Logic Analyzer or the Simulation Data Inspector during the simulation. The crash occurs for simulations that generate large amounts of logged data, on the level of millions of points for some signals, with a simulation stop time greater than 5 seconds.
Workaround: Unzip the attachment to this EBR and save the?workaroundForBugReport1983143.p?file to a folder on your MATLAB path. Run this script at the beginning of?each MATLAB session.?

Summary: In the SerDes Designer app, setting?Channel Loss?to?0?after plotting?BER?freezes the app.
Description: In the SerDes Designer app, setting Channel Loss  to 0  after plotting BER   freezes the app.??

Summary: Incorrect results for model simulation in Rapid Accelerator mode
Description: The results of model simulation in Rapid Accelerator mode might be incorrect when both of the following conditions are true: The model contains Model Reference blocks inside reusable subsystems with multiple instances. The referenced models have parameter arguments with non-Auto storage classes.

Summary: Simulink Test Manager figure capture error when IntegerHandle property is off
Description: In Simulink Test Manager, an error occurs when Store MATLAB figures  is on and a test creates a figure with the IntegerHandle  property off . The test fails with this error message:? Invalid input for argument 1 (rhs1):  Value must be a scalar.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash during simulation or code generation for a model with MATLAB Function block and System object
Description: MATLAB might crash during simulation or code generation with the following assertion error failed assertion in dom_core/lowering/TypedScopeLoweringImpl.cpp at line 169:? This error might occur for a model that meets the following conditions:? The model contains a MATLAB Function block. The MATLAB Function block contains a user defined System object. The fixed-point instrumentation  mode for the model is set to 'MinMaxAndOverflow'.

Summary: Some blfread function arguments are incorrectly processed when a channel is not specified
Description: If the channel ID is not specified in the blfread  arguments, the function attempts to read all channels as it should, but some input arguments are incorrectly processed. For example, the following code should read all channels, but the input parameters are not processed correctly:   candb = canDatabase("myDB.dbc");   data = blfread("myLog.blf","Database",candb); This results in the error:   Matrix dimensions must agree.
Workaround: To work around this issue, be sure to specify individual channels and read them separately:  candb = canDatabase("myDB.dbc");   data = blfread("myLog.blf",1,"Database",candb);

Summary: MATLAB hangs when disparitySGM function is?run on NVIDIA Volta GPUs
Description: MATLAB might hang when you?run the disparitySGM  function on NVIDIA Volta GPUs. This issue occurs when the inputs to disparitySGM  function are GPU arrays as?shown below. I1 = gpuArray(I);?I2 = gpuArray(J);?disparityMap = disparitySGM(I1,I2);?

Summary: Generated code might crash for C++ class interface models with nested model reference that accesses timing information
Description: Generated code might crash if all of the following conditions are true: The model has nested model reference. The bottom referenced model contains a block that accesses absolute time (e.g. Digital Clock block, Sine Wave? block), sample hit information (multi-rate models), or variable sample time (e.g. Pulse Generator block). The bottom referenced model has empty model_initialize   function. The model configuration parameter?Code interface packaging ?is set to?C++ class .

Summary: "Initial fluxes" parameter for the Three-Phase Transformer (Two Windings) block not visible
Description: For the Three-Phase Transformer (Two Windings) block, if, in the "Configuration" settings, the "Simulate saturation" and "Specify initial fluxes" checkboxes are selected, then in the "Parameters" settings, the initial fluxes parameter is not visible.
Workaround: To specify the initial fluxes, use the "set_param"? function. For the per unit configuration, the parameter is "Initial fluxes [ phi0A , phi0B , phi0C ] (pu)" and the default value is "[0.8 -0.8 0.69999]". For the SI configuration, the parameter is "Initial fluxes [ phi0A , phi0B , phi0C ] (V.s)" and the default value is "[1273.5 -1273.5 1114.3]".For example,?>> set_param(gcb,'InitialFluxes','[0.8 -0.6 0.4]')?

Summary: Polyspace compilation might fail with message: error: identifier "va_start" is undefined
Description: If?a header file contains functions that call?va_start()  and va_end() , Polyspace Bug Finder might fail to compile if the header file is used with the option -include . For example, if a header file my_header.h  contains this code: #include void foo(int n,? ...){  va_list vl;  va_start(vl,?n);  va_end(vl);}  You can see the following error message during the compilation of my_file.cpp   if you use the analysis option?-include?my_header.h .   Verifying my_file.cpp (X/Y)    <pathto>\myfile.h,? line XX: error: identifier "va_start" is undefined  my_file.cpp: warning: Failed compilation./<pathto>
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Live Editor becomes unstable when Hide Code?button pressed or live script with code hidden opened
Description: In the Live Editor, if the Hide Code  button is pressed or a live script with the code hidden is opened, the Live Editor becomes unstable.  This occurs, for instance, when either of the two documentation examples "Create a Real-time Dashboard Using the Live Editor" or "Create an Interactive Form Using the Live Editor" are opened in MATLAB. This is because the code in the two live scripts that open, SolarPanelEstimator.mlx and RealTimeDashboard.mlx, is hidden by default. When this occurs, the Output Inline  and Output on Right ?buttons stop working and the code becomes inaccessible and therefore cannot be edited. In addition, if a live script is opened with the code hidden, the Live Editor is unable to load the outputs in the file and displays the warning message "Warning: Unable to load outputs".
Workaround: To retrieve the code that was hidden within the Live Script, you can follow the below steps:1. While in the Live Script, use the CTRL + A command to Select All (in Mac using CMD + A)2. Use CTRL + C to copy everything that was selected (or CMD + C in Mac)3. Open a new Live Script and paste the copied code.? You should now be able to view the code and save it.

Summary: When a simulation logs more than 5 GB of data, an error occurs at the end of the simulation or when accessing the logged data
Description: When a simulation logs more than 5 GB of data, the following error is seen at the end of the simulation or when accessing the logged data: Undefined function or variable 'mdl'.
Workaround: Despite the error, the simulation has completed successfully. To access the logged data, export signals using the Simulation Data Inspector UI or programmatic interface.?

Summary: Report Explorer Simulink Data Dictionary component with access to base workspace might cause report generation error
Description: In Report Explorer, report generation of a report with a Simulink Data Dictionary component might end with an error when both of these conditions are true: The data dictionary is in the?Dictionaries in list that you specified in the    Simulink Data Dictionary Loop component.       The data dictionary has access to the base workspace.?    The error message is similar to :  In component Simulink Data Dictionary: The specified entry is not present in the dictionary"  ?"Error while evaluating TimerFcn for timer 'timer-1'?         ?

Summary: "Uniqueness Constraint Violation" error when opening a System Composer architecture model
Description: A "Uniqueness Constraint Violation" error occurs in System Composer models that have two or more Reference Components that: Both reference the same model, and Are connected to each other?  You can ignore this error. It will continue to occur multiple times while the model is open.

Summary: polyspace-configure does not correctly handle relative paths to preincludes in a build command
Description: If you use a preinclude option with your build command, for instance the -include  option of gcc , and the path to the include file is relative, polyspace-configure  does not handle the build option correctly. For example, if you call polyspace-configure  this way: polyspace-configure gcc -include limits.h main.c?  it produces this warning:? WARNING: Could not find pre-include file limits.h?  And the generated options file or project file contains an incorrect path to?limits.h .
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Signals cannot be mapped to ArTypedPerInstanceMemory when signal is?inside Variant or For Each Subsystems
Description: In an AUTOSAR Software Component model, signal lines cannot be mapped to ArTypedPerInstanceMemory inside Variant Subsystems. The following error occurs during code generation: The model [model name]?has a subsystem block [block name] which contains a block () that has a '_PIM' storage class registered with it. The storage class is unstructured. Data inside of 'Variant Subsystem' or 'For Each Subsystem' can not be unstructured.
Workaround: Use a Data Store Memory block to represent the ArTypedPerInstanceMemory instead of mapping the signal line directly.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: An export-function model with merged buses generate unconditional code for Variant Subsystem outputs
Description: A Variant subsystem containing buses may generate unconditional code for its conditional outports when all of the following conditions are satisfied:  The Simulink model is an export-function model containing variant subsystem.  The parameter Propagate conditions outside of variant subsystem  is turned on.  The Variant subsystem has flexible interfaces.?  Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  parameter is turned on.  The inactive variant choice contains a Merge block.  The Merge block inside the inactive choice merges input bus signals.   You may notice that some?of the outports are shown as conditional in the model, may appear unconditional?in the generated code.

Summary: Compatibility checker incorrectly produces a warning about enum types used in Bus Assignment block
Description: For a model containing an enum  type in a Bus Assignment block, the compatibility checker incorrect warns that a block uses an unsupported block port data type. Code inspection also fails.

Summary: IR Construction failed error during parallel model reference builds
Description: While updating or building a model reference hierarchy in parallel, the following error might occur when building a referenced model containing a Stateflow chart: IR Construction failed

Summary: Zip file created by packNGo does not contain some header files from shared utilities folder
Description: When you create a zip file by setting the PackageGeneratedCodeAndArtifacts  configuration parameter to 'on'  or by running the packNGo  command, the generated zip file might not contain some header files that are located in the shared utility code folder, for example, slprj/target/_sharedutils .
Workaround: To ensure that all header files from the shared utility folder are added to the zip file, use this procedure:1. Generate code for your model.2. Go to the shared utility folder.bDir = RTW.getBuildDir(model)cd(bDir.SharedUtilsTgtDir)3. In the shared utility folder, update buildInfo.mat:bi = load('buildInfo.mat');incFiles = dir('*.h*')incFiles = {incFiles.name}addIncludeFiles(bi.buildInfo,incFiles)save('buildInfo.mat', '-struct','bi')4. Run the packNGo command, or regenerate code for your model with PackageGeneratedCodeAndArtifacts set to 'on'.5. Check that the zip file includes all header files that are located in the shared utility code folder.

Summary: When creating AMI parameters, setting Type to String and Usage to In, InOut, or Out results in corrupt AMI tree
Description: When creating? customized AMI parameters, you can select the?Type ?to be?String ?andthe?Usage ?to be any of these four options:?In ,?InOut ,?Out ,or?Info . But when?Type ?is to?String ?and?Usage ?to?In ,?InOut ,or?Out , clicking?Ok ?in the?Add Parameter...? or?EditParameter... ? in the IBIS-AMI Manger window results in a corrupt AMI tree.
Workaround: The only?way to repair the corrupt AMI tree is to rebuild themodel with?Usage?set to?Info?whenthe?Type?is set as?String.

Summary: MATLAB exit takes a long time if there are network issues
Description: If MATLAB encounters a network communication problem while exiting, the process might take up to 60 seconds to complete.

Summary: Links to imported requirements not found when using Query links from SL in DNG widget
Description: If you create direct links from SL blocks to DNG items by using Link to Selection in DNG  context menu shortcut, these links may be queried from DNG web page through the Query links from SL  hyperlink in the Simulink Requirements widget.?  However, if you import DNG requirements into Simulink Requirements .slreqx  file, and establish links between SL blocks and imported items, Query links form SL  action fails to list the links that correspond to selected item.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector fails code inspection when a Simulink model redefines boolean type with a custom type using datatype replacement table
Description: Simulink Code Inspector fails to correctly map the Simulink boolean  type to its actual representation in the generated code if it is redefined using a custom type in the datatype replacement table. In the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, this table is on the Code Generation > Data Type Replacement  pane.

Summary: Start method on?System.Diagnostics.Process .NET class might throw no matching signature error
Description: MATLAB might throw an error when you call the Start method for System.Diagnostics.Process objects on Windows 10 version 1903(18353) or later.? For example:? p = System.Diagnostics.Process();p.StartInfo.FileName = 'C:\ WINDOWS\ system32\ notepad.exe';p.Start();  Error: No method 'Start' with matching signature found for class 'System.Diagnostics.Process'
Workaround: Use Start(p) instead of p.Start()

Summary: Code inspection fails to verify code for Simulink.Signal object that is used inside a non-inlined graphical function
Description: Code inspection fails to verify the generated code from a model that contains a non-inlined graphical function inside a Stateflow chart. The input to the Stateflow chart is a Simulink.Signal  object that is used by the non-inlined graphical function. Code inspection does not verify this input in the generated code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: GPU Coder might produce incorrect CUDA code with unified memory mode under specific conditions
Description: CUDA code generated by GPU Coder might produce incorrect results when the design meets following conditions:? The GpuConfig.MallocMode  property of the coder configuration object is set to 'unified'.  Has a function (say foo ) with an unbounded variable (say var_a ) accessed inside GPU regions. This function calls another function (say bar ) which does not have any GPU regions. foo  passes var_a  to bar,  and bar  resizes var_a.   ?

Summary: Database Toolbox always connects to the default catalog when connecting to a Microsoft SQL Server database
Description: When you create a database connection to a Microsoft SQL Server database, Database Toolbox?always connects to the default catalog even if you specify a different catalog name for the connection.?

Summary: Dashboard block in model might cause a delay at simulation start
Description: If the model includes a Dashboard block, these blocks can delay the start of Simulink Real-Time external mode simulation by up to 30 seconds.

Summary: Automated arrangement of a Simscape model might cause signal lines to be deleted
Description: If you automatically arrange a Simscape model, some or all of the connections that originate from the same branching point might be deleted.

Summary: Unexpected error when a referenced model dependency is missing
Description: Simulating or generating code for a model hierarchy might produce an error when all of the following conditions apply:? Simulation or code generation files already exist for the model hierarchy. A referenced model dependency is missing.  The error message appears after a status check for a model reference simulation or code generation target. ### Checking the status of model reference code generation target for model 'sub' used in 'mid'?Error using get_model_dependency_type_message.p?? ? ? ? ? ? Assertion failed.

Summary: Stateflow generates model reference simulation artifacts in wrong folder
Description: When you specify a different simulation cache folder and code generation folder using Simulink.fileGenControl , Stateflow generates the model reference simulation artifacts under the code generation folder instead of simulation cache folder.

Summary: Simulink cache file might become corrupted
Description: After you run or build a model reference hierarchy with automatic parallel building enabled (using Parallel Computing Toolbox), Simulink might throw an error on subsequent runs or builds: The Simulink cache?<model_path>\mymodel.slxc?has been corrupted. To use this Simulink cache, you need to recreate it.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Use of signals or parameters with storage class Type set to FlatStructure might lead to incorrect code
Description: Incorrect initialization code might be generated if all these conditions are true: Model contains signals and parameter with complex data type with custom storage class Type  set to FlatStructure . Initialization value of signal or parameter with complex data type is empty. Signal and parameter has Data access  set to Direct . Signal and parameter has Data initialization  set to Static  OR parameter has Data initialization  set to Auto .  For example, a model uses inSignalStructComplex  signal and paramStruct  parameter with struct Name  set to myStruct . The generated definition is: myStruct_type myStruct = {   /* inSignalStructComplex */   0.0,     /* paramStruct */   30.0 };  inSignalStructComplex  should have complex data. The correct generated definition for this struct should be:? myStruct_type myStruct = {   /* inSignalStructComplex */ {0.0, 0.0}  /* paramStruct */ 30.0};
Workaround: Specify the initialization value for signal or parameter that has complex data.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: At model?compilation, Simulink.LookupTable object Min and Max values are ignored when set via command line
Description: At model?compilation, Simulink.LookupTable  object Min  and Max  values are ignored when the object properties are set and applied to the lookup table via the command line. These Simulink.LookupTable  object properties are affected: Table  Breakpoints   When the object is applied via the lookup table block dialog,?these object properties are honored.

Summary: lmstart and lmboot fail when run as root
Description: Starting the license manager using lmstart -u  or lmboot -u  results in the following failures: # ./lmstart -u jsmithChecking license file for local hostname and local hostid . . .Taking down any existing license manager daemons . . .No license manager daemons running . . .Starting license manager . . .Debug logfile = /var/tmp/lm_TMW.logError: Cannot find file /var/tmp/lm_TMW.ldThe license manager has not started properly.Check the logfile for any error messages.# ./lmboot -u jsmithError: Cannot find file /var/tmp/lm_TMW.ld

Summary: Unable to use enumerated types defined in a data dictionary for child or leaf state activity monitoring in Stateflow
Description: When you monitor the child or leaf state activity of a chart or state in Stateflow, you can manually define the enumerated type used by these states. However, if the enumerated type was defined in a data dictionary attached to a model, Stateflow throws an error: Enumeration 'enumTypeName' specified for monitoring the state activity of 'STATE_NAME' does not exist in the MATLAB workspace.?
Workaround: Provide a definition of the enumerated type using a MATLAB file instead of in the data dictionary.?

Summary: Deployed MATLAB functions might error or crash on originating system
Description: If you create a deployable archive on a system with any MATLAB add-on installed using the MATLAB Compiler or MATLAB Compiler SDK, the CTF-file produced can cause errors when run on the system that it was created on. This behavior is isolated to the machine that the CTF-file was created on, and does not occur if the file is run on a different system.

Summary: MATLAB crashes after updating block library for model that uses data elements configured with Code Mappings editor?
Description: ?MATLAB crashes when the following conditions are true: You use the Code Mappings editor to configure model data for code generation. You push block changes to the library from a block with a disabled link or restore an unresolved library link.

Summary: SerDes Designer becomes unresponsive during Export SerDes System to Simulink or Make IBIS-AMI model for SerDes System in absence of C++ compiler
Description: Selecting Export SerDes System to Simulink  or Make IBIS-AMI Model for SerDes System  from the SerDes Designer Export button causes the SerDes Designer to become unresponsive, if a C++ compiler is not installed and configured as the default in MATLAB. An error message appears in the MATLAB command window: Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event ButtonPushed defined for class  matlab.ui.internal.toolstrip.SplitButton:  Expected one output from a curly brace or dot indexing expression, but there were 0 results.  Error in serdes.internal.apps.serdesdesigner.TestbenchExport/exportSimulink (line 62)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? switch mexCompilerInfo.ShortName  This is followed by a full stack trace. SerDes Designer remains in the busy state showing a wait cursor, and will not respond to input.? Closing the app window will prompt to save the design if it has unsaved changes.
Workaround: To avoid the bug, configure the default C++ compiler before using SerDes Designer, In the MATLAB command window, enter:mex -setup C++?See??Change Default Compiler?for details.

Summary: Jobs on Windows parallel workers fail with error?"Failed to open system certificate store" when using?MATLAB Job Scheduler security level 3
Description: MATLAB uses CA root certificates from the system's Windows certificate store to verify servers when making HTTPS connections. When running MATLAB Job Scheduler jobs with security level 3, workers can fail to open the user's certificate store, which makes the jobs fail. The MATLAB worker logs include the line: java.lang.RuntimeException: Failed to open system certificate store.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using signal lines that resolve to?Simulink.Signal object can generate incorrect PLC code
Description: Simulink PLC Coder does not support code generation for Simulink signals that resolve to a?Simulink.Signal  object. If the Signal name must resolve to Simulink signal object ?property is enabled or if this option is set programmatically by using the MustResolveToSignalObject  port parameter, the software generates incorrect PLC code. The generated code can result in unexpected behavior such as having unassigned output variables.

Summary: Model Advisor?edit-time violations are reported for a model when saved in Code Perspective view
Description: If you save a model while the Code Perspective view is active, edit-time checking for all supported Model Advisor checks stays enabled even after exiting Code Perspective.?
Workaround: You can turn off the edit-time checks by selecting?Analysis?>?Model Advisor >?Display Advisor Checks in?Editor and re-saving the model.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: While Iterator with While loop type set to do-while can produce incorrect simulation results
Description: A While Iterator subsystem can produce incorrect simulation results when all of the following conditions are true: The model is configured for normal mode simulation. While loop type  parameter for the corresponding While Iterator block is set to do-while . Maximum number of iterations  parameter for the corresponding While Iterator block is set to 0 .  With do-while  selected, the contents of the While Iterator subsystem are executed at least once in each time step. In this case, zero is an invalid setting for the Maximum number of iterations .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reusing Delay block state crossing subsystem boundary may lead to incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when all of the following conditions are met: A Delay block is inside of a subsystem. The Delay block input or output connects to a global buffer outside of the subsystem. The global buffer could be a global signal or Data Store Memory block, for example. The Delay block input or output connects to the same global buffer inside of the subsystem. Model configuration parameter Optimize global data access  is set to Use global to store temporary results . Model configuration parameter Reuse global block outputs  is selected.  In the incorrect generated code, the same global temporary variable is incorrectly reused across the subsystem function boundary.

Summary: MATLAB crash when running Model Advisor check for dry hydraulic nodes on subsystem with unconnected Physical Modeling connector port
Description: MATLAB crashes when running the Model Advisor check for dry hydraulic nodes on a Simulink model which contains a specific block connection topology. The crash occurs when a Simulink model contains a subsystem with a Physical Modeling connector port that is both: Connected outside the subsystem? Unconnected inside the subsystem
Workaround: There are two different workaround strategies: Connect the Physical Modeling connector port inside the subsystem to another block with a Physical Modeling?port.Remove Physical Modeling connector ports which are unconnected inside subsystems.

Summary: Incorrect properties shown for component ports created when Property Inspector is open and inspecting an existing port
Description: If you add a port to a component when inspecting the properties of its existing port: The Property Inspector shows incorrect properties when you select the new port for inspection. The new port is missing in the API or Spotlight View.
Workaround: Delete the port showing incorrect properties.Close the Property Inspector or focus it on a component or architecture.Create the port again.

Summary: Unable to assign an interface to selected ports from Interface Table
Description: Assigning interfaces to selected ports from Interface Table is not working as expected. When you right-click on an interface in Interface Table, you see a context menu option to assign the interface to selected ports. Selecting this menu option does not assign current interface to selected ports and you are not notified of any errors or warnings.

Summary: Opening Simulink or MATLAB Coder might cause MATLAB to hang
Description: Opening Simulink or MATLAB Coder might cause MATLAB to hang because of out-of-date graphics drivers or faulty hardware.
Workaround: Update the graphics drivers. If the issue remains, connect the monitor to an integrated graphics port.

Summary: AUTOSAR model update reverts library block signal mapping from ArTypedPerInstanceMemory or StaticMemory to Auto
Description: Updating an AUTOSAR model, or saving and reopening the model, incorrectly reverts the AUTOSAR mapping of a signal if both of the following conditions exist: The signal is mapped to ArTypedPerInstanceMemory  or StaticMemory . The source of the signal is one of the following: A library block. A block inside of a library block.   Under these conditions, the mapping of the signal reverts to Auto . Effectively, the described signals cannot be configured as ArTypedPerInstanceMemory  or StaticMemory .

Summary: Importing spreadsheets containing charts in From Spreadsheet block causes MATLAB crash
Description: From Spreadsheet block does not support?chart sheets. Importing spreadsheets containing chart sheets?causes MATLAB to crash on Windows systems.
Workaround: Note:?This workaround is for Simulink R2017b only.?To import data from a spreadsheet that contains chart sheets:?Unzip the attachment and save the workaroundForBugReport1974193.p file to a folder on your MATLAB path.Run this script at the beginning of each MATLAB session.Open the spreadsheet and move the chart sheets to the right of all other sheets.Save the spreadsheet.On the From Spreadsheet block dialog box, for the Sheet name parameter, select a sheet that contains data.Simulate the model.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when copying/pasting/deleting or inlining a component that contains sub-components linked to Simulink behavior models
Description: If you perform any of the following actions on a component that contains sub-components that have been linked to Simulink behavior models, then MATLAB crashes:?Cut/copy-paste Drag-and-drop? Undo after delete? Inline component

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector fails code inspection when a bus element of type int8 is mapped to a struct element of type char
Description: Code inspection fails for a compatible model if a bus element of type int8  is mapped to an imported struct element of type char .?
Workaround: In order to workaround this issue and successfully verify the model, modify the type of the bus element to uint8 and the type of the struct element to unsigned char.

Summary: Block reduction leads to unconditional assignment of signal with non-Auto storage class
Description: When a signal in a model has a non-Auto storage class, and that signal is the output of a block inside a conditionally executed subsystem, the expectation is that the code generated for the model will conditionally assign the variable corresponding to that signal. However, if that block is reduced, the signal is then computed by its source block, which may be a block that is outside the conditionally executed subsystem. In that case, the variable in the code will be assigned unconditionally, which is unexpected behavior.
Workaround: Disable block reduction for the model, by clearing the model configuration parameter Simulation Target > Block reduction.

Summary: Merge conflict shown in Simulink Requirements link dependency information XML files
Description: While working in a distributed workflow with Simulink Requirements in a Simulink Project, the link dependency information can show merge conflicts for files in the Simulink Project. When working with version control, the merge conflicts can appear when you try to resolve versions of files in the project. Users might also see duplicate entries in the link dependency XML file corresponding to their artifacts.
Workaround: The Simulink Requirements API slreq.refreshLinkDependencies will regenerate the link dependency information XML files for the currently open project in MATLAB. The regeneration of the XML files should resolve the conflicts since it replaces the files in the .SimulinkProject folder of the current Simulink Project. It is recommended to checkout these XML files from version control, if needed, before running the API.>> slreq.refreshLinkDependencies

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Use of Function Caller block with multiple outputs of the same type and dimension might lead to incorrect C++ code
Description: Incorrect C++ code might be generated if all these conditions are true:? ERT model with model configuration parameter?Code interface packaging  set to C++ class . Model contains a Function Caller block. The Function Caller block calls a function with multiple outputs of the same type and dimension. The outputs of Function Caller block are fed into a virtual bus.?

Summary: Result filters not saved when Polyspace user interface is closed
Description: If you apply filters to narrow down the display of results in the Polyspace user interface, the filters are not automatically saved.  When you exit the user interface and reopen the results, to return to the previous reduced display of results, you have to reapply the filters.
Workaround: Explicitly save the filters before closing the Polyspace user interface.To save the filters, select?File > Close Results.

Summary: slreq.refreshLinkDependencies API fails with an incorrect error message
Description: Updating the link dependency information for Simulink Requirements link sets using  slreq.refreshLinkDependencies  API command results in an incorrect error message like below:  Error using char? Conversion of element 1 from <missing> to character vector is not supported.?  Error in slreq.app.LinkSetManager/registerOpenArtifacts?  Error in slreq.app.LinkSetManager/updateMetadataFromLinkset?  Error in slreq.app.LinkSetManager/buildProjectMetadataForLinksetFile?  Error in slreq.app.LinkSetManager/buildProjectMetadata?  Error in slreq.app.LinkSetManager/reIndexProjectMetadata?  Error in slreq.app.LinkSetManager/reIndexMetadata?  Error in slreq.refreshLinkDependencies?

Summary: Setting the CurrentTime property of?VideoReader?crashes MATLAB
Description: On Windows? platform, using?VideoReader  to seek to certain frames in a WMV-file crashes MATLAB.  For example, the following code:   vidObj = VideoReader('myWMVfile.wmv');  vidObj.CurrentTime = 30;  returns this error and then crashes MATLAB:   No more frames available to read from file.

Summary: Unexpected exception error when opening a model on Windows platform
Description: The Diagnostic Viewer reports the below unexpected exception on opening a Simulink model:  Unexpected exception (mwboost::interprocess::intermodule_singleton initialization failed) was caught.  Trying to add blocks from the quick insert shortcut menu might then cause MATLAB to crash.If you encounter a crash. The stack trace will include these lines:?   [ 16] 0x00007fff6e0444f2 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019a\bin\win64\VCRUNTIME140.dll+00017650 CxxThrowException+00000194  [ 17] 0x00000000f3c91fb2 bin\win64\sqldb.dll+00401330 sqldb_c_unregister_custom_function+00168370  [ 18] 0x00000000f3c92ee8 bin\win64\sqldb.dll+00405224 sqldb_c_unregister_custom_function+00172264  [ 19] 0x00000003e86645a2 bin\win64\sl_block_search.dll+00673186 sl_block_search::SLBlockDetails::setBlockPath+00222610  [ 20] 0x00000003e8666f14 bin\win64\sl_block_search.dll+00683796 sl_block_search::SLBlockDetails::setBlockPath+00233220  [ 21] 0x00000003e8560375 bin\win64\quicklaunch\slglue\sl_searchext.dll+00131957 us_destroy_activator_com_mathworks_sl_searchext+00053669  [ 22] 0x00000003e8560d68 bin\win64\quicklaunch\slglue\sl_searchext.dll+00134504 us_destroy_activator_com_mathworks_sl_searchext+00056216  [ 23] 0x00000003dd5dc55d bin\win64\glue2.dll+05948765 GLUE2::Search::Provider::urlScheme+00005117  [ 24] 0x00000003dd5e975d bin\win64\glue2.dll+06002525 GLUE2::Search::ModeInfo::operator=+00017277?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table Dynamic block?might incorrectly cast intermediate difference of interpolation algorithm
Description: Lookup Table Dynamic block?might generate code with an incorrect cast of the intermediate difference?result of the interpolation algorithm?when all of the following conditions are true: The Lookup Method  parameter of the block is set to Interpolation-Use End Values .  The?inputs x and xdat have integer or fixed-point data type. The output has signed integer or?fixed-point data type. The?Table input ydat has unsigned integer or fixed-point data type.  This incorrect cast leads to a mismatch in results between?normal mode simulation and standalone code?generation.
Workaround: Replace the Lookup Table Dynamic?with a combination of?the Prelookup and?Interpolation?Using Prelookup blocks configured for?an equivalent one-dimensional table lookup. Configure the Prelookup block:Breakpoint data source set to?Input port.Index search method parameter?set?to Binary search.Extrapolation method parameter set?to Clip.Configure the Interpolation?Using Prelookup block:Number of sub-table selection dimensions?parameter set?1.Table data Source parameter set to Input port.Interpolation method parameter set to Linear point-slope.Extrapolation method parameter set?to Linear.Connect these two blocks.

Summary: Analysis status appears as "completed" despite failure from license error
Description: In a remote verification using MATLAB Parallel Server (previously MATLAB Distributed Computing Server), when there are more workers than available licenses and no license is available at the moment, the analysis fails instead of being queued. Furthermore, the analysis status appears as "completed" despite the error. The analysis log shows the license error:  |      Licensing error: -4,0. *** License error *** Terminating.

Summary: Compatibility checker incorrectly produces a warning about enum types used in Resettable Delay block
Description: For a model containing an enum  type in a Resettable Delay block, the compatibility checker incorrect warns that a block uses an unsupported block port data type. Code inspection also fails.

Summary: Broken dynamic hyperlinks in custom documentation
Description: Some dynamic hyperlinks that point to a location in a file might be broken when you view your custom documentation in the MATLAB help browser.

Summary: Variant reduction fails with?multiple inconsistent definitions error
Description: When a model has a referenced model and both are associated with the same Simulink data dictionary (say modelData.sldd), variant reduction of the model might fail with the below error message:  Symbol 'vcdo' has multiple inconsistent definitions:?  * VariantConfigurations= in modelData.sldd (Update others to match)  * VariantConfigurations= in modelData_r.sldd (Update others to match)

Summary: ReLU layer outputs can incorrectly contain NaN values in convolutional neural networks
Description: The output of a?reluLayer ? may erroneously contain NaN  values  instead of 0 .? This can happen when all of the following conditions are met: The ReLU layer is preceded by a convolution layer; The convolution layer preceding the ReLU layer contains one or more?NaN   values in its bias parameters; and The network is executed on the CPU.
Workaround: When training on the CPU, this issue can be avoided by inserting a?dropoutLayer?with a dropout probability of zero before the ReLU layer.?When calling any of the following functions on the trained network, set the 'Acceleration' name-value pair to?'none':predictclassifypredictAndUpdateStateclassifyAndUpdateStateactivations?

Summary: oslc.configure?might not connect to?IBM Rational DOORS Next Generation
Description: When you perform?oslc.configure ? setup for IBM Rational DOORS Next Generation (DNG) integration, MATLAB might be unable to establish a working connection with the DNG server. You may get multiple password prompts, and/or an error saying "maximum recursion limit of 500 reached".

Summary: Error when clicking Show in document?for references imported from IBM Rational DOORS Next Generation module
Description: If you import DNG modules from a ReqIF file, clicking on Show in document  in the Requirements Editor throws an error.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect execution order when Merge block is in a function-call feedback loop
Description: When all of following conditions are met, relative execution order of source and destination blocks of a Merge block might be incorrect: The Merge block is inside a Function-Call Subsystem block.? The output signal for the Merge block is branched. One destination is directly connected to an Outport block that feeds back to the input of the same function-call subsystem. The other destination connects to a non-virtual block inside this function-call subsystem. The Merge block has non-virtual source blocks inside this function-call subsystem.? When the Configuration Parameter Diagnostics  > Data Validity  > Underspecified initialization detection  is set to either:  Classic .  Simplified  and the Outport block directly connected to the Merge block has its Source of initial output value  parameter set to Input signal , or Source of initial output value  is set to Dialog  and Initial output  is set to [] .   In this case, the destination non-virtual block of the Merge block might be executed before the source block.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Merge block output connected to an Outport block.

Summary: Error when using rapid accelerator mode with parameter AdvancedOptControl?set to value -SLCI
Description: Simulink might produce an error while simulating a model in rapid accelerator mode when a model?contains a MATLAB Function block and has the parameter AdvancedOptControl ?set to the value?-SLCI .  The error is due to an Inf or NaN initializer element that is not constant and is similar to the following:? example_raccel_model.c(42): error C2099: initializer is not a constant ?
Workaround: Remove '-SLCI' from the 'AdvancedOptControl' parameter.? For example >> set_param(gcs, 'AdvancedOptControl', '');

Summary: Calling Simulink Functions from within explicit partitions may cause build failures when simulating the model in Rapid Accelerator mode
Description: Simulating a model in rapid accelerator mode may fail for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model is a not an export-function model. The model contains partitions using the Schedule As   subsystem block parameter. The partitions call a global Simulink Function. The Simulink Function contains a block that has a constant sample time such as a Constant block.   The model fails with the following error: Unable to build standalone executable to simulate the model <model> in rapid accelerator mode.  This error is caused by an undefined reference error from the compiler.?

Summary: Axes toolbar is slow to appear or does not appear on direct hover
Description: The axes toolbar is slow to appear over the set of axes.  In some cases, if you do not hover over the set of axes before hovering over the axes toolbar, then the axes toolbar does not appear at all.

Summary: Using Outgoing Links Editor dialog may result in breaking existing links to requirements
Description: When you use the legacy Outgoing Links Editor to modify existing links in Simulink Requirements, some links may appear broken after making changes to other links.? For example, if you open the Outgoing Links Editor for a Simulink block that has two outgoing links to?requirements?in .slreqx, and delete the 1st link, the second link may be highlighted as unresolved (yellow icon) in the Links View.? This does not happen every time, but may happen under certain conditions that may not be immediately clear.? For example, this may happen when the remaining link stores a relative path to the local file, while the current user preference for document file path reference is NONE (short name only) or ABSOLUTE (full path to file).
Workaround: Do not use the Outgoing Links Editor to modifying existing links.? You can still use it to add new links where source object does not yet have links.If you need to delete an existing link, you can use the Delete action in the Links View in Simulink Requirements.?If you need to modify the description label for the link, you can edit it in Simulink Requirements?Editor or using the newer (slreq.*) command-line API.? Note that it is not possible to modify the type of the link in Outgoing Links Editor.

Summary: Polyspace Access dashboard values not updated
Description: When you perform certain operations in the Polyspace Access dashboard, the values displayed in the dashboard are not updated. The dashboard displays older cached data instead. This behavior might happen when: You move a project to a folder that already has cached dashboard data. You upload a new run to a project.
Workaround: Use the following steps to work around this issue:Disable the Polyspace Access web server cache by appending /setcachemode/NoCache to the URL you use log in to Polyspace Access. For example:https://access-host:9443/setcachmode/NoCacheThen continue using Polyspace Access using the regular URL. For example:https://access-host:9443Note that disabling the cache decreases the performance of Polyspace Access.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect raw-to-physical and physical-to-raw conversion errors in A2L files
Description: When using A2L files with LINEAR and COMPU_VTAB characteristics, the COMPU_METHODs for converting between physical and raw data might compute incorrect results.

Summary: Unequal image size error returned by the trainYOLOv2ObjectDetector function.
Description: The trainYOLOv2ObjectDetector function returns an "Unexpected image size " error while training a detector. The error can occur when you use unequal image sizes or when object bounding boxes are close to the image border. The error the function returns is:   Error using nnet.internal.cnn.MultiInputMultiOutputMiniBatchDatastoreDispatcher>iCellTo4DArray (line 389)?Unexpected image size: All images must have the same size.   Error in nnet.internal.cnn.MultiInputMultiOutputMiniBatchDatastoreDispatcher/  defaultResponseBatchingFcn (line 292)??

Summary: Sample time specified for an element of a bus port might be ignored
Description: Sample time specified for an element of a bus port might be ignored if all of the following conditions apply: The element is a nonbus signal. A bus object defines the element. The element does not directly correspond to an Out Bus Element block.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when generating code for a model with flexible bodies and certain types of output signals
Description: MATLAB crashes when generating C code for a Simscape Multibody model containing both of the following: 1) At least one General Flexible Beam block 2) At least one of the following modeling patterns: Any block from the "Joints" sub-library, when sensing accelerations, actuation forces or torques, and when enabling any of the outputs from the "Composite Force/Torque Sensing" section The Transform Sensor block from the "Frames and Transforms" sub-library, sensing any of the outputs from the "Angular Acceleration" and "Acceleration" sections Any block from the "Constraints" sub-library, sensing any forces and torques    This can occur during Rapid Accelerator simulation or when generating C code using Simulink Coder or Embedded Coder, for example.

Summary: Data type might be automatically specified for new element of bus port
Description: When you change the element specified by an In Bus Element or Out Bus Element block, signal attributes might transfer from the original element to the new element. Since you did not specify signal attributes for the new element, the signal attribute specification might cause unexpected behavior.

Summary: In Bus Element and Out Bus Element blocks might display incorrect compiled attributes
Description: In Bus Element and Out Bus Element blocks might display incorrect compiled attributes if they specify signal attributes for an element that does not have a corresponding In Bus Element and Out Bus Element block.

Summary: Output underflow error in Analog Output Generator crashes MATLAB?
Description: When using the Analog Output Generator app with audio channels, an underflow error might occur. Closing the error pop-up results in a MATLAB crash.

Summary: Running certain web apps causes MATLAB Web App Server performance degradation?
Description: Some MATLAB apps created using App Designer use 100% of the CPU when running for a long time. These apps, when deployed as web apps, slow down the response of the MATLAB Web App Server and cause the performance of the server to degrade.
Workaround: Restart the MATLAB Web App Server service. The name of the service depends on the operating system you are using:Windows Service Name:? MATLAB Web Apps (R2018b) or MATLAB Web Apps (R2019a)Linux and macOS Service Name: mw-webapps-R2018b or mw-webapps-R2019a

Summary: Induction Machine (Single-Phase) block produces incorrect torque when machine does not have exactly two pole pairs
Description: The Induction Machine (Single-Phase) block produces incorrect torque when the machine does not have exactly two pole pairs. A power sign error in the per-unit torque equation for this block causes the torque on the mechanical end to be incorrect. The power sign does not follow the standard motor convention. The convention is that when the auxiliary winding current leads the main winding current, the machine torque is negative, and when the main?winding current leads the auxiliary winding current, the machine torque is positive.?

Summary: Crash or error during code generation of a "for" loop if a use of the loop variable is removed by early optimization
Description: MATLAB might crash or error during code generation of a "for" loop, if a usage of the loop variable is removed by early optimization. The loop variable refers to the variable immediately following "for". If this occurs, the stack trace generated by MATLAB includes this line: EML::InferImpl::InferFor::defcheck_for at b:\  matlab\  src\  eml\  dom_eml\  infer\  inferfor.cpp:544 (in matlab\  bin\  win64\  eml.dll)

Summary: Simulation target type redefinition error when multiple S-functions share header declaring user-defined types
Description: The simulation target for a model might fail to build when all of the following conditions are true: The model contains multiple S-functions generated by Legacy Code Tool. The S-functions include a common header file that contains a user-defined data type.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reusable referenced model with instance-specific parameter inside a For Each Subsystem block can lead to wrong answer
Description: The code generator can generate incorrect code for? a model when all of these conditions are true: The model is referenced by a parent model running in Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator simulation mode. The model's configuration parameter?Total number of instances allowed per top model ?is set to?Multiple . The model has a For Each Subsystem block which references another model. The referenced model's configuration parameter Total number of instances allowed per top model  is set to Multiple .? The referenced model's model workspace has a parameter object, whose Storage Class  column is set to Model default , and the check box in the?Argument  column is selected.  When these conditions exist, the incorrect code generated for the model can lead to incorrect simulation results.

Summary: AUTOSAR adaptive generated code might cause run-time signal handler registration error
Description: The main.cpp  code generated for an AUTOSAR adaptive component model uses signal handlers. When registering the signal handlers, the generated code might omit a statement setting sigaction  flags to 0 ( action.sa_flags = 0 ), indicating no flags. Executing the code generates the following run-time error: Registering signal handler failed:? Not Supported

Summary: AUTOSAR adaptive generated code might cause run-time error related to timerspec on some platforms
Description: Code generated from an AUTOSAR adaptive component model contains a timerspec structure, used in scheduling step function execution. The timerspec structure can specify time as a combination of seconds and nanoseconds. However, the generated code only uses the nanoseconds field to specify the step time, even for rates over 1 second. Some platforms require the nanoseconds value to be less than a second, and generate a run-time error when executing Simulink generated code with rates over 1 second.

Summary: Generated code for AUTOSAR adaptive model with root inport that subscribes to event might cause run-time exception
Description: Code generated from an AUTOSAR adaptive model might cause a run-time exception for an uninitialized service proxy object, if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a root inport that subscribes to an event from a service instance. The adaptive application tries to find the service instance using the FindService  function. The adaptive application initializes the service proxy object using the service instance found with FindService .  Under these conditions, the generated code contains an incorrect unconditional event subscription call. At run-time, when the adaptive application executes, the service instance might not be available. If the service instance is not available, the service proxy object in the adaptive application remains uninitialized. The uninitialized service proxy object causes a run-time exception.

Summary: Simulink might crash when simulating a model that logs frame-based signals
Description: When you log a signal as frame-based, Simulink might crash while starting the simulation. The crash usually happens when you log a scalar signal as frame-based, but might also occur when you log non-scalar signals as frame-based.? To check whether a signal is logged as frame-based, right-click the logging badge on the signal and select Properties . A signal logged as frame-based has the Input Processing  property set to Columns as channels (frame based) .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Preprocessor conditionals may be missing for Variant Subsystem with export-function models as choices
Description: Variant Subsystem invoked by an asynchronous system?may generate incorrect code in the below scenario:? Variant Subsystem? has two choice M1 and M2, where M1 and M2 are two model blocks referring to two export-function models.  M1 has V == 1 and M2 has V == 2 conditions set by the Variant Subsystem.   An example generated code:  void myasynccall() {?  ? M1();?  ? M2();  }?  Expected code:  void myasynccall() {?  #if V == 1  ? M1();?  #endif?  #if V == 2?  ? M2();  #endif?  }?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation with For Each Subsystems and Variants
Description: The generated code is incorrect when all the following conditions are true :   The inputs to a For Each Subsystem are partitioned using the Input Partition  pane of the For Each  block inside a For Each Subsystem. The partitioned inport block has a variant condition.

Summary: Tab groups are incorrectly laid out in a grid layout?
Description: A tab group parented to a grid layout always shows up in the top left corner of the grid, regardless of the layout option specified by the user.?As a result, when running an app containing tab groups parented to a grid layout, tab groups will be incorrectly laid out, potentially covering other components.?
Workaround: Temporarily place the tab group in a different cell, then call the drawnow function, and finally set it to the final location you want it to appear at in the app.Example (programmatically outside App Designer):f = uifigure;g = uigridlayout(f);c = uibutton(g);tg = uitabgroup(g);% Workaround: Set the tab group temporarily in a different location and call drawnowtg.Layout.Row = 2;drawnow;% Set the tab group to the desired final locationtg.Layout.Row = 1;Example (interactively in App Designer):1. Drag a tab group into a cell, e.g. row 3, column 12. Create a startup function and place the workaround in it, e.g.% Set the tab group temporarily in a different location and call drawnowapp.TabGroup.Layout.Row = 4;drawnow;?% Set the tab group to the desired final locationapp.TabGroup.Layout.Row = 3;Note:The temporary location MUST be different from the final location you set.To make sure that is the case, you can set the temporary location to a cell 'outside' the grid, for example, if the grid is of size 5x5, set the temporary Row to 6.An alternative is to add 1 to the row (or column), and then place it back.
