Summary: Behavior of SerDes clock recovery blocks lock to sub-optimal position and Analog Channel block uses wrong impedance when single-ended signaling resulting in larger variation of DFE tap weight in time-domain adaptation
Description: Simulation behavior of SerDes clock recovery blocks (serdes.CDR and serdes.DFECDR) lock to sub-optimal position and the Analog Channel block uses wrong impedance when single-ended signaling. This results in larger than expected difference in DFE tap weight between LTI/Init analysis and time-domain adaptation.?When you set the ˇ®ModeˇŻ parameter of the DFECDR block to 'Adapt', the time-domain simulation adaptation of the DFE tap weights do not arrive at the optimum solution (defined by the LTI/Init analysis) because of a clock recovery phase offset from the ideal position. This issue becomes more noticeable when the ˇ®SignalingˇŻ parameter in the Configuration block is set to ˇ®Single-endedˇŻ and ˇ°Channel modelˇŻ parameter of the Analog Channel block is set to ˇ®Loss modelˇŻ.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data Type Conversion block with Nearest Integer rounding mode might sometimes generate HDL incorrect code
Description: Conversion of a floating-point value to a fixed-point value might generate incorrect HDL code that differs from Simulink behavior when: The floating-point value is outside the representable range of the data type such as?single(-0.024180471897125)  The conversion is performed using a Data Type Conversion block with Integer rounding mode  set to Nearest ?

Summary: Simulink Diagram reporter can generate illegible diagrams
Description: The Simulink Diagram reporter can generate illegible diagrams. This occurs when a complex diagram contains fine detail. For example, the operators on arithmetic blocks are illegible in complex block diagrams.
Workaround: Set the Scaling, MaxHeight, and MaxWidth properties of the slreportgen.report.Diagram reporter as follows:    diagReporter.Scaling = "zoom";    diagReporter.MaxHeight = "100in";    diagReporter.MaxWidth = "100in";This script uses these settings to generate fully legible diagrams:import mlreportgen.dom.*import mlreportgen.report.*import slreportgen.report.*import slreportgen.finder.*model_name = 'f14';load_system(model_name);rpt = slreportgen.report.Report('myreport', 'pdf');rpt.CompileModelBeforeReporting = false;finder = DiagramFinder(model_name);results = find(finder);for result = results    chapter = Chapter('Title', result.Name);    diagReporter = getReporter(result);    diagReporter.Snapshot.ScaleToFit = true;    diagReporter.Scaling = "zoom";    diagReporter.MaxHeight = "100in";    diagReporter.MaxWidth = "100in";    add(chapter, diagReporter);    add(rpt, chapter);endclose(rpt);close_system(model_name);rptview(rpt);

Summary: Composite thermal variant?of Switched Reluctance Machine block triggers an error
Description: Some internal connections are missing in the thermal variants of the Switched Reluctance Machine block. Models that include the composite thermal variant of the block trigger simulation errors.

Summary: Tabular edits in Signal Editor user interface might cause data values to be rearranged
Description: The Signal Editor user interface table might rearrange data values if you enter one or more duplicate time values in the table.

Summary: AC Cable block overestimates self-inductance
Description: The AC Cable block calculates self-inductance as a function of the specified geometry parameters. The calculated self-inductance is overestimated by a factor of two.

Summary: Live script becomes unusable after text that is selected from right to left is replaced using the Find & Replace tool
Description: If a manual selection is made in a live script from right to left and then replaced using the Find & Replace tool, the live script stops working correctly and becomes unusable.
Workaround: Close the live script and reopen it. Make a left-to-right selection to perform the replace instead of a right-to-left selection.

Summary: MATLAB crashes if exit or quit are run from a MATLAB timer object
Description: MATLAB crashes if?exit ?or?quit ?are run from a MATLAB timer object.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Scalar variable incorrectly indexed as an array in generated code
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:A scalar input branches and feeds into to two ports of a subsystem. Inside the subsystem, one branch feeds a nested For Each subsystem. The second branch does not feed a For Each subsystem.  In the incorrect generated code, the scalar input (for example, int myVar ) is passed as a pointer to the function representing the outer subsystem. The pointer is then incorrectly indexed as if it were an array (for example, myVar[42] )? inside the subsystem function body. The executable created from the generated code will cause a crash during run-time.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect?results are possible for a model containing?floating-point to fixed-point data type conversion when the Code replacement library is?AUTOSAR 4.0
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a floating-point to fixed-point data type conversion (cast) operation, performed by a Data Type Conversion block or another block such as an Addition block. In the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Code replacement library  parameter? is set to AUTOSAR 4.0 ? In the generated code, the data type conversion operation is replaced with AUTOSAR's? data type conversion routine Mfl_Cvrt_f32_outtype  . The fixed-point data type of the data type conversion operation output has a non-unit slope (that is, a value other than 1).  The generated value for the second input argument for Mfl_Cvrt_f32_outtype   incorrectly uses the fixed exponent  of the output data type rather than the expected value of?-1* fixed exponent.

Summary: SAR ADC configured for 5 bits or less generates incorrect digital codes.
Description: WhenSAR ADC is configured for?5 ?bits or less, ADC output gets stuck to acertain digital code for specific inputs. For example, with 0.5  volts DC input,an?N -bit ADC, the digital output goes up to 2^(N -1), andthen freezes. So for a?3 -bit ADC, the output gets stuckat?4 ?instead of providing the correct digital output of?6 .Similarly, for?4 -?and?5 -bit ADCs, the output gets stuckat?8 ?and?16 ?respectively

Summary: Detailed description of dead logic found in C/C++ custom code is not reported
Description: When you perform dead logic analysis on a model that contains C/C++ custom code and if dead logic is found in the code, Simulink Design Verifier does not report the detailed description of the dead logic.    For example, if there is dead logic in C/C++ code associated with S-Function or C Caller block, then the presence of dead logic is reported. But, the details such as C/C++ file name and the line number that results in dead logic are not reported.

Summary: Passing arrays of fixed-point values to the complex function can cause a size mismatch error during code generation
Description: This issue occurs when all of the following conditions are true:? In your MATLAB code, an input to the complex ?function is a multi-dimensional array of fixed-point values. The code generator can determine the sizes of some of the dimensions of this array. The code generator cannot determine the sizes of the other dimensions of this array. These dimensions are variable-size.  In such situations, the code generator can treat all dimensions of the array that the?complex ?function returns as variable-size. This behavior can cause a size mismatch in a subsequent assignment or expression. Here is an example MATLAB code that is affected by this issue. function D = test(n) % B is a vector. B = repmat(fi(1,1,16,10), n, 1); % C is treated as a matrix during code generation. Both dimensions of C are% treated as variable-size because the value of the fixed size of the% second dimension is lost when complex() is called. C = complex(B,B); % D is a vector. D = repmat(fi(complex(1),1,16,10), n, 1); % Size mismatch:% the left hand side is 1x1% the right hand side is 1x? D(1,:) = C(1,:); This issue affects MATLAB Coder and the use of MATLAB in Simulink via MATLAB Function blocks, MATLAB System blocks or MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Security Issue: User credentials might be visible in Polyspace Access R2019a web server log when using deprecated URL to sign in
Description: Security updates are available for Polyspace Access R2019a.  If you use a deprecated sign-in URL (https://hostname:port/auth/login) user credentials might be visible in the web server log. No action is required if you use the recommended sign-in URL for Polyspace Access R2019a (https://hostname:port).? Download the?update ZIP file , and follow the update instructions here .

Summary: MATLAB might crash when BLAS functions are called in a nested context
Description: MATLAB might crash when following conditions are met: BLAS functions are called in nested functions The sub-function contains coder.inline('never')   For example, the following code pattern causes MATLAB to crash: function a = simple_blas(b, c, d) ? ? t = simple_mul(b, c);??? ? a = t * d;??end?  function t = simple_mul(a, b)?? ?coder.inline('never');?? ?t = a * b;?end?  You may experience similar issues with FFT  and LAPACK  functions.?
Workaround: Avoid using?coder.inline('never') when BLAS functions are called in a nested context.

Summary: Parallel build of referenced models can place artifacts in wrong folders if Simulation cache folder and Code generation folder differ
Description: If you specify different values for CacheFolder   and?CodeGenFolder  by using, for example, Simulink.fileGenControl , then a parallel build of referenced models can place some code generation artifacts in the simulation cache folder and some model build artifacts in the code generation folder.?

Summary: MATLAB crash while computing derived ranges for persistent matrix variables in float to fixed conversion
Description: A MATLAB crash occurs when both of the following conditions are met :? There is a persistent variable matrix with known dimensions. The base type of this variable is a numeric type other than double (e.g. int8, uint32, single).?  You see this snippet in the stack trace: ?  Assertion at b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\const\cg_const.cpp line 937:  bin\win64\cg_ir.dll cg_const_create_matrix_numeric_or_bool_from_double  bin\win64\emlcoder.dll EMC::setEnableDebugPrintIR  bin\win64\emlcoder.dll watermark::operator=

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The bin2dec?function does not treat 0?elements as white space
Description: For code generation, the? bin2dec ?function interprets  0  elements in the input array as  '0'  elements. MATLAB interprets  0  elements as white space. For example, in code generation? bin2dec(['1',0])? returns  2 .? In MATLAB it returns 1 .?
Workaround: If the input might contain 0 elements, replace them with ' '?before calling bin2dec.b(b == 0) = ' ';??x = bin2dec(b);
Products: MATLAB Coder, Simulink Coder, Embedded Coder

Summary: No error message when using overflowed in non-AUTOSAR target context
Description: Stateflow might not throw an error when the?overflowed  operator is used for either simulation and code generation for non-AUTOSAR targets. The operator is supported only for AUTOSAR targets.

Summary: Non-tunable parameters for a Simscape block are not initialized when using a Parameter Writer block
Description: This problem applies to a Model block that uses instance parameters and a Parameter Writer block to initialize non-tunable parameters for a Simscape block. The Simscape block is within the referenced model. The Parameter Writer block in the top model is able to change the values of the model instance parameters, but the changes are not applied to the non-tunable parameters for the Simscape block. The simulation result is incorrect.

Summary: AUTOSAR Signal Invalidation block does not accept non-zero enumeration values for initial condition
Description: Updating or building an AUTOSAR model generates an error if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a Signal Invalidation block that is connected to a root outport. The AUTOSAR data access mode of the root outport is set to ExplicitSend . The data value signal of the Signal Invalidation block has an enumerated data type. The initial condition of the Signal Invalidation block is a non-zero enumeration value.  The model generates an error stating that the Initial value enumerator type must have zero as the default value.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when converting a component to a variant, reference, or implementation when it is connected to a branched system input port
Description: There is a crash that occurs when the user does the following:  The user has a input system port which is connected to two or more components The user selects one of the above components and selects "Add Variant", "Save As Architecture", or "Create Simulink Behavior".

Summary: Error when using?activations,?classify, and?predict?with a TransformedDatastore?and 'Acceleration','MEX'.
Description: When the functions activations , classify , and predict   are used with a TransformedDatastore   object as the input data and the acceleration name-value pair 'Acceleration','MEX' , the MEX function is generated without error. However,??MATLAB returns an error when the MEX function is executed during subsequent calls of?activations ,?classify , or?predict . ?The error generated is Incorrect class forexpression 'in': expected 'single' but found 'gpuArray'.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Use of code replacement library in models containing For Each Subsystem might lead to incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated if all these conditions are true:?Model contains a For Each Subsystem. The model uses a custom code replacement library. At least one operation/function?is replaced by custom function in the generated code. For Each Subsystem's inports are arguments to the custom function. For Each Subsystem's?inports are connected to a model reference block. NumIters  should be greater than 1 and Partition Width  should be greater than 1 and not power of 2.

Summary: EtherCAT Async SSC/SoE Upload and Download blocks stop functioning after first execution
Description: EtherCAT Async SSC/SoE Upload and Download blocks work correctly when executed in a model the first time only, but not after that. These asynchronous SoE blocks read or write only the first time each block is enabled during model execution. You can use the write block for one-time initialization of parameters on a drive.

Summary: slreq.importViewSettings command does not work if the Requirements Editor or requirements perspective is used in the current session.
Description: slreq.importViewSettings  function works only if the Requirements Editor has not been opened in the current MATLAB session, and the requirements perspective has not been opened in the current MATLAB session.  slreq.exportViewSettings  function works only when the Requirements Editor is closed.

Summary: Size mismatch error when using?activations,?classify, and?predict with a datastore with non-square images and 'Acceleration','MEX'
Description: When using?inference methods (predict , classify  and activations ) with the option "Acceleration" set to "MEX"?on a datastore containing non-square images,?and ifthe number of images in the datastore is not a multiple of the minibatchsize, then you see an error like the following about a size mismatch during anassignment.  Unable to performassignment because the size of the left side is 28-by-28-by-1-by-14 and thesize of the right side is 28-by-40-by-1-by-14.?

Summary: Verification stuck in the C to Intermediate Language phase
Description: When translating a procedure that uses a large, complex array, the verification can appear to be stuck in the "C to Intermediate Language" phase, with an indication like the following:? ?- translating procedure foo1 (92 / 103)  ?- translating procedure foo2 (93 / 103)
Products: Polyspace Code Prover, Polyspace Code Prover Server

Summary: Simulation fails to report error when SimEvents blocks are connected to Inport and Outport blocks
Description: When a SimEvents block is connected to an Inport block or an Outport block inside an Atomic Subsystem block, simulation should report an error, indicating that this connection is not supported. No error appears and the simulation runs even though entity entry and departure from an?Atomic Subsystem block are not supported.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Entities carrying string type data might produce wrong simulation results
Description: Simulating a SimEvents model might produce wrong results when all of the following conditions are met:? An entity or a message carries string type data. The entity type can be Anonymous, Structured, or Bus object. The entity or the message is stored and there is a delay between the entity's generation, and the use of the entity's data.  When these conditions are met, reading of string type entitydata returns wrong values.

Summary: Simulink duplicates glyphs from an input method editor (IME) in notes
Description: In the?Notes? pane, when you insert a glyph using an IME, such as Chinese, Japanese, or Korean, the glyph is inserted twice.

Summary: If the axle torque input is zero, the Longitudinal Wheel block does not use the initial angular velocity block parameters
Description: If the axle torque input port?AxlTrq ?is zero, the Longitudinal Wheel block does not use the initial angular velocity block parameters. Specifically, the block does not use the?Wheel initial angular velocity, omegao ?parameter.
Workaround: Do not provide the axle torque?AxlTrq? input port a signal that is exactly zero. Instead, you can use a signal that is near zero, for example?2*eps?N*m, where eps is a very small number approaching zero. To achieve this, consider either approach:??Use a Sum block to add the axle torque signal to a Constant block with a value set to?2*eps?Use an IC block to set the initial condition of the axle torque?AxlTrq?input signal to??2*eps

Summary: Polyspace Metrics when used with Polyspace Server products might fail with error message: exception Option(Option) raised
Description: If you try to start Polyspace Metrics using a Polyspace Server product (with the polyspace-server-settings  executable), the following error appears in the polyspaced.log  file: * Checking CPUs configuration... ************************************Checking configuration for CPU my-hostname...Processor : Intel Core Processor (Skylake, IBRS)R.A.M. : 7.79 GBO.S. : Linux Debian 9.3User: poly- product 1 :- installation folder : /usr/local/Polyspace/R2019a_server- version : 10.0 (R2019a)exception Option(Option) raised.

Summary: Root-level Inport block holds final value when streaming data from a DatasetRef object?
Description: When you use a root-level Inport block to stream external input data from a DatasetRef  object, the block holds the final value at its output after the last data point in the external input data, regardless of the Interpolate Data setting. The block should only hold the final value when Interpolate Data is selected.

Summary: Index exceeds the number of array elements error when importing requirements from Excel
Description: When importing requirements from Microsoft Excel, if the unique ID column is the last column in the spreadsheet, the import operation can result in an error:? Index exceeds the number of array elements?

Summary: Undefined function error when running a MATLAB Function block with breakpoints
Description: If a model is run once, and rerun after adding a breakpoint, Simulink outputs the following error: Undefined function or variable 'fallbackType'.?  Caused by:?  Variable 'fallbackType' does not exist in base workspace??
Workaround: Delete the slprj folder from your working directory before running the model again.?

Summary: Polyspace-configure might miss some build information on Bazel when using Linux containers
Description: The polyspace-configure  command might miss some build information on Bazel when using Linux containers. The command can crash as a result and display the message Segmentation fault .
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify specific unit conversions in a Unit Conversion block
Description: For a Unit Conversion block, code inspection fails to verify conversions between these units: fpm ?to?fps  ft*lbf/ slug/ degR ?to?ft*lbf/ slug/ K  hr  to min  min  to s?

Summary: 'makima'?interpolation produces incorrect results with permuted?grid?coordinates and values.
Description: The 'makima' interpolation method produces incorrect results when operating on a permutedversion of the grid coordinates and grid values in 2-D, 3-D, and N-Dinterpolation with griddedInterpolant ,interp2 , interp3 , and interpn . The fix for this issue adjusts the underlying 'makima' formula, which changes the results in any number of dimensions, including 1-D.For 1-D 'makima'  interpolationwith griddedInterpolant  or interp1 , the results in R2019b andlater releases are slightly different, but equally valid.? As an example of the issue,  'makima'? interpolation on a 2-D grid (x,y,V)  does not produce the same results as interpolation on the 2-D grid (y,x,V.')  obtained by swapping the x  and y  coordinates and transposing the grid values V .? Namely, in the following example, the interpolation results Vq1  and Vq2  should be the same (up to a small numerical round-off difference), but instead differ by a large amount: ? ? % Grid coordinates (x,y)? ??x = 1:10;? ??y = 1:10;? ??% Grid values: V(i,j) represents the value at grid node (x(i),y(j))?? ??V = peaks(10);? ??% Query coordinates: new points where we want to interpolate? ??xq = linspace(1, 10, 100);? ??yq = xq;?? ??% Interpolate the original 2-D grid (x,y,V)? ??G1 = griddedInterpolant({x,y},V, 'makima');? ??Vq1 = G1({xq, yq});? ??% Interpolate the transposed grid (y,x,V.') and transpose back? ??V_swap_xy = V.';? ??G2 = griddedInterpolant({y,x},V_swap_xy, 'makima'); ? ??Vq2 = G2({yq,xq});? ??Vq2 = Vq2.';? ??% The difference between the two interpolation results?Vq1?and?Vq2?should be zero? ? % (or around 1e-15 to account for different numerical round-off between the two).? ? % But this is not the case:? ??max(max(abs(Vq1-Vq2)))? ??ans =? ??? ? 0.0130

Summary: SVG not supported for DOM image objects in Microsoft Word reports
Description: SVG format is supported for mlreportgen.dom.Image  objects in HTML and PDF reports, but not in Word reports.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when deleting a subsystem that contains a Model block
Description: MATLAB might crash when you delete a Subsystem block that contains a Model block if all of these conditions apply:? The Model block references a model that is referenced by other Model blocks in the model hierarchy. The referenced model is open in more than one tab in the same window.

Summary: MATLAB Online incorrectly replaces selection with "undefined" when refactoring code into a function.
Description: When refactoring code into a function in the MATLAB Online Editor and Live Editor,?the selection is incorrectly replaced by the text "undefined". The selection should instead be replaced by the name of the newly created function file. This occurs regardless of the content of the selection or the name of the refactored function.??

Summary: Linker error when running the example "Integrating GPU Coder into Simulink"
Description: The example "Integrating GPU Coder into Simulink" generates an undefined reference linker error when the model is updated. For example, on a Windows platform,? you might see an error similar to the following: ### Linking ... link.exe /nologo /dll /MANIFEST /OPT:NOREF /export:mexFunction /export:mexfilerequiredapiversion /OUT:gpucoder_sobelEdge_sfun.mexw64 /map:"gpucoder_sobelEdge_sfun.map" @gpucoder_sobelEdge_sfun.mol Creating library gpucoder_sobelEdge_sfun.lib and object gpucoder_sobelEdge_sfun.exp c2_gpucoder_sobelEdge.obj : error LNK2019: unresolved external symbol sobelEdge referenced in function sf_gateway_c2_gpucoder_sobelEdge gpucoder_sobelEdge_sfun.mexw64 : fatal error LNK1120: 1 unresolved externals NMAKE : fatal error U1077: '"C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio 14.0\VC\Bin\amd64\link.exe"' : return code '0x460' Stop.Component:Make | Category:Make errorUnable to create mex function 'gpucoder_sobelEdge_sfun.mexw64' required for simulation.  Starting with R2018b, the target code generation language when using GPU Coder is C++. However, the?gpucoderdemo_sobelEdge.slx  model in this example still uses C as its target language. As a result of this language mismatch, the code generation tool is not able to resolve the external symbols resulting in the error shown.
Workaround: Change the target language of the Simulink model to C++ by following these steps:Open the?gpucoder_sobelEdge.slx model in Simulink.In the Simulink editor, select Simulation > Model Configuration Parameters.In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, select Code Generation?pane.In the Target selection section, set the Language to C++ and click Apply.In the?Configuration Parameters?dialog box, select?Simulation Target?pane.Add a space character (" ") under Additional build information > Include directories.Click OK and save the model.Run the example again to simulate Sobel edge detection in Simulink with GPU Coder.The attached screenshots show these steps.

Summary: Error when updating Simulink diagram containing bus element ports directly?connected to explicit partitions
Description: Connecting a bus element port directly to an explicit partition results in an error, and a possible MATLAB crash.
Workaround: Place a block that belongs to an implicit partition between the bus element port and the explicit partition.

Summary: Many examples missing in HDL Coder documentation
Description: When you install MATLAB and open the documentation for HDL Coder, you see a number of examples missing from the documentation. For example: When you view the Examples section of the HDL Coder documentation, the page shows only 19 examples. This view should include more than 100 examples.  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? >> web(fullfile(matlabroot, '/help/hdlcoder/examples.html')) On some documentation pages the Featured Examples section does not display any examples. For instance, see the Getting Started category.   ? ? ? ? ? ? ?>> web(fullfile(matlabroot, '/help/hdlcoder/getting-started-with-hdl-coder.html'))
Workaround: All examples, including those missing from the install, are available in the HDL Coder web documentation. To open the web documentation and see the examples, enter this URL in your web browser:?https://www.mathworks.com/help/hdlcoder/examples.html??You must login to your MathWorks Account to see this page, if you haven't already.?

Summary: LibGetNumRuntimeExportedTasks always return 1
Description: LibGetNumRuntimeExportedTasks  always return 1 which may result in incorrect answer.

Summary: Using coder.checkGpuInstall or MEX acceleration on a machine with Turing GPU may result in an error
Description: When the?coder.checkGpuInstall  API ?is run on a machine with Turing GPU (compute capability of 7.5 or higher), you might get the following error: Error setting property 'ComputeCapability' of class 'GpuConfig': Invalid value '7.5'.   Allowed values are: 3.2,  3.5,  3.7,  5.0,  5.2,  5.3,  6.0,  6.1,  6.2,  7.0,  7.1,  7.2   The same error is encountered when performing?MEX Acceleration  for a deep learning network object on a machine with Turing GPU.? For example,? >> net = alexnet;  >> im = imresize(imread('peppers.png'),[227 227]);  >> classify(net,im,'Acceleration','mex');       Note that this does not affect GPU code generation. You may set? ComputeCapability  to a lower value or? use CustomComputeCapability  property in code configuration object. For more information, see?Configuration parameters for CUDA code generation .

Summary: Invalid input of type slreq.data.SourceItem error when querying links from IBM Rational DOORS Next Generation widget browser window
Description: After selecting an item in IBM Rational DOORS Next Generation, if you click Query links from SL ?in the custom widget in the Mini Dashboard, an error is displayed:  Invalid input of type slreq.data.SourceItem

Summary: Simulation or code generation target rebuilds unnecessarily
Description: A model reference simulation or code generation target rebuilds unnecessarily when all of the following conditions apply:  A model workspace defines a model argument used by the referenced model. The model workspace is stored in either a MAT-file or MATLAB file. The MAT-file or MATLAB file changed.

Summary: False red C++ check when calling a method from an array element
Description: In very rare situations involving double inheritance and a method call from an array element, Code Prover can give a false red check.? Here is an example:? struct A{     virtual ~A() ; }; struct B {     virtual void f() { }; }; struct C : public A, public B{ }; int main() {     C cs[2];     C* c = &cs[1];     c->f();  // red OOP check}; Polyspace returns the following information and displays a red check: ID 4: Incorrect object oriented programming  Error: incorrect this-pointer type of f  The type of the this  pointer is correct, so the check should be green.

Summary: Aerospace Blockset block World Magnetic Model 2015 block and Aerospace Toolbox wrldmagm function do not support WMM2015v2 model
Description: Aerospace Blockset block World Magnetic Model 2015  block and Aerospace Toolbox wrldmagm  function do not support the WMM2015v2 model, released by NOAA in February, 2019. This new model addresses degraded performance of WMM2015(v1) in the Arctic region and supersedes it. For more information about the WMM2015v2 release, see?https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/news/world-magnetic-model-out-cycle-release .

Summary: Error dialog might display when calling Test Harness check from the UI or with sltest.harness.check
Description: An error dialog containing the following text might be displayed when calling Test Harness check from the UI or with sltest.harness.check :? An error has occurred during execution of Action named checkHarnessModelAction.?  Caused by:?  Undefined variable "studio" or class "studio.raise".?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer when generating code for a model with joint state targets and flexible bodies or floating mechanisms
Description: Wrong answers are possible when executing generated C code for a Simscape Multibody model containing the following: 1) Joint state targets (whether position or velocity) 2) At least one of the following modeling patterns: Floating mechanisms (systems of components that are not explicitly connected to a World Frame block via a joint block) General Flexible Beam blocks  Under these circumstances the joint state targets might be ignored, leading to incorrect initialization of the model and subsequently erroneous results when simulating the model using the generated code. This can occur during Rapid Accelerator simulation or when generating and executing C code using Simulink Coder or Embedded Coder, for example.
Workaround: For the specific case of rigid multibody systems with implicit 6-DOF joints, i.e., floating mechanisms not containing any flexible body blocks, explicitly add World Frame blocks and 6-DOF Joint blocks to the model, removing the need for Simscape Multibody to implicitly add the joints.

Summary: "File does not exist" error when updating results of previous custom import
Description: You might see an error about a missing MAT file ("File XYZ.mat does not exist" ) when you try to perform the Update action under these conditions: You have an updated version of the external requirements document with a different requirement module or top-level container ID due to a configuration or version number change. You rely on slreq.ReqSet.updateSrcFileLocation()  API to reconfigure your previously imported content to match the updated ID of the source document.

Summary: Tests might execute slowly when there are many logged signals including at least one verify signal
Description: When the model simulated in the test case contains many logged signals, and at least one of these is a verify signal, test execution may be slow.

Summary: Polyspace plugin in Eclipse cannot run on source files in virtual folders
Description: When you try to run Polyspace on a source file in a virtual folder, this message appears: The operation is not applicable to the current selection. Select a source file in the Project Explorer and retry.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Powertrain Blockset incorrectly checks out an Aerospace Blockset license when running models saved in R2017b, R2017a, or R2016b
Description: If you run a model saved in R2017b, R2017a, or R2016b that contains vehicle body blocks, Powertrain Blockset can incorrectly check out an Aerospace Blockset license. Specifically, models that contain these blocks can incorrectly check out a license:  Vehicle Body Total Road Load Vehicle Body 1DOF Longitudinal Vehicle Body 3DOF Longitudinal?
Workaround: In the model, replace these vehicle body blocks with the same blocks from the Simulink library browser:?Powertrain Blockset > Vehicle Dynamics > Vehicle Body 1DOF LongitudinalPowertrain Blockset > Vehicle Dynamics > Vehicle Body 3DOF LongitudinalPowertrain Blockset > Vehicle Dynamics > Vehicle Body Total Road LoadAfter you replace the blocks, make sure that you update the parameters with values that were in the original model.

Summary: 20-MHz preamble segments are incorrectly transmitted for some HE TB configurations
Description: Some 20-MHzsubchannels in the pre-HE portion of an 802.11ax high-efficiency trigger-based (HE TB) waveform are incorrectly transmitted.This problem occurs when generating 802.11ax HE TB waveforms: In R2018a by using the heWaveformGenerator ? function in the "802.11ax Parameterization for Waveform Generation and Simulation" example.? In R2018b or later by using the heTBWaveformGenerator  function in the "802.11ax Parameterization for Waveform Generation and Simulation" and?"802.11ax Packet Error Rate Simulation for Uplink Trigger-Based Format" examples.  For example, in R2018a, this code generates an HE TB waveform where only the third 20-MHz subchannel is transmitted, but both the third and fourth subchannels should be transmitted. You can view this with the plotAllocation  function.? cfg = heTriggerBasedConfig;cfg.ChannelBandwidth = 'CBW80';cfg.RUSize = 242;cfg.RUIndex = 3;tx = heWaveformGenerator(1,cfg);plotAllocation(cfg);?  This code demonstrates the issue in or after R2018b.? cfg = heTBConfig;cfg.ChannelBandwidth = 'CBW80';cfg.RUSize = 242;cfg.RUIndex = 3;tx = heTBWaveformGenerator(1,cfg); plotAllocation(cfg);

Summary: Database Toolbox truncates data when importing a database column with the nvarchar(max) data type from a Microsoft SQL Server database
Description: When you import data into MATLAB from a Microsoft SQL Server database, the ODBC interface of the Database Toolbox truncates data that comes from a database column with the nvarchar(max)  data type.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Bus connected to atomic subsystem might produce silent wrong answer or make MATLAB unresponsive
Description: Simulink might produce a silent wrong answer when all of these conditions apply: You select a nested nonvirtual bus from a nonvirtual bus. The selected nonvirtual bus becomes an input to a virtual bus. The virtual bus connects to an atomic subsystem. In the atomic subsystem, you select a signal from the nested nonvirtual bus.  MATLAB might become unresponsive during update diagram when all of these conditions apply:? A virtual bus contains a nonvirtual bus. The virtual bus connects to an atomic subsystem.
Workaround: Unzip and run the attached script every time you start Simulink. The script enables Simulink to convert a nonvirtual bus into a virtual bus when the nonvirtual bus is contained in a virtual bus.

Summary: Incorrect validation of SpatialMappingMatrix property in heTBConfig and heTriggerBasedConfig objects
Description: The existing implementation requires you to provide a full-band spatial mapping matrix for smaller resource unit (RU) sizes. If you provide a full-band spatial mapping matrix, the incorrect RU is extracted from it. This problem occurs when you generate an 802.11ax high-efficiency trigger-based (HE TB) waveform: In R2018a by using the heWaveformGenerator ? function with the SpatialMapping  property of the heTriggerBasedConfig ? object set to 'Custom' . You can find this functionality ?in the "802.11ax Parameterization for Waveform Generation and Simulation" example.? In R2018b or later by using the heTBWaveformGenerator ? function with the SpatialMapping  property of the heTBConfig ? object set to 'Custom' . You can find this functionality in the "802.11ax Parameterization for Waveform Generation and Simulation" and?"802.11ax Packet Error Rate Simulation for Uplink Trigger-Based Format" examples.

Summary: Coding rules violations for MISRA-C:2012 are missing from results
Description: If you run a Polyspace Bug Finder analysis (R2019a) for checking MISRA-C:2012  violations and try to import comments in the same run from pre-R2019a results, the previous coding rule violations might not appear in the new results. If the issue happens, an error message like this appears in the log: ps_results_db: could not find an internal (family=12) result type named 'MISRA_C3_DIR_1_1'ps_results_db: could not find an internal (family=12) result type named 'MISRA_C3_DIR_2_1'ps_results_db: could not find an internal (family=12) result type named 'MISRA_C3_DIR_3_1'ps_results_db: could not find an internal (family=12) result type named 'MISRA_C3_DIR_4_1'
Workaround: This issue occurs in the first analysis only. Perform another analysis to see the results.

Summary: In SAR ADC architecture input voltage range errors out when input voltage is not multiple of 0.5 volts.
Description: Simulation of a SAR ADC block whose input voltage range is not multiple of 0.5 volts throws a run-time error. For example, with an input voltage range vector of [-1 1] volts, the SAR ADC works as expected. However, using an input voltage range of [-sqrt(2) sqrt(2)] volts or [-0.8 0.8] volts results in abrupt termination of simulation with a run-time error.

Summary: MATLAB might crash while building a model with a Reusable custom storage class specification on root i/o
Description: MATLAB may crash while building a model that meets the following conditions: A pair of root-level inport and outport signals contain the same Reusable custom storage class specification. At least two blocks are in between the pair of root-level inport and outport blocks.
Workaround: Instead of a Reusable custom storage class, use function prototype control to achieve the reuse of root-level inport and outport. For more information, see?Optimize Buffering for Arguments.

Summary: MATLAB crash during XCP data acquisition in Simulink with Vector XCP Simulator
Description: MATLAB might crash when the XCP master blocks receive a DTO with an ODT number that is out of bounds. This happens while using the CAN XCP blocks with the Vector XCP Simulator.

Summary: Performance regression using Variant Manager for model with large data dictionary
Description: Variant Manager?may be slow when Simulink model is linked to a data dictionary containing large number of Simulink.Parameters. This may occur for any of the below scenarios: Variant configuration activation.  Launching Variant Reducer.  Usage of the?Simulink.VariantManager.findVariantControlVars  API from the command line.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Initial Condition parameters for blocks are not initialized when using a Parameter Writer block
Description: This problem applies to a Model block that uses instance parameters and a Parameter Writer block to set the initial conditions of blocks inside a masked subsystem. The subsystem is within the referenced model. The Parameter Writer block in the top model is able to change the values of the model instance parameters, but the changes are not applied to the initial conditions of the blocks. Simulation output is incorrect.

Summary: Component ports do not get updated correctly for a linked component when ports are modified in the reference model
Description: Adding a port to a referenced model does not create a corresponding port correctly on components that reference the model. New ports are available visually and can be connected in the Composition Editor, but cannot be accessed programmatically or viewed in a Spotlight View.

Summary: parfor loops can stall after automatically attaching files
Description: When running a parfor  loop where the workers are running on a MATLAB Parallel Server cluster, execution can stall immediately after you attach files to the pool that are needed to execute the loop. When the problem occurs, the loop stops and you see the following message in the command window:  Analyzing and transferring files to the workers ...done.
Workaround: To avoid the problem, explicitly attach all files required by the parfor loop to the parallel pool before executing the parfor loop. Use the addAttachedFiles method of the parallel pool.

Summary: Analysis returns error in log file No OIL files found in OSEK multitasking mode
Description: Polyspace analysis can finish with a completed status on the server and the following message in the log file: Error: No OIL files found in automatic multitasking mode. In 'custom' mode,? the '-osek-multitasking' option looks for OIL files in all provided directories. Make sure that they contain at least one file with .oil extension. This can happen systematically with an analysis sent to the server using the option -osek-multitasking  and batch mode option -batch .
Products: Polyspace Code Prover Server, Polyspace Code Prover

Summary: Inactivity status not detected so license not released
Description: The Polyspace user interface does not detect idle status. So, as long as the user interface is open, a license can be checked out indefinitely even when you are not performing actions in the user interface.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when opening Simulink on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 6
Description: When Red Hat 6 is missing the redhat-lsb package, MATLAB might crash when opening Simulink. A typical installation of Red Hat 6 includes this package, so this issue occurs only when Red Hat 6 was installed with a "minimal" installation, or the redhat-lsb package was manually uninstalled.
Workaround: Install the redhat-lsb package by entering the following in a command terminal:  # yum install redhat-lsb

Summary: Navigation fails from external document to Simulink, displays "server not found" error
Description: Simulink Requirements does not honor the setting for?Enable external connectivity at MATLAB startup . As a result, when you click on a navigation hyperlink from an external document to a Simulink model, MATLAB displays a "server not found" error.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when saving a Simulink model with custom code
Description: If a Simulink model has custom code specified in the Configuration Parameters  > Simulation Target , saving this model might trigger access violation with stack trace indicating: SLCC::CCScopeData::getScopeDataAsMxArray

Summary: MATLAB might crash with inactive Simulink Function in an Export-Function model
Description: MATLAB might crash during update diagram when a Simulink model contains all of the following:  Variant inactive Simulink Function  Variant inactive Function-Call Subsystem (Export-Function model)   The stack trace includes the following lines:  matlab/ bin/ glnxa64/ libmwsl_engine_classes.so  matlab/ bin/ glnxa64/ libmwsl_compile.so  matlab/ bin/ glnxa64/ libmwsimulink.so

Summary: Output modules not synchronized with NI 9215 (BNC)
Description: Output modules and the NI 9215 (BNC) are not automatically synchronized when they are in the same CompactDAQ chassis. ?For example, the following code shows that an input can be significantly out of phase with the output. s = daq.createSession('ni');% NI cDAQ-9179 (cDAQ2)aiDev = 'cDAQ2Mod8'; % NI 9215 (BNC)aoDev = 'cDAQ2Mod6'; % NI 9263addAnalogOutputChannel(s, aoDev, 0, 'Voltage');addAnalogInputChannel(s, aiDev, 0, 'Voltage');s.Rate = 25000; f = 1000;A = 1;t = linspace(0,1,round(s.Rate))';Vout = A*sin(2*pi*f*t);%%queueOutputData(s, repmat(Vout,1,numChannels))[Vin, timestamps] = startForeground(s);figureplot(t, Vin(:,1),'o',timestamps,Vout,'x')xlim([0 0.005])xlabel('Time (s)')ylabel('Voltage (V)')

Summary: Misconfigured bus ports with bus objects might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: When a bus object defines an element in a bus port, the bus object determines the data type of all elements of the bus. Changing the data type of the elements of the bus from the block dialog is not supported. If you add an element to the bus that does not exist in the bus object and set its data type to a bus object, MATLAB might crash.

Summary: JavaScript TypeError when saving an App Designer app
Description: If you attempt to save an app without ever visiting the App Designer Code View, MATLAB throws the following error instead of saving the app: TypeError: Cannot read property 'length' of undefined

Summary: LTE HDL SIB1?Recovery example failing to decode PDSCH due to incorrect interpretation of transport block length
Description: In the LTE HDL SIB1 Recovery example, the DCI message parsing section (ltehdlDCIResourceAllocation) sometimes wrongly interprets the transport block length. This error results in failure to decode the PDSCH.

Summary: Saving model to previous version breaks XCP block library links
Description: When you export a model containing XCP blocks to an earlier version with the option File >? Export Model to > Previous Version  then open the exported model in the older release, the blocks appear as broken library links.

Summary: Incorrect coverage for first assignment statement in Stateflow state when "on" state action is present
Description: When analyzing coverage for a Stateflow state in a C Chart, the Condition and MCDC results for the first logical assignment statement in the state might be incorrect if this state also contains an "on..." state action. For example, consider a state with entry  and on  actions. State1  en:?  ? a = w && x;  ? b = y && z;  on every(1, tick):  ? c = x || y;   MCDC and Condition coverage results for the logical expression (w && x)  found in the first assignment statement might be incorrect.? Note that this issue only affects coverage for this first assignment in the state.

Summary: Maximum path length exceeded during parallel build of referenced models on Windows
Description: The Windows operating system imposes a limit on the maximum length of a file path. Parallel building of referenced models might generate file paths that exceed the limit. For example, if: Referenced models have long names. The Windows operating system's temporary folder has a long file path.
Workaround: On Windows, the TMP and TEMP environment variables specify the location of the temporary folder. By default, the environment variables specify a subfolder in the Users folder. To reduce the length of the file path, specify a location that is closer to the root of the drive. For example, C:\Temp.

Summary: MEX command not work outside of MATLAB on Windows
Description: mex  command does not work outside of MATLAB on Windows in R2018b.
Workaround: From the system operating prompt, type:<matlabroot>\bin\win64\mexwhere?<matlabroot> is the value returned by the matlabroot command.

Summary: EtherCAT Sync SSC/SoE Download block handles the IDN value incorrectly
Description: The EtherCAT Sync SSC/SoE Download block handles the IDN (identification number) value incorrectly, passing the wrong value to the S-function for the IDN. This issue results in incorrect EtherCAT device configuration. Depending on the EtherCAT device, this can result in incorrect answers or an error message when running the model on the target.?
Workaround: Adapt your model to use the EtherCAT Async SSC/SoE Download block rather than the EtherCAT Sync SSC/SoE Download block.

Summary: Coverage not recorded for assignments under "on" state actions in C Charts
Description: When analyzing coverage for Stateflow states in C Charts, coverage is recorded for logical expressions in assignment statements only when these statements are found under the entry , during , or exit  state actions.? Assignment statements under "on " state actions (such as "on event_name " or "on every(1, tick) ") are not analyzed for coverage.
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Design Verifier, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Check

Summary: Inference on vector regression networks errors for different sized inputs
Description: Inference on vector regression networks errors when the following conditions hold true-?? 1. Input size during inference is different than the one used for training. 2. The input is passed to the network through a transform or combine datastore.

Summary: If the axle torque input is zero, the Longitudinal Wheel and Combined Slip Wheel 2DOF blocks do not use the initial angular velocity block parameters
Description: If the axle torque input port AxlTrq ?is zero, the Longitudinal Wheel and Combined Slip Wheel 2DOF blocks do not use the initial angular velocity block parameters. Specifically, theCombined Split Wheel 2DOF block does not use the Initial rotational velocity, omegao  parameter Longitudinal Wheel block does not use the?Wheel initial angular velocity, omegao ?parameter
Workaround: Do not provide the axle torque?AxlTrq? input port a signal that is exactly zero. Instead, you can use a signal that is near zero, for example 2*eps N*m, where eps is a very small number approaching zero. To achieve this, consider either approach:??Use a Sum block to add the axle torque signal to a Constant block with a value set to 2*eps?Use an IC block to set the initial condition of the axle torque?AxlTrq?input signal to??2*eps

Summary: MATLAB displays stale data in uitable when size of data changes
Description: If a?uitable ?is created with a large data set, and then the data set size is reduced and then increased again, MATLAB?displays stale fields of the data. For example MATLAB displays stale data when this code is run: u = uitable(uifigure);drawnow;pause(1);u.Data = array2table(ones(10));drawnow;pause(1);u.Data = array2table(randn(2));drawnow;pause(1);u.Data = array2table(zeros(10));
Workaround: After resizing the data in the uitable, assign random data of the same size to it before assigning it the actual data.For example:?u = uitable(uifigure);drawnow;pause(1);u.Data = array2table(ones(10));drawnow;pause(1);u.Data = array2table(randn(2));drawnow;pause(1);u.Data = array2table(randn(10));drawnow;pause(1);u.Data = array2table(zeros(10));

Summary: Code inspection fails to verify the code for the elements of a bus object that originate from Simulink.Signal object outside of Stateflow Chart
Description: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify the generated code from a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Stateflow chart. The chart contains a non-inlined graphical function. The function selects elements from a bus object that is specified on a Simulink.Signal  object outside of the chart.?  The code that Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify is similar to the following: BusObject.<BusElement> = 5;

Summary: Polyspace Access times out when the database is too large
Description: When you try you to access the Polyspace Access database, you see this error message:   The server response timed out. This is caused by a performance issue when there are too many results stored in the database.
Workaround: Clean up your database by regularly deleting old and obsolete results.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: While-loop used to increment or decrement a single variable omitted from the generated code
Description: Incorrect results are possible for a MATLAB function containing a while-loop with the following properties: The expression for the while-loop condition is a simple relational comparison between a variable and a constant (for example, k < 5 or k >= 10). In the following text, this variable is referred to as the loop induction variable. The body of the loop contains a statement in which the loop induction variable is incremented or decremented by a constant (for example, k = k + 1 or k = k - 5). The body of the while loop contains no other statements. The loop induction variable is assigned to a persistent variable after the while-loop.  The code generator might incorrectly omit code for the while-loop. For an example see the file issue_example_1950912.m in the attached issue_1950912.zip:  persistent P6;?  [SNIP]?  P31 = 1;  while P31<=6     P31 = P31 + 1;  end  P6( 1, 1 ) = P31( 1, 1 ); In the generated code? for issue_example_1950912, there is no loop corresponding to the while-loop above. This can be observed by comparing the numeric result of the generated Mex file against MATLAB:?  >> cfg = coder.config('mex');>> cfg.IntegrityChecks = 0;>> cfg.ResponsivenessChecks = 0;>> codegen -config cfg issue_example_1950912>> [~,~,~,ml_out] = issue_example_1950912();>> [~,~,~,cg_out] = issue_example_1950912_mex();>> disp(ml_out - cg_out)   56
Workaround: Replace the while-loop with an assignment to the final value of the loop induction variable. For an example see issue_workaround_1950912.m:  persistent P6;?  [SNIP]?  P31 = 7;  P6( 1, 1 ) = P31( 1, 1 );>> cfg = coder.config('mex');>> cfg.IntegrityChecks = 0;>> cfg.ResponsivenessChecks = 0;>> codegen -config cfg issue_workaround_1950912>> [~,~,~,ml_out] = issue_workaround_1950912();>> [~,~,~,cg_out] = issue_workaround_1950912_mex();>> disp(ml_out - cg_out)?   0

Summary: Identify Modeling Clones tool might not detect clones when Maximum number of different parameters parameter is set to a value greater than 0
Description: The Identify Modeling Clones tool might not detect clones when the Maximum number of different parameters  parameter is set to a value greater than 0 for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a signal that branches and connects to blocks that are of the same type.? One of these blocks is commutative, and the other block is noncommutative.  For example, a signal might branch and connect to two Product blocks. One Product block is noncommutative because the Number of Inputs  block parameter is set to */ . The other Product block is commutative because the Number of Inputs   block parameter is set to ** .

Summary: Whitespace converted to '+' symbol when searching the MATLAB documentation
Description: When searching the MATLAB documentation either in the Help browser or by using the?docsearch ?command,? any whitespace character in the search term is converted to a '+' symbol.  For example, the command?docsearch plot tools ?returns search results for "plot+tools" instead of "plot tools". This is an issue because the search term "plot+tools" only returns results where the two words are exactly sequential while the search term "plot tools" returns results that contain the two words anywhere in the document.

Summary: Variable Editor cannot be launched from the Model Explorer Dialog pane for custom attribute properties of data objects
Description: In the Model Explorer Dialog pane, when you select?Open Variable Editor  from the menu near the value for a custom attribute property of a data object, the Variable Editor does not launch.
Workaround: Launch the dialog from the custom attribute property in the Model Explorer?Contents?pane.?

Summary: Code inspection fails or MATLAB crashes when local data is defined but not used inside a non-inlined graphical function in Stateflow chart
Description: MATLAB might crash or code inspection might fail for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a non-inlined graphical function inside a Stateflow chart. The chart contains locally-scoped data that is defined inside the non-inlined graphical function but is not used. The unused local data is optimized out of the generated code.??  Simulink Code Inspector does not ignore the local data causing MATLAB to crash during code inspection or for Simulink Code Inspector to fail.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during code generation from a model with large (>2000) partitions
Description: Creating more than about 2000 distinct rates or partitions in a model might cause MATLAB to crash during code generation.

Summary: Error when opening App Designer app saved over a later release app
Description: If you save an app, created in R2017b or earlier, over an existing app saved in App Designer release R2018a or later, the saved app file is corrupted. The error that displays when attempting to open the app in releases R2018a, R2018b, and R2019a is?Reference to non-existent field 'code' . The saved app opens without error in releases R2017b and earlier.
Workaround: When saving an app in App Designer in releases older than R2017b, either:* Do not choose an existing file saved in R2018a, R2018b, or R2019a* Save the app to a new file, not an existing one.?

Summary: Tooltips that indicate an error or warning state are missing icons
Description: Tooltips that indicate an error or warning state in MATLAB, Simulink, or toolbox apps are missing the error (!) or information (i) icons.

Summary: Simulink can crash during upgrade of models using the Upgrade Advisor
Description: When upgrading models to a new release of Simulink using the Upgrade Advisor, Simulink may crash.? This occurs after there has been an error saving one of the models.?
Workaround: Change settings to ensure that no errors will occur when saving the models.? Two such settings are "Block diagram contains disabled library links" and "Block diagram contains parameterized library links" in the "Diagnostics" section of the Configuration Parameters window.? Alternatively:? set_param(modelname,'SaveWithDisabledLinksMsg','warning')? set_param(modelname,'SaveWithParameterizedLinksMsg','warning')?

Summary: Line-level traceability can lead to exceedingly long HDL code generation time
Description: On the HDL Code Generation > Report  pane, you can specify the TraceabilityStyle . By default, the?TraceabilityStyle ?is line-level . When you use this option and enable Generate traceability report , in some cases, the report generation can take a significantly long time.
Workaround: Specify comment-based as the?TraceabilityStyle and then generate the traceability report.

Summary: Allowed temperature ranges for thermocouples are narrower than those supported by National Instruments
Description: Data Acquisition Toolbox supports temperature ranges for thermocouple channels that are narrower than the ranges supported in NI-MAX.  For instance, using NI 9214 or NI USB-TC01, the range supported by the toolbox for a type J thermocouple is 0 to 750 degrees Celsius, while NI-MAX supports a range of -200 to 1200 degrees Celsius. The following code indicates the limited toolbox range: s=daq.createSession('ni');addAnalogInputChannel(s,'Dev1',0,'Thermocouple');s.Channels(1).ThermocoupleType = 'J';s.Channels(1).Rangeans =0 to +750 Celsius

Summary: C Caller Block displays the same error message when issue with custom code is fixed
Description: The C Caller Block displays an error if a header file is missing or the file name is wrong. When you fix the issue and rerun the model,? the same error message is displayed.
Workaround: Rename the header file, insert an empty line in the source code C file, and rerun the model.

Summary: Error when exporting a project using relative path
Description: If you export a project using export(project)  and use a relative path, the export succeeds, but you see an error similar to the following: Error using matlab.project.Project/export?  Could not create the file "timestableproj.mlproj". foundation::storage::vfs::InvalidIRIException

Summary: Slower MCDC analysis of Test Harness model compared to regular model
Description: Simulation of a Simulink test harness takes a longer time to complete when model coverage analysis is enabled and the structural coverage level is set to?Modified Condition Decision Coverage (MCDC) .  By comparison, MCDC analysis of an equivalent model that is not configured as a test harness takes less time to complete.??
Workaround: To work around the issue, do one of the following:Disable the MCDC metric if it is not necessary for your coverage analysis. Changing the structural coverage level to Condition Decision should improve performance.? This setting can be adjusted via the Coverage pane of the Configuration Parameters or by manually setting the value of the corresponding parameter?CovMetricStructuralLevel.If MCDC analysis is necessary, then avoid running this analysis on test harnesses. Instead, detach and export the test harness, then perform the coverage simulation on the resulting model. To export an individual test harness, from?the test harness menu, select?Analysis?>?Test Harness?>?Detach and Export Harness, as described in the documentation page?Manage Test Harnesses.
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Check, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Design Verifier

Summary: Simulating harness model containing a library block with coverage filter leads to error
Description: Simulating a harness model containing a library block with a coverage filter leads to an error:An error occurred during simulation of Model block '...'. Model 'simulink' is not loaded. A fatal error occurred when accessing the coverage tool: cvi.TopModelCov.modelStart?

Summary: Assertion error when generating code for model : 'In computeAndSetType: Type checker is unable to infer the type of Node 'hcat'. Please provide the type explicitly.'?
Description: Simulink might error unexpectedly when generating code for a model with the message: CGIR assertion 'In computeAndSetType: Type checker is unable to infer the type of Node 'hcat'. Please provide the type explicitly.'?  This error might occur when the model uses one or more Simulink.AliasType? objects ?and one or more of the following conditions is true: The model contains concatenation of a?Simulink.AliasType ?signal with a signal that has the same data type as the BaseType  of the Simulink.AliasType .  The model contains concatenation of different Simulink.AliasType ?signals that have the same specified BaseType . The model contains concatenation of a Simulink.AliasType ?signal with a signal driven by a Constant block.  For examples of this issue see the models within issue_1948052.zip: issue_model_1948052_signal_mismatch and issue_model_1948052_parameter_mismatch. In issue_model_1948052_signal_mismatch there are Mux blocks that concatenate a signal of?single_alias ?type and?single ?type. In issue_model_1948052_parameter_mismatch there is a Mux block driven by a signal of single_alias  type and a Constant block .
Workaround: Adjust the model so that signals of the exact same data type drive the inports of blocks that perform concatenation (for example, Mux and Vector Concatenate). This may involve:Replacing the use of a Simulink.AliasType with its specified BaseType.?Replacing the uses of two or more?Simulink.AliasTypes of the same BaseType with a single?Simulink.AliasType.Inserting a Data Type Conversion block to unify the types of signals feeding a block performing concatenation.?A workaround for issue_model_1948052_signal_mismatch can be seen in the attached model issue_workaround_1948052_signal_mismatch. All inputs in the model have been adjusted to have a single_alias type. This unified all signals driving mux blocks to have the type single_alias.If a constant block is driving a concatenating block like issue_model_1948052_parameter_misatch, use a Simulink.Parameter to specify the value of the Constant block driving the concatenating block with properties:A?DataType?matching the Simulink.AliasType driving the other inports of the concatenating blockA storage?class other than AutoA workaround for issue_model_1948052_parameter_mismatch can be see in the attached model issue_workaround_1948052_parameter_mismatch. The value of the Constant block is now specified as single_alias_ONE which resolves to a Simulink.Parameter with DataType single_alias and a Const storage class specification.

Summary: Check data types for blocks with index signals incorrectly flags an index that contains a math operation
Description: The Model Advisor check Check data types for blocks with index signals ?(Check ID: mathworks.hism.hisl_0022 ) might incorrectly flag blocks that use arithmetic operators (+/-)? to calculate index variables.

Summary: Labeling data not saved when exiting Signal Analyzer while in?Signal Labeler
Description: If, while you are using?Signal Labeler, you close the Signal Analyzer app by using the X  or Close button? in the title bar, a dialog box prompts you about saving the app data. If you click Yes , only the signal data is saved. No labeling data is saved.

Summary: Label data might be deleted if extracted signal is deleted
Description: In Signal Analyzer, if you extract a signal from a plotted member of a labeledSignalSet ?object and then delete the signal, the labeling information is lost. This bug affects the labeledSignalSet ?only in the scope of Signal Analyzer. Any label data from the labeledSignalSet  in the MATLAB workspace remains intact.

Summary: Links for plotting scripts in the Model a Road Profile with Varying Elevation and Friction example do not work
Description: The documentation for Model a Road Profile with Varying Elevation and Friction example shows how to convert the model in the Four-Wheel Drive Testbed example into the model in the Four-Wheel Drive Testbed with Road Profile example by adding a Road Profile block. The model in the Four-Wheel Drive Testbed with Road Profile compiles and runs without issue, but the links in the model annotation?cannot access code that automatically plots speed and compares differentials.?

Summary: Unsupported input type error when creating link between reference objects
Description: Creating a link between objects of type slreq.Reference  by using the slreq.createLink()  function may throw an?error. The error occurs when running the following command with the first argument being an object of type slreq.Reference  imported from an external tool via ReqIF? file: >> slreq.createLink(ref1, ref2)?  Error using slreq.utils.resolveSrc (line 127) slreq.utils.resolveSrc() does not support input of type slreq.Reference

Summary: Inconsistent outputfrom Inport blocks that load string data in fast restart simulations?
Description: When you run multiple simulations in fast restart of a model that loads string data, the Inport block output for the first simulation can differ from the output in subsequent simulations. If the simulation stop time exceeds the time corresponding to the last value in the external input data, the first simulation uses ground values and subsequent simulations hold the last external input value.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when calling segmentGroundFromLidarData
Description: MATLAB might crash when you call the?segmentGroundFromLidarData ?function.

Summary: Phased Array System Toolbox app?Radar Waveform Analyzer errors on?launching
Description: MATLAB might throw an error and become unresponsive when you try to launch the Radar Waveform Analyzer app if you do not have certain products installed in addition to Phased Array System Toolbox. Undefined variable "matlabshared" or class "matlabshared.application.layout.GridBagLayout".?
Workaround: You can work around this issue by installing the Antenna Toolbox, RF Toolbox, or Communication Toolbox in addition to the Phased Array System Toolbox.

Summary: Code inspection produces an error when a model has the same name as one of its blocks
Description: A model fails code inspection if a model has the same name as one of its blocks and the block with the same name as the model connects to another block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing multiple?Reusable custom storage classes
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: A pair of root-level inport and outport signals contain the same Reusable  custom storage class specification. There are two blocks in between the pair of root-level inport and outport blocks. There is another Reusable  custom storage class specified between the blocks and it drives a separate root-level outport.

Summary: Compilation might fail with error message: segmentation fault occurred in "ps_cxx_fe"
Description: If?a source code file contains some constant initialization of structurevariables inside a C++ class declaration, Polyspace Bug Finder might fail to compile this file.? For example, if you have this class declaration in a file?my_file.cpp : Class myClass {private:struct myStruct { int field1; bool field2, ... };const myStruct var = {.field1 = 1; .field2 = false; ...}; }; You see this error message during the compilation phase of my_file.cpp :?     Verifying my_file.cpp (X/Y)      Error: A segmentation fault occurred in "ps_cxx_fe.exe".    my_file.cpp: warning: Failed compilation.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Failure to update previously imported contents from a .reqif file with modified name
Description: If you imported requirements from A.reqif ? and later you are given an updated version of A.reqif ,? Simulink Requirements allows you to update the previously imported data. However, if your updated .reqif  file has a new name, and you point the update procedure to this newer file, the update process fails.? You see an error: XXX.mat file not found .?
Workaround: When exporting updated requirements from external tools, use the same name for the .reqif file as the previously imported .reqif file. Alterntively, rename the updated .reqif to match the original file name.??For example, if the original export?was done to SysReqs.reqif?file, and the next export created SysReqs(1).reqif file, and you need to update the requirements imported?originally imported from SysReqs.reqif file,? then create a local copy of SysReqs(1).reqif and rename to?SysReqs.reqif, then perform the update from this file.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: VideoReader reports incorrect video frame dimensions?when reading some H.264 encoded MP4 files on macOS
Description: On macOS, when using?VideoReader? to read certain types of H.264 encoded MP4 files, MATLAB returns incorrect frame dimensions.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: ?Variable Integer Delay block outputs wrong answer when Upper Limit overflows data type of Delay length input
Description: The Variable Integer Delay block may generate incorrect code when both of these conditions are true: The Delay length  source is Input?port  The delay?length Upper Limit  value?does not fit in the range of the?input delay length data type (causing an?overflow)  For example, if the?Delay length ?input type is?'int16' ?and the delay length?UpperLimit? parameter is set?to?65535 ,?the type of?'int16' ?cannot represent?UpperLimit ?because?cast(65535, 'int16') ?returns?32767? in MATLAB.? The incorrect code causes the input signal?to be delayed by some value other than the specified delay length. This causes?a mismatch in behavior between normal mode simulation and all code generation targets (accelerator mode, rapid accelerator mode, standalone code generation, and so forth).
Workaround: To convert the Delay length input to a type that will fit the value of Upper Limit, connect a Data Type Conversion block to input d of?the Variable Integer Delay block.

Summary: Upgrading a project leaves Simulink models with disabled library links
Description: Running the Project Upgrade can incorrectly disable library links in Simulink models if they do not fail any Simulink Upgrade Advisor checks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation and code?generation results for Shift Arithmetic block when sum of Bits to shift and Binary points to shift exceeds 32-bit integer maximum value
Description: Incorrect simulation and code?generation results occur for Shift Arithmetic block under these conditions: Source of Bits to shift  parameter is set to Input port  Data type of the Bits to shift  input port (s) signal is double  Total shift due to the addition/subtraction of the? Bits to shift ?input port signal value and Binary points to shift  parameter value exceeds the 32-bit integer maximum value

Summary: Callback order for App Designer app reverts back to original order when MATLAB closes
Description: You can reorder callbacks in App Designer by dragging and dropping them in the Code Browser Callbacks tab. However, ?after you close and restart MATLAB, ?the callbacks revert back to their original order.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing Reusable?custom storage classes
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: A root-level inport signal to a block has a?Reusable  custom storage class. A non root-level outport signal from that block has the same?Reusable ?custom storage class. A second block that connects to this non root-level outport signal and has a different sample time from the first block. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Treat each discrete rate as a separate task ?parameter is selected.
Workaround: Remove the Reusable custom storage class from the non root-level inport or outport signal.

Summary: Models containing Ideal Airspeed Correction block do not pass Underspecified data types configuration parameter diagnostic check
Description: Models containing the Ideal Airspeed Correction block do not pass the Underspecified data types  (UnderSpecifiedDataTypeMsg ) configuration parameter diagnostic check.

Summary: Some?Simulink Coverage?model coverage reports in the IEC Certification Kit include incorrect coverage data
Description: The IEC Certification Kit provides baseline Simulink Coverage model coverage reports to use for comparison with coverage reports that are generated in a users' environment. The following baseline model coverage reports do not reflect the Simulink Coverage product changes and will not match the user-generated reports: mSFSatOnOverflow_CoverageReport.html  hexternalmfilemcdc_cov.html  mEMLMultipleRunMixed_cov.html  mModelRefSubsys_cov.html  mmodelblock_cov.html mmodelblock_cov.html

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using Dataflow subsystems in referenced models may cause build failure
Description: In a Simulink model hierarchy, when both the top-level and referenced model contain Dataflow subsystems and the model names are longer than 9 characters, building the top-level model fails.
Workaround: Change model names to be less than 9 characters in length.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing a?Model?block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model references another model. The referenced model's output port connects to a root-level?Outport?block. The signal connecting to the root-level Outport block contains a storage class. A non-zero constant signal drives the referenced model output port that connects to the root-level Outport block.
Workaround: Remove the storage class specification on the Model?block's output port and use function prototype control to achieve the customization of root-level Outport . For more information, see?Optimize Buffering for Arguments.

Summary: Source Code pane shows incorrect results or Java error message
Description: If you open more than one source code view, or if several users view the same results, the displayed source code might be corrupted. The Source Code pane shows either a Java NullPointerException ?error message or incorrect results, such as incorrect location of checks or the wrong source code.

Summary: Using parsim to simulate a model inside a referenced project closes the parent project
Description: When simulating a model inside a referenced project using parsim, the parent project will be closed if it's open. The referenced project with the model will then be loaded before simulating the model.

Summary: Power spectrum computation using the Welch method in Diagnostic Feature Designer may return wrong results
Description: Any changes made to the power spectrum computation options, such as the choice of window and overlap, are ignored when computing using the Welch method.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Sincos block can generate incorrect generated model.
Description: When a Sincos block with Approximation method?as CORDIC?and?HDL?block property "UsePipelinedKernel"?turned off, the generated model is not correct.

Summary: Dot indexing error when importing requirements from DOOR module without mapping
Description: The Map Attributes dialog box is a part of Import configuration that allow you to map DOORS user attributes to custom attributes in Simulink Requirements, or to skip?certain attributes you do not want to import. If you do not need to make any adjustments and proceed to Import step without opening the Map Attributes dialog, or if you open the dialog and close it without clicking OK , an error pops up after requirements?import. All requirements are imported correctly, but the popup displays?Unable to import , and the imported Requirements Set remains unsaved.
Workaround: It is safe to close the error popup, but you must save the imported requirements. Because of the error,?the destination Requirement Set remains unsaved until you manually save it.

Summary: HDL code generation from windowing functions fails
Description: MATLAB throws an error when you try to generate HDL code from MATLAB functions containing windowing functions such as parzenwin , tukeywin , triang , gausswin ,? and nuttallwin.

Summary: Some linear algebra operations with sparse, singular inputs?produce memory leaks in the generated code
Description: With sparse, singular inputs, calling the following functions results in generated code which contains memory leaks: mldivide? (or the '\ ' operator) if the first input is a sparse, singular matrix inv

Summary: Simulation results may change when you enable Simulink Coverage for a model containing an Absolute Value block
Description: The simulation result for a Simulink model set to normal mode may change when you enable Simulink Coverage and the following conditions are met: The model contains an Absolute Value block? with "Enable zero-crossing detection" set to true . The input to the Absolute Value block crosses zero. The Absolute Value block drives a block with continuous states, such as the Integrator block. You use a variable-step continuous solver, such as ode45 .  The resultant run may have a small difference in the numerical result computed for blocks downstream of the absolute value block.
Workaround: Disable Simulink Coverage to produce the correct simulation results.
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Check, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Design Verifier

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The generated code does not call the overloaded end method of a class
