Two element wise arithmetic operations use the same single or double matrix variable. The matrix variable definition occurs in between the two?element wise arithmetic operations.?  When the above conditions are met, the generated code might incorrectly use stale values of a variable instead of using the updated value due to the definition.?? For the attached example, the code generator?generates the following incorrect code in which second call to _mm_mul_pd  uses stale input values stored in r0  :? void vectorOperations(double IA1[14]) {  ? ? int idx;  ? ? __m128d r0;   ? ? __m128d r1;  ? ? ? for (idx = 0; idx <= 12; idx += 2) {  ? ? ? ? ? r0 = _mm_loadu_pd(*(double (*)[2])&IA1[idx]);  ? ? ? ? ? r1 = _mm_mul_pd(r0, r0);  ? ? ? ? ? _mm_storeu_pd(&IA1[idx], r1);  ? ? ? ? ? r1 = _mm_mul_pd(r0, r0);  ? ? ? ? ? _mm_storeu_pd(&IA1[idx], r1);  ? ? ? ?}  }   After?following the steps?in the?workaround section, the code?generator generates the following code where updated IA1  is loaded into r0 , r1  before being used?in second call to _mm_mul_pd:   void vectorOperations(double IA1[14]) {???? int idx;???? __m128d r0;???? __m128d r1;???? __m128d r2;?????for (idx = 0; idx <= 12; idx += 2) {????????r0 = _mm_loadu_pd(*(double (*)[2])&IA1[idx]);??????? r1 = _mm_loadu_pd(*(double (*)[2])&IA1[idx]);??????? r2 = _mm_mul_pd(r0, r1);??????? _mm_storeu_pd(&IA1[idx], r2);??????? r0 = _mm_loadu_pd(*(double (*)[2])&IA1[idx]);?????? ?r1 = _mm_loadu_pd(*(double (*)[2])&IA1[idx]);??????? r2 = _mm_mul_pd(r0, r1);??????? _mm_storeu_pd(&IA1[idx], r2);??? }}       ?
Workaround: Unzip the attached P-file,?workaroundForBugReport1907910.p.Insert the P-file on the MATLAB file path.For every new MATLAB session, in the Command Window, enter the following command:run workaroundForBugReport1907910.p?

Summary: Referenced models are re-compiled unnecessarily when adding parameters to a parameter set in Test Manager
Description: Referenced models in a top model may be repeatedly compiled each time a parameter is added to a parameter set in a Test Manager test case.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model?with a reset state inside For iterator or While Iterator Subsystem block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a For Iterator or While Iterator Subsystem block. Inside the For Iterator or While Iterator Subsystem block, there is at least one Unit Delay block. The input or output port of the Unit Delay block drives the For Iterator or While Iterator?Subsystem's output port. The parameter?States when starting  ?of the For Iterator or While Iterator block inside the For Iterator or While Iterator subsystem is set to reset.  The For Iterator or While Iterator subsystem is conditional executed. For example, the subsystem is inside a Triggered subsystem, or its loop bound is variant and the loop doesn't implement until the passage of a few simulation time steps.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generating code for a model hierarchy that contains referenced models in Normal mode with zero-crossing signals may take a long time to generate code and detect incorrect zero-crossings in RSim target
Description: When generating code from a model containing referenced models in normal-mode which have zero-crossing signals at any level of the hierarchy, the intermediate model .rtw  file may be excessively large in size and cause a slow-down in the code generation process. In addition, if the code is generated for the RSim target, the solver may detect zero-crossings in incorrect directions, or may not detect the zero-crossings that should be detected.??
Workaround: Set the Simulation Mode parameter of any Model blocks to Accelerator if they are referencing directly or indirectly a model that contains zero-crossing signals.

Summary: Graphic EQ produces error with certain gain settings
Description: The Graphic EQ block can generate an error when using very low gains (<1e-6) or when its? EQ Order  parameter is set to the default value of 2.

Summary: quat2angle, dcm2angle, and rod2angle functions might return complex results
Description: Some singularity inputs to quat2angle , dcm2angle , and rod2angle  functions might return complex numbers with?small imaginary parts.

Summary: spi function returns an error on Windows
Description: When trying to create an spi  object with 'aardvark' or 'ni845x' adaptors on Windows, MATLAB returns the following error: >> s = spi('aardvark', 0, 0)   Error using spi (line 66)?  Undefined function or variable 'adaptor'.?

Summary: Long FMU parameter names may cause Simulink failure
Description: Simulink fails to load an FMU with more than 100 parameters if the first 60 or more characters of the parameter names are identical.
Workaround: Users must change parameter names to avoid the limitation.

Summary: Deploying an application using the databaseDatastore function on an MPS instance fails with an error
Description: When using an MPS instance, the?databaseDatastore ? function fails with the following error:? [err] [worker:72] Error using databaseDatastore (line 71) [err] [worker:72] SQLite file does not exist.
Workaround: On an MPS instance, access a MATLAB installation file by using this command instead:?toolboxdir('__product__')

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with Reusable custom storage class and Initialize, Reset, or Terminate Function block
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: A root Inport block and a root Outport block connects to an Initialize Function block, a Reset Function block, or Terminate Function block. The same Reusable custom storage class is specified at both root Inport and root Outport blocks.
Workaround: Remove the Reusable custom storage class from the root Inport block and root Outport block.

Summary: "Access denied" error during model protection
Description: When you create or modify a protected model on Windows machines, you might receive an "Access Denied" error.?

Summary: Running large numbers of tests in?parallel mode using the Test Manager causes warnings
Description: When you run test cases in parallel mode using the Test Manager and Parallel Computing Toolbox, the messages that are passed between host and workers can cause conflicts if the model that is being simulated on a worker is being closed or compiled on the host.  Test Manager automates the scheduling and simulations on the workers, therefore the warnings may appear sporadically for a very large number of simulations when the above condition is met.
Workaround: Start a?worker pool manually?using parpool?and execute the following command.Simulink.sdi.enablePCTSupport('manual');?Now, execute the?tests from Test Manager in?parallel mode.?

Summary: Exporting more than 999 runs or results to an MLDATX file from Simulation Data Inspector or Test Manager fails without warning
Description: When you save a session in the Simulation Data Inspector or export results from the Test Manager in Simulink Test, the operation fails without warning if the Simulation Data Inspector session contains more than 999 runs or the Test Manager session contains more than 999 results.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calls to coder.ceval with GPU arguments can produce CPU arguments in the generated code
Description: For a coder.ceval  call, the generated code may incorrectly pass CPU pointer for a GPU argument if the argument is an unbounded variable. For example, consider a dummy function: function y = mysamplefunc(x)   ? ?y = coder.nullcopy(0);  ? ?coder.ceval('a_c_function', coder.rref(x,'gpu'), coder.wref(y));  end   Generating code with an unbounded input as shown below will result in incorrect code.? codegen -config coder.gpuConfig -args {coder.typeof(0, [1, inf])} mysamplefunc?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Absolute-time temporal logic operators execute incorrectly in Stateflow charts resumed from a saved simulation state
Description: When you save and restore the simulation state for your model, an incorrect simulation time might be saved when all of the following conditions are true: Your model contains a Stateflow chart that uses absolute-time temporal logic operators in state and transition actions. You use quotes with the time unit keyword in the temporal logic operator, for example:?after(1000,'sec') .  If the simulation time is not saved correctly, then temporal logic operators may return an incorrect value. It is possible for transitions guarded by temporal logic operators to evaluate as true at unexpected times.
Workaround: Remove any quotes surrounding the time unit keyword?sec, msec, and?usec, for example:?after(1000,sec)?

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier fails during Compatibility Check
Description: Simulink Design Verifier fails during compatibility check with the assertion:?(dtTable != nullptr)&& (busTypeId > 0)  under all these conditions: The model contains a chart with a MATLAB Function     defined inside it.  An input/output parameter of the MATLAB Function?inside a chart?is a     Bus with its Type  set to?Inherit: From definition in chart.
Workaround: If any input/output parameter of a MATLAB Function defined inside a chart is a Bus, then explicitly set its Type using the Bus Object.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Erroneous memory reuse of inner MATLAB system object in context of cell arrays
Description: When using MATLAB system objects in the context nesting them via a cell-array, the system objects state may be shared for two cell-array elements. This issue can occur when the following conditions are met: Define your own custom MATLAB system objects (i.e. class inherits from matlab.system) Define a cell-array property with private access that can contain other MATLAB system objects An outermost MATLAB system object that contains a cell-array property with private access that can contain other MATLAB system objects  See the attached BugReport1902005Details.zip  for examples that demonstrate this issue. ? ? ? ? ? ?? ??
Workaround: Avoid using cell-arrays with MATLAB system objects and instead use structures. For an example, see the A_withWorkaround.m file in?BugReport1902005Details.zip.

Summary: sgtitle?does not overwrite an existing subplot grid title
Description: sgtitle   adds a new subplot grid title on top of an existing title instead of overwriting the existing title.
Workaround: To avoid multiple subplot grid titles, delete existing titles before adding a new one.t = sgtitle('My Title');delete(t)Alternatively, instead of creating a new title, modify the existing title using the String property.t = sgtitle('My Title');t.String = 'My Different Title';

Summary: MATLAB crashes when closing a model after an undo operation
Description: MATLAB might crash when you perform these steps in the Simulink Editor: Right-click a Subsystem block. Select Subsystem & Model Reference >?Expand Subsystem . Undo the subsystem expansion. Close the window without saving the model.

Summary: MATLAB might not?find a new class definition after multiple path changes
Description: After a folder containing a class definition is removed from the path and there is still an existing instance of the class, and a new folder containing a class with the same name is added to the path, MATLAB might fail to find the new class definition. Instead, MATLAB continues to use the previous definition, which is no longer on the path. The following reproduction steps demonstrate this behavior:? % dir1?classdef EnumClass?????enumeration?????????a?????????b?????????c??????????end?end?% dir2?classdef EnumClass?????enumeration?????????a?????????b?????????c??????????????e?????????f?????end?end?>> addpath dir1?>> o = EnumClass.a;?>> enumeration(o);?Enumeration members for class 'EnumClass':?????a?????b?????c?>> rmpath dir1?>> addpath dir2?>> enumeration(o);?Enumeration members for class 'EnumClass':?????a?????b?????c?????e?????f?>> rmpath dir2?>> addpath dir1?>> enumeration(o); % Incorrectly uses the class definition in DIR2?Enumeration members for class 'EnumClass':?????a?????b?????c?????e?????f?
Workaround: Call clear all after making changes to the MATLAB path.?

Summary: Descriptor State-Space (DeSS) block uses full matrix format instead of sparse matrix format for its solver Jacobian
Description: Descriptor State-Space (DeSS) block should support sparse solver Jacobian but it didn't in R2018b. This may have negative impact on simulation performance for systems with large number of continuous states.?

Summary: The check "Check data types for blocks with index signals" (ID: mathworks.hism.hisl_0022) erroneously flags MATLAB Coder methods
Description: The check "Check data types for blocks with index signals" (ID: mathworks.hism.hisl_0022) looks at index operations inside a MATLAB Function block. The check erroneously flags MATLAB Coder directives like coder.inline('always'), coder.ceval, coder.wwref,etc. as a violation for indexing that is not using an integer or enumeration data type.

Summary: executecql and partitionRead functions?throw a Java exception for bigint, smallint, counter columns with more than 5000 rows
Description: The executecql  and partitionRead  functions?can throw a Java exception if both of the following conditions are true: The result contains a column defined as a bigint , smallint , or counter  on the Cassandra database. The result has more than 5000 rows.  The error resembles the following: Error using cassandra/readCassandraResultSet (line 1084)Java exception occurred:java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: 5000at com.mathworks.toolbox.database.utilities.longArrayList.add(longArrayList.java:35)at com.mathworks.toolbox.cassandra.CassandraResultSet.readData(CassandraResultSet.java:136)Error in cassandra/executecql (line 457)            resultsTable = this.readCassandraResultSet(results);Error in cassandra/partitionRead (line 533)                resultsTable = executecql(this,"SELECT * FROM " + keyspace + "." + tableName                +";","ConsistencyLevel","ONE");

Summary: Support Software Installer fails if the proper SSL certificates are not available
Description: The Support Software Installer uses SSL certificates to establish secure internet connections when downloading third-party content. When? the expected SSL certificates are invalid or are not available on your machine, the third-party content fails to download. MATLAB displays an error message that begins with:? Failed to download the third-party software?

Summary: Stateflow editor enters text edit mode when trying to move objects
Description: If you try to reposition an object on the Stateflow canvas by clicking and dragging on the object's label or text area, the Stateflow editor enters text edit mode instead of moving the object with the mouse cursor.
Workaround: Click and drag on another part of the object besides the label or text area.?

Summary: When RebuildOnOpen is true for an externally saved test harness, running a test case in the Test Manager may silently resave the harness.
Description: When RebuildOnOpen  is true  for an externally saved test harness, running a test case in the Test Manager may silently resave the harness.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, set the Test Harness RebuildOnOpen flag to false before running the test.

Summary: The trainFasterRCNNObjectDetector function produces a CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS error
Description: The?trainFasterRCNNObjectDetector  issues an error during training. The error, CUDA_ERROR_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS, occurs when regions of interest are near the image boundary.?

Summary: Variable-step simulation of a Simulink model containing one or more?FIR Decimation?blocks?may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when you run a model containing a FIR Decimation block, and all of the following conditions are true:  The model uses a variable-step solver The?Rate options ?parameter on the?block is set to?Allow multirate processing; ?the sample time of the output port of the block is?K ?times slower than that of the input port where K  is the integer value specified for the Decimation factor  parameter The greatest common denominator of all the discrete sample times in the model is a small number relative to the magnitude of the individual sample times.  To help determine if the greatest common denominator of all the discrete sample times in the model is a relatively small number, switch the model to a fixed-step solver and then press Run. If the condition is present, Simulink throws an error similar to:? The computed fixed step size (...) is 1000000.0 times smaller than all the discrete sample times in the model... You can fix this error either by switching to a variable step solver...

Summary: fetchOutputs errors on jobs submitted from a R2016a MATLAB client to a R2018b MATLAB Job Scheduler.
Description: A job submitted from a R2016a MATLAB client to a R2018b MATLAB Job Scheduler will finish successfully, but will throw an error when you try to fetch the results. The same error is thrown when you try to access the 'Error' or '"Warnings' properties of the job's tasks.  >> fetchOutputs(job);  Error using parallel.task.MJSTask/hGetProperty (line 196)  An error that is unknown to this version of MATLAB has occurred on the server. Original message:  unrecognized method hash: method not supported by remote object  Error in parallel.Task/get.Warnings (line 105)  ? ? ? ? ? ? diagnosticWarnings = task.hGetProperty('DiagnosticWarnings');  Error in parallel.Task/hWarnAboutWarnings (line 564)  ? ? ? ? ? ? warnings = task.Warnings;  Error in parallel.Job/fetchOutputs (line 986)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? thisTask.hWarnAboutWarnings('parallel:job:WarningsDuringTaskExecution', {thisTaskID});?

Summary: Error when selecting variables for model that has an active configuration reference
Description: In the following Simulink Design Optimization UIs, an error is thrown when you try to specify variables to tune for a model with an active configuration reference. Response Optimization tool, when creating a new Design Variables Set or Uncertain Variables Set Parameter Estimation tool, when clicking Select Parameters Sensitivity Analysis tool, when clicking Select Parameters  The error message is similar to: The variable 'configSet' cannot be resolved by the model 'mdl'. Make sure that the variable is defined in the model, base workspace, or data dictionary.

Summary: Index exceeds the number of array elements (0) error when editing or simulating Test Sequence block
Description: Opening a Test Sequence block or simulating a model containing a Test Sequence block can cause the error?Index exceeds the number of array elements (0) .

Summary: Errors from GPUs with compute capability 7.5 or above on first use of deep learning or linear algebra functions
Description: If you have a GPU with compute capability of 7.5 or above, including the latest Turing architecture cards, and you try deep learning or linear algebra functions, you see an error on first use. Parallel Computing Toolbox uses the cuBLAS Linear Algebra library to execute linear algebra operations on the GPU. Deep Learning Toolbox uses the cuDNN Deep Learning library to train and execute convolutional neural networks on the GPU. The versions of these libraries used by MATLAB error on GPUs with a compute capability of 7.5 or above, including the latest Turing architecture cards. If you try to train or predict with a deep learning network, or use linear algebra operations, using one of these GPUs, you see an error the first time cuDNN or cuBLAS is used in a MATLAB session. The second time you run the code in the same MATLAB session it will be successful. If you reset the GPU using reset  or gpuDevice  then the issue will occur again. For cuDNN, you see this problem with the function?trainNetwork  and methods of DAGNetwork  and SeriesNetwork  such as classify ,?predict? and?activations . The error usually has the ID?'nnet:cnngpu:CuDNNError '?and the message?'Unexpected error calling cuDNN: CUDNN_STATUS_EXECUTION_FAILED. '?? For cuBLAS, you see this problem with linear algebra functions like mtimes , mldivide  and inv . The error varies based on the method but always includes a message like 'CUBLAS failed with error status:?CUBLAS_STATUS_EXECUTION_FAILED '. You might also see an inaccurate warning. The warning ID is 'parallel:gpu:device:DeviceLibsNeedsRecompiling ' and?the message incorrectly reports this: 'The CUDA driver must recompile the GPU libraries because your device is more recent than the libraries. Recompiling can take several minutes.'?  To see if your GPU is affected, in MATLAB, enter:   gpuDevice Examine the field?ComputeCapability . If this value is?7.5 ?or higher, your card will error on the first execution of deep learning or linear algebra code.
Workaround: Run the following script after starting MATLAB.addlistener(parallel.gpu.GPUDeviceManager.instance(), 'DeviceSelected', @turingWorkaround);function turingWorkaround(~,~)try? ? gpuArray.eye(2)^2;catch MEendtry? ? nnet.internal.cnngpu.reluForward(1);catch MEendendPlace the code in your startup.m to run automatically. You still see the warning as it cannot be permanently disabled, but you can ignore the warning.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized outputs for Truth Tables using MATLAB Action Language
Description: If you create a Truth Table that has output data, but do not write to that output every time step, the value of the output might be undefined instead of retaining the previous value.
Workaround: Configure the output signal as a test point.

Summary: Dangling expression might be in generated code for a model with S-Function block
Description: Simulink Coder might generate a dangling expression while expression folding for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains an S-Function block.? The block has a TLC implementation that outputs an expression in TLC. The S-Function block uses signals with custom data types.  For example, consider the lines of code below:? y=a+b;   z=y+c;   The code generator?generates the dangling expression?a+b ?after expression folding:? a+b;?  z=a+b+c; ?

Summary: Model with a root bus inport may throw an error during code generation
Description: When you generate code for a model which has a root inport set to a non-virtual bus data type, you may see the below error in the MATLAB Command Window or Diagnostic viewer: Internal error while creating code interface description file: codeInfo.mat. Aborting code generation

Summary: Export-function model fails to update when two function-call root-level Inport blocks drive a common sample time independent Model block
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when it meets the following conditions: The model is an export-function model. The model contains a Function-Call Subsystem block that includes a Model block. The referenced model for the Model block has the configuration parameter Periodic sample time constraint  set to 'Ensure sample time independent' . The Function-Call Subsystem block is driven by two function-call root-level Inport blocks through a Mux block.  When updating the model, Simulink throws an error message similar to the one below.? Model block 'testMdl/Chart/a/Model' is driven by an asynchronous function call at inport 1. An asynchronous function call that directly or indirectly involves multiple asynchronous function calls is not allowed to drive a model block input port.

Summary: Parallel simulations might produce incorrect simulation results
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy in parallel might produce incorrect simulation results when all of the following conditions are true:The top model has the?Enable parallel model reference builds ?configuration parameter selected. The top model?Rebuild ?configuration parameter is set?to?Never . The top model?Never rebuild diagnostic? is set to?none . The Simulation cache folder contains only Simulink cache files. The model hierarchy contains a Model block set to simulate in accelerator mode. The referenced model contains another Model block set to simulate in accelerator mode. The model referenced by the second Model block was modified before the previous simulation. The previous simulation used a parallel pool of workers. The previous simulation rebuilt simulation targets according to the top model?Rebuild ?configuration parameter setting of?Always ,?If any changes detected , or?If any changes in known dependencies detected .

Summary: Error when using Multiply-Accumulate block? in a model
Description: If you add the Multiply-Accumulate block to a model or if you double click on this block when inside a model, you get this error message: Error evaluating 'MaskDialog' callback of Multiply-Accumulate block(mask( '<modelname>/Multiply-Accumulate'. Callback string is 'hdlblkmask_streaming_number_of_samples_cb(gcb);'   Undefined function 'hdlblkmask_streaming_number_of_samples_cb' for input arguments of type 'char'.?

Summary: Changes made to variables in the model workspace of accelerated model references using SimulationInput object are silently ignored
Description: New values specified for variables using??setVariable? method of?Simulink.SimulationInput ?object are silently ignored when ALL of the following conditions are true:? setVariable  is used to change values of variables in the model workspace of a referenced model. The Simulation mode  of the referenced model is?Accelerator . The?Model Reference Rebuild ?setting of the parent model is "If any changes in known dependencies detected".
Workaround: Change the Model Reference Rebuild setting to "If any changes detected"

Summary: Documentation search hangs on Chinese Windows systems when configured to search the installed Chinese documentation
Description: On Chinese Windows systems, when the help preferences are set to use the locally installed Chinese documentation, MATLAB hangs when you use the search.
Workaround: To search the documentation, change the help preferences to use the Chinese documentation on the web or the locally installed English documentation.?

Summary: Running Simulink Test test files with MATLAB Unit Test may result in multiple coverage reports for the same model
Description: Multiple Simulink Coverage reports for the same model may be generated under these conditions: A Simulink Test test file is configured to collect coverage using Simulink Coverage. The Simulink Test test file is run using the MATLAB Unit Test Runner. There are three or more test cases in a test suite, and each test case uses the same model as the system under test (SUT).  The Simulink Coverage reports are shown on the result set, and paths are printed to the MATLAB Command line when using the sltest.plugins.ModelCoveragePlugin .
Workaround: The last coverage report in the Result Set or printed to the command line will have the total aggregated results.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Minimize Global Resets and Model Reference blocks can cause incorrect module instantiation
Description: When using Minimize Global Resets in a design which also has a Model Reference block, the module instantiation for the Model Reference block can have incorrect inputs. The module for the Model Reference block maintains its reset signal, and the first data input to the module is passed in as the reset signal instead (In1_signal -> reset_port). Every subsequent input n  is passed in to the port pertaining to input n-1 ?(e.g. In2_signal -> In1_port) and the last input port is left unconnected. The result can be either HDL compilation issues or HDL testbench failures depending on the data type conflict.

Summary: Accessing Cloud Center clusters without a valid online license displays an incorrect error message
Description: When you attempt to use a Cloud Center cluster and you do not have an online license for MATLAB Parallel Server, you can get one of the following error messages: You are not signed up to use MATLAB Parallel Cloud. To sign up, click here . You are not signed up to use MATLAB Parallel Cloud. To sign up, run parcluster('MATLAB Parallel Cloud')   The message should say: Your MathWorks account is not associated with a MATLAB Parallel Server license that is managed online.   If you want to use Cloud Center clusters, sign up for an online license for MATLAB Parallel Server. On the MATLAB Home  tab, in the Environment  section, click Parallel  > Create and Manage Clusters , then click the Create Cloud Cluster  button in the toolstrip. If you do not want to create a cluster at this point, then close the dialog once you have reached the Create Cluster  stage.

Summary: Sporadic MATLAB crash when using confusionchart or wordcloud
Description: MATLAB may crash when using the confusionchart  or wordcloud  functions.

Summary: Build process fails when?TargetHWDeviceType is set to RTW.HWDeviceRegistry object that has no Alias value
Description: You can register a custom hardware device through a?RTW.HWDeviceRegistry  object. If you do not specify a value for the Alias  property of the object and specify the device for the model configuration parameter? 'TargetHWDeviceType' , then building the model produces this error: Error using mf.zero.PrimitiveSequence/ at?Invalid index.?Error in Simulink.filegen.internal.FolderSpecificationTokens/ resolveTargetEnvironemntToken (line 138)?????????????????value = device.AliasList.at(1);?Error in Simulink.filegen.internal.FolderSpecificationTokens (line 63)?????????????this.registerToken('TARGETENVIRONMENT', Simulink.filegen.internal.FolderSpecificationTokens.resolveTargetEnvironemntToken(hardwareDevice, ?stf));?Error in Simulink.filegen.internal.FolderConfiguration (line 227)?????????????this.Tokens = Simulink.filegen.internal.FolderSpecificationTokens(this.ModelName, ?isProtected, resolveLoadDependentTokens, ?stf);?Error in slbuild_private (line 459)?????folders = Simulink.filegen.internal.FolderConfiguration(mdl);?Error in sl (line 15)?[varargout{1:nargout}]=feval(varargin{:});?Error in slbuild (line 83)?????sl('slbuild_private', ?mdl, ?target, ?varargin{2:end});?Error in rtwbuild (line 234)???????????????slbuild(sys, ?'StandaloneRTWTarget', ?...
Workaround: To specify an Alias property value for the RTW.HWDeviceRegistry object, use the device registration procedure:function sl_customization(cm)  cm.registerTargetInfo(@loc_register_device);endfunction thisDev = loc_register_device  thisDev = RTW.HWDeviceRegistry;  thisDev.Vendor = 'myDevVendor';  thisDev.Type = 'myDevType';  thisDev.Alias = {'VendorTypeAlias'};  thisDev.Platform = {'Prod', 'Target'};  thisDev.setWordSizes([8 16 32 32 32]);  ...end

Summary: MATLAB crash due to unresolved path of an externally saved test harness
Description: MATLAB might crash when you change the interface of a subsystem or chart that has an externally saved test harness, if the test harness is not on the MATLAB path.?
Workaround: To workaround this issue, add the saved location of the test harness to the MATLAB path.

Summary: Error converting configuration to reference and saving the mat file in a path that contains a space character
Description: When you convert a configuration to a configuration reference, if you select the Save configuration set to file  option and choose a file/ path name that contains a space character (for example:?c:\ path name\ filename ), an error occurs because of the space character in the file path.
Workaround: Save the file to a folder that does not contain a space character in the path.

Summary: Intensity value lost when using pcdownsample, pcdenoise, and pcmerge functions
Description: The pcdownsample , pcdenoise , and pcmerge  functions return a pointCloud  object with an empty value for the Intensity  property.?

Summary: cd?command fails for NTFS paths/network drive letter paths having junctions
Description: On Windows, the MATLAB?cd?command fails in the below scenarios :    NTFS network path has directory junctions either to directories in different hard disk partitions, or physical drives on a remote machine.   The directory junction, or symbolic link, is created using the Windows? mklink? command.  For example, junction1?is a directory junction to folder?Z:\folder1?on the Z: drive on a remote machine. Diagnostic error shown is as below:   cd \\RemoteMachine\ShareFolder\junction1?   Error using cd    Cannot CD to \\RemoteMachine\ShareFolder\junction1 (Name is nonexistent or not a directory).   NTFS network path is mounted as a drive letter path and has a directory junction to directories on different hard disk partitions or physical drives on a remote machine.    For example,?junction1?is a directory junction to folder?Z:\folder1?on the Z: drive on a remote machine and?the shared network path is mounted to drive letter Y: such that Y:\ is equivalent to \\RemoteMachine\ShareFolder      Diagnostic error shown is as below:               cd? Y:\junction1               Error using cd                Cannot CD to??Y:\junction1? (Name is nonexistent or not a directory).?                  Note: Make sure Z:\ drive/partition does not exist on your local machine

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model?with an?Assignment block
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: Configuration Parameters > System target file ?parameter is set to 'ert.tlc' . Configuration Parameters >?Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks  parameter is selected. The Input data port and at least one index signal port of an Assignment block share the same source.  This may result in an uninitialized local variable in the generated code.
Workaround: Clear the Configuration Parameters >?Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks?parameter.

Summary: MATLAB will crash when loading a figure with pinned data tips
Description: If you save a figure with pinned data tips and then attempt to load the figure, MATLAB will crash.

Summary: Foreground streaming operations might fail at low scan rates
Description: A startForeground  operation with National Instruments hardware might fail at low scan rates. For example, using the NI USB-6351, a session running at 100 Hz might fail with the following message: s = daq.createSession('ni');addAnalogInputChannel(s,'Dev1',0:15,'Voltage');s.Rate=100;startForeground(s); Warning: Error occurred while executing the listener callback for event DataWritten defined for class asyncio.InputStream:Unable to perform assignment because the left and right sides have a different number of elements.
Workaround: For devices that support faster sampling, running at a higher rate avoids this issue.

Summary: niqe?function has dependency on Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox or Financial Toolbox
Description: If you call the niqe  function and you do not have Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox or Financial Toolbox installed, you get the following error: 'nanmean' requires one of the following:  ? Financial Toolbox  ? Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox    Error in niqeModel/calculateNIQEscore (line 173)  ? ? ? ? ? ? mu_testparam? ? ?= nanmean(full_NSSfeatures);   Error in niqe (line 89)  score = model.calculateNIQEscore(im);? ?

Summary: PowerPoint template background image missing from PPT API presentation
Description: The? background image that is specified in the slide layout of a PowerPoint template might not appear in the output presentation that is generated by using the PPT API.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initialization value for State Writer block that writes to the state of fixed-point data type
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that satisfies the following conditions: Model contains State Writer block that writes to a state of fixed-point data type. State Writer block writes a fixed-point value where the real world value and stored integer (SI) value do not match.  In such cases, the generated code for the state writer might write an incorrect value to the state variable.
Workaround: Use the Initial condition?block parameter of the corresponding block rather than the State Writer inside an Initialize Function. If the initial condition is not a constant or if the State Writer is not inside an Initialize Function, convert the state to an integer type of the same word length and signedness as the fixed point type and work directly with the stored integer value, if possible. For example, if the State Writer is writing the state of a Delay block, convert the Delay to an integer type and use data type conversion blocks at the delay input and output to convert the fixed point type to its stored integer value.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unit conversion factor may be wrong between frequency and angular_frequency physical quantities
Description: Unit conversion factor?between frequency and angular_frequency physical quantities?may?be wrong. For example, the conversion?between 1/s@frequency  and rad/s@angular_frequency  may?output a wrong numeric?result. This error may?occur in the Unit Conversion block and in implicit conversions.
Workaround: Use Gain block and specify Gain as the conversion factor, instead of using the Unit Conversion block. Avoid implicit conversion between frequency and angular frequency.

Summary: Vehicle wheel in 3D environment wobbles when you simultaneously vary the wheel camber and rotation angles
Description: In the 3D visualization environment, if you simultaneously vary the camber (roll) and rotation (pitch) angles on the vehicle wheel, the wheel rotates about the wrong axis.? The wheel appears to wobble.

Summary: The Model Advisor check "Check input and output settings of MATLAB Functions" (ID: mathworks.maab.na_0034) flags Enums and Bus Objects
Description: Enums and Bus Objects does not have complexity information on them but the MAAB "Check input and output settings of MATLAB Functions" (ID: mathworks.maab.na_0034) flags both the types for not having the right complexity setting as per the guideline and recommends to change them.

Summary: XCP Configuration block generates error when A2L file has extended flag for CAN_ID_MASTER or CAN_ID_SLAVE
Description: In a Simulink model with a XCP Configuration block, if an A2L file is specified that includes an extended CAN_ID_MASTER  or CAN_ID_SLAVE  parameter, the following error occurs.   Error evaluating 'InitFcn' callback of XCP Configuration block

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Deep learning code generation with the TensorRT library produces incorrect results for networks containing maxPooling2dLayer with asymmetric padding
Description: Generating C++ code for prediction from a deep learning network using the TensorRT library produces incorrect results, if network contains maxPooling2dLayer  with asymmetric padding. ?For example, a network trained with the following layer structure, produces incorrect results. ?layers = [imageInputLayer([3, 3, 1], 'Normalization', 'none', 'name', 'input')   ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?maxPooling2dLayer(2, 'Stride', [1 1], 'Padding', [0 1 0 1], 'name', 'maxpool')  ? ? ? ? ? regressionLayer('name', 'regression')];  R2018b does not support code generation for maxPooling2dLayer  with asymmetric padding when targeting the TensorRT library.?
Workaround: Use cuDNN library when generating code for networks containing?maxPooling2dLayer?with asymmetric padding.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong output?for AUTOSAR models?with Switch block with alias type on input signal
Description: Incorrect code is generated for an AUTOSAR model if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a Switch block with two data inputs and a control input. The data and control signals are vectors (size > 1). The data type of one data input signal is an alias type of the other. One of the data input signals is passed to the block output based on the control input signal. The block output feeds the root outport.  Under these conditions, in the generated code, the data written to the root outport is incorrect.

Summary: Simulation of an S-Function with a large number of elements in DWork fails with "Memory Allocation Error"
Description: When you run a model that contains an S-Function with a large number of elements in DWork, Simulink might throw a "Memory Allocation Error". In rare situations where the number of elements in DWork is extremely large, MATLAB will crash.

Summary: Flight Control Analysis Tools dialog windows appear partially offscreen on low-resolution monitors
Description: Flight Control Analysis Tools dialog windows, used for analysis workflow input selection, appear partially offscreen on low-resolution monitors.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Invalid error thrown with asynchronous interrupts driving ports with variant condition
Description: When an asynchronous system is driving a variant conditionalinput port of a subsystem, model throws an error as below:  Because the block '<block name>' is asynchronously executed, destination at output port 1 must be a rate transition, or root output port that inherits sample time, or an asynchronous function-call subsystem.   When user selects?Configuration Parameters>Solver>Solverdetails>Tasking and sample time options>Automatically handle ratetransition for data transfer? and simulates, Simulink inserts rate transition blockincorrectly which may cause wrong result.

Summary: DPI component generation errors if the model contains Simscape blocks
Description: Code generation errors when generating a DPI component from a Simulink model that contains Simscape blocks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation with Function Packaging configured for a conditional non-virtual Subsystem
Description: Incorrect code may be generated when a Variant Source is connected directly to an Inport of a non-virtual Subsystem Below are the scenarios where the incorrect code generation may happen:? Non-virtual Subsystem contains more than one input port. A multiple input Variant Source is directly connected to Inport of a non-virtual Subsystem. Condition on the input port is not same as the subsystem's condition. The non-virtual Subsystem has Function Packaging  parameter set to?Reusable function/Nonreusable  function.  ?Example:Consider a Variant source inside the non-virtual subsystem. The subsystem block's condition is (for example: A==1||A==2). However, Inport1 directly connected to Variant source within has condition A==1. Inport2 connected to Variant source has condition A==2.

Summary: Incorrect confusionchart data display with sorted classes
Description: If you create a chart with the?confusionchart  function, and change the order of its classes  with the  sortClasses  function, then the chart does  not display correctly in the Live Editor and does not save and load correctly  at the command line.  If you save and then reload the chart, then the order of data in the chart is permuted with respect to the order of class labels, so the chart does not show the correct data. The  confusionchart  function is  available in both Deep Learning Toolbox and Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox.
Workaround: To work around this problem outside Live Editor, before saving the chart, use the sortClasses function to return the order of classes to that set on chart creation. The chart will then save and load correctly.When using Live Editor, after using the sortClasses function, open the chart in a figure window.

Summary: Running?test case with MATLAB Unit Test framework?and the ModelCoveragePlugin results in an error for models containing a Model Reference block with a test harness
Description: When using the sltest.plugins.ModelCoveragePlugin  class?to generate coverage reports for a model which includes a referenced model which has a test harness, the following error can occur: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.    Error in sltest.internal.sources.ModelSource (line 18)  obj.TopLevelModel = managerCoverageResults.TopLevelModel;    Error in sltest.internal.coverage.returnModelCoverageResults (line 20)  theSources(end+1) = ModelSource(coverageResults(coverageResultsIndex),resultSetIDs(resulSetIndex));    Error in sltest.plugins.ModelCoveragePlugin/runTestSuite (line 222)  [modelCoverageResults,modelSources] = returnModelCoverageResults(plugin.ResultSetIDs);    Error in matlab.unittest.plugins.TestRunnerPlugin/invokeTestContentOperatorMethod_ (line 686)  [varargout{1:nargout}] = method(plugin, pluginData);    Error in matlab.unittest.plugins.TestRunnerPlugin/runTestSuite (line 226)  iter.CurrentOperator.invokeTestContentOperatorMethod_(@runTestSuite, pluginData, iter);    Error in sltest.plugins.TestManagerResultsPlugin/runTestSuite (line 59)  plugin.runTestSuite@matlab.unittest.plugins.TestRunnerPlugin(pluginData);    Error in matlab.unittest.plugins.TestRunnerPlugin/invokeTestContentOperatorMethod_ (line 686)  [varargout{1:nargout}] = method(plugin, pluginData);    Error in matlab.unittest.TestRunner/evaluateMethodOnPlugins (line 756)  [varargout{1:nargout}] = iter.CurrentOperator.invokeTestContentOperatorMethod_(method, pluginData, iter);    Error in matlab.unittest.TestRunner/doRun (line 411)  runner.evaluateMethodOnPlugins(@runTestSuite, pluginData);    Error in matlab.unittest.TestRunner/run (line 290)  result = doRun(runner);    Error in TestModelCoveragePlugin (line 41)  testResult = runner.run(ts);
Workaround: Leverage the coverage results after adding the sltest.plugins.TestManagerResultsPlugin object to create a Model Coverage report using the cvhtml function.runner = matlab.unittest.TestRunner.WithTextOutput();runner.addPlugin(sltest.plugins.TestManagerResultsPlugin);suite = testsuite('SimulinkTestFile.mldatx');results = runner.run(suite);?cvhtml('/path/to/coveragePage.html',results.Details.SimulinkTestManagerResults.CoverageResults);

Summary: Installing latest update to Visual Studio 2017 can cause target computer to report real-time application download error
Description: Installing the latest update to Visual Studio 2017 (15.6 or later) and building a model can cause an error when you download the real-time application to the target computer. The error that appears on the target computer is: Unable to load DLL dependency: KERNEL32.dll.GetFileSizeEx  Because the Visual Studio 2017 update modifies the Windows 10 SDK on the development computer, this update causes the same issue (error on real-time application download) to occur for models built after the update with Visual Studio 2015 on that development computer.? For more information about this update, see?Update Visual Studio 2017 to the most recent release .
Workaround: For MATLAB releases from R2016b through R2018b update 1, use these steps to rerun the Visual Studio 2017 installer and change the version of the Windows 10 SDK that is installed.Rerun the Visual Studio 2017 installer.With the installer running, push the 'modify' button.Choose 'Individual components' in the top menu bar.A long list of components that are installed is shown.Scroll down to the 'SDKs, libraries, and frameworks' section.Continue down to the Windows 10 SDK choices.Deselect 'Windows 10 SDK (10.0.17763.0)' and make sure 'Windows 10 SDK (10.17134.0)' is selected.Click the 'Modify' button in the lower right corner to complete the change.Now, both Visual Studio 2015 and Visual Studio 2017 will compile models that can be loaded on the target computer.

Summary: Warnings are not displayed in the Simulation Manager
Description: When using the Simulation Manager  to monitor the status of Simulink simulations, warnings generated during the simulation are not shown in the Diagnostics  tab when there are multiple similar warnings. The status of the simulation correctly indicates "Completed with Warnings'. For example, if there are two or more warnings about unconnected lines, the Diagnostics  tab will show up blank. Multiple different warnings or a single warning message will be shown as expected.?
Workaround: The warnings can be?accessed?from?SimulationMetadata?on the?SimulationOutput?object.

Summary: Failure to import DOORS module with tables: Dimensions of arrays being concatenated are not consistent
Description: Simulink Requirements fails to import a?module?of?IBM Rational DOORS requirements if this?module contains a table of requirements, and?one or more cells were?previously deleted?from the?original DOORS table.? The import function throws the following error message:  Dimensions of arrays being concatenated are not consistent

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with?OptimizeBlockOrder?model configuration parameter set to Improved code execution speed
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains more than one Function Caller block. In the Model Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Optimize block operation order in the generated code? parameter is set to Improved code execution speed.
Workaround: Set?the?Optimize block operation order in the generated code??model configuration parameter to None.

Summary: Crash when generating code for model that uses Simulink Function and Function Caller blocks and Stateflow
Description: Generating code for a model causes MATLAB to crash if all of these conditions are true: Model uses an ERT-based system target file. Model includes Simulink Function or Function Caller blocks and is using Stateflow. Model data and function interfaces are configured with code mappings. For example, you can configure code mappings by using the Code Mappings editor.  When the crash occurs, the stack trace contains text which is similar to the following text: [ 2] 0x00007ff641e05479 bin/glnxa64/libmwcodermapping.so+01885305 _ZN15coderdictionary23SimulinkFunctionMapping27getCodeInputOutputArguments EPK8SLRootBDRSt6vectorINSt7__cxx1112basic_stringIcSt11char_traitsIcESaIcEEESaISA_EESD_+00000233?

Summary: Incorrect simulation results possible when names of variables are longer than 55 characters
Description: Using MATLAB identifiers of length exceeding 55 characters within Simscape run-time parameter expressions could cause simulation failure or incorrect results.

Summary: Figure shown in parsim example is incorrect
Description: The figure shown in parsim example "Rapid Accelerator Simulations Using Parsim" is incorrect. Creating the plot using the output from the simulations gives a different plot than the one shown in the documentation. Specifically, the correct plot should show eight different curves corresponding to each of the simulations.

Summary: Deleting a linked block from a library might crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB crashes or produces an error when the following conditions occur: A Simulink library links to blocks inside the same library. The library is unlocked for modification. Another window or tab contains a linked block. The source block is deleted from the library.  If you select the linked block inside the library after the source block has been deleted, MATLAB crashes. If source block deletion fails, the MATLAB command window displays this error: Assertion failed: Deviant '43' :?object of type 'SLM3I.Block' [id = -1 ] is not present at?b:\ matlab\ src\ m3i\ kernel\ deviant\ deviant.cpp:72:?

Summary: A saved RF Blockset model containing an RF Filter block reopens with default RF Filter block settings and not with the saved settings.
Description: If you open a saved model, it will run the model with the default RF filter block settings even though the mask contains the correct saved settings.? This is a silent wrong answer.

Summary: Unable to open or delete Spectrum Analyzer Block
Description: You might be unable to open or delete Spectrum Analyzer block if both of the following conditions are true:? Model saved with Channel Measurements enabled. Inherited or specified sample rate is greater than 10kHz.

Summary: When closing a window MATLAB sometimes crashes with an abnormal termination message
Description: MATLAB might occasionally crash when you close some windows, such as Add-On Explorer, Add-On Manager, App Designer, UIFigure windows, or the Simulink Template Gallery. The crash report includes the line:   "std::terminate() detected"  And the report lists the method:   webwindow_extprocess:BrowserAppProcess::~BrowserAppProcess

Summary: Simulink uses old values of data dictionary entries when dictionary is closed or recreated while in use
Description: If a Simulink model uses a data dictionary and you manually recreate the dictionary at the command-line (by deleting the original dictionary and creating or copying in a new dictionary), the model continues to use the data from the original dictionary. The same problem occurs if you manually close the dictionary (by calling Simulink.data.dictionary.closeAll ) and then open and modify the dictionary by using the command-line dictionary API.
Workaround: Avoid deleting then copying or recreating data dictionaries. Instead, use the command-line dictionary API to modify the contents of the dictionary. Do not close the dictionary prior to making changes.Alternatively, close all models and dictionaries that use a dictionary before deleting then copying or recreating it.

Summary: Incorrect promotion of LookupTableObject parameter of lookup table block to mask
Description: Promotion of the LookupTableObject  parameter of a Lookup Table block to its mask may result in the following error: Reference to non-existence field 'moveTo'   Older release models that have the LookupTableObject  parameter promoted to the block, may lose this promoted parameter when the model is upgraded to R2018b release version.

Summary: Polyspace compilation error related to type size_t in a project created with polyspace-configure from a Cygwin build
Description: In a C++ project created with polyspace-configure , when you run Polyspace, you might see an error message related to the type size_t . For example: C:\pathto\include\c++\6.3.0\new, line 116: error: first parameter of allocation function must be of type "size_t"  |? void* operator new(std::size_t) |??????? ^?   The message indicates that the underlying type of?size_t  used in the Polyspace analysis conflicts with the underlying type in a standard library. The issue can happen from many reasons. In this bug, the issue happened because the underlying type of?size_t ?was set incorrectly by the prior run of the?polyspace-configure  command. The polyspace-configure ?command when run on a Cygwin build used Cygwin-style paths?(/?cygdrive/?c/** ) for the?%TEMP%  environment variable instead of?the Windows-style one. As a result, an error happened?in the?polyspace-configure ?run, which looked like this: polyspace-configure: ...WARNING: Compiler <pathto>/bin/<compilername>.exe (with extension '.c') returned 1 during preprocessing  polyspace-configure: ...ERROR: Could not read preprocessed file <random value>_preprocess2.txt?   When the error happened,?polyspace-configure  used some default heuristics to deduce the underlying type of?size_t , which happened to not match the one used in your standard library headers.  This behavior has been observed with a build command using TI compilers.?
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: Site Viewer terrain data contains geographical offset
Description: The terrain data used by Site Viewer and RF Propagation functions contains a geographical offset. The offset amount is location-dependent and may be up to 125 meters. The offset may be visible for some locations in Site Viewer and reduces the accuracy of signal strength estimates by the following functions:?coverage , sinr , sigstrength , and link .

Summary: Embedded Coder might generate uncompilable code for models with reusable interface and continuous time state charts
Description: Embedded Coder might generate uncompilable code under the following conditions: The model is configured with Configuration Parameters  > Code interface packaging  set to Reusable function . The model contains a Stateflow chart that calls Simulink functions with continuous?states. In the Stateflow chart, block parameter Function packaging  is set to anything other than Reusable function .  The generated C code might not compile and an error message like this is issued: $(model_name)_PrevZCX' : undeclared identifier
Workaround: Set the?Function packaging?block parameter of the Stateflow chart that calls Simulink functions with continuous?states to Reusable function.

Summary: Linearization I/O on Bus Inport blocks might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: When you place a linearization I/O on a Bus Inport block and the corresponding element of the Inport block is a leaf element of a bus signal, MATLAB might crash during the linearization.
Workaround: Add a Signal Conversion block after the Bus Inport block.?Set the Signal Conversion block to signal copy.?Remove the linearization I/O from Bus Inport block output port.?Add linearization I/O at the inserted Signal Conversion block output port.?

Summary: MATLAB windows operate with limited functionality or fail to start on Linux
Description: When using MATLAB on Ubuntu 18.10, some tools, including Simulink and the Add-Ons Manager, can produce this error or warning message: Error using matlab.internal.webwindow (line 315) The MATLABWindow application failed to launch because of missing libraries. Install the packages which contain the following libraries:?? ? libgtk-x11-2.0.so.0???? libgtk-x11-2.0.so.0???? libgdk-x11-2.0.so.0   Other tools, like the MATLAB Help browser, operate with limited functionality but do not throw an error or warning.In the Live Editor, a dialog box displays stating that "The Live Editor is unable to run in the current system configuration".  These issues occur because MATLAB requires?the libgtk2.0-0 package, which is not installed by default on Ubuntu 18.10. For a similar issue see?Bug Report?1765886
Workaround: To install the required library on an Ubuntu system, run this command:$ sudo apt-get install libgtk2.0-0

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation when using the?forward?operator in Stateflow and the FIFO queue is overflowed
Description: Using the the  forward ?operator for f orwarding a message to a FIFO queue that is overflowed?may produce incorrect generated code or wrong results when simulating using rapid accelerator mode.

Summary: TextArea is not scrollable when Editable?is set to 'off'
Description: TextArea  object is not scrollable when the  Editable  property value is set to  'off' , but it is scrollable when  Editable ?is set to  'on' .  When  Editable ?is 'on'  (default value), the text area is scrollable:?  ? ? txa = uitextarea(uifigure,'Value',repmat('a',1,100));  When  Editable ?is 'off'  , the text area is no longer scrollable:?  ? ? txa.Editable = 'off';
Workaround: Position a Label component so it is wide enough to cover the content of an editable text area, but narrow enough not to cover the scrollbar. This makes only the scrollbar interactive, so all scroll interactions must occur with the mouse positioned over the scrollbar itself. Since this makes the editable text area non-interactive, the content cannot be edited.? ? % Create a TextArea with 'Editable' set to 'on' (default value)? ? f = uifigure;?? ? txa = uitextarea(f,'Value',repmat('a',1,100),'Tag','BlockedTextArea');?? ? %?Block mouse interactions of TextArea with a Label component? ? blockingLabel = uilabel(f,'Position',txa.Position,'Text','','Tag','BlockingLabel');??? ? % Position Label to cover the TextArea portion, but not the scrollbar.? ? % This makes only the scrollbar interactive.? ? % Scrollbar width is Operating System specific.?? ? % 15 is an approximation for Windows.?Mac scrollbars tend to be narrower.?? ? scrollbarWidth = 15;? ? blockingLabel.Position(3) = blockingLabel.Position(3) - scrollbarWidth;?? ??? ? % Use the figure's SizeChangedFcn to update the Label's position?? ? % whenever the TextArea's position changes due to a figure resize.? ? % The AutoResizeChildren property needs to be 'off' to use this callback.? ? f.AutoResizeChildren = 'off';? ? f.SizeChangedFcn = @blockingLabelResize;?? ? % Define the callback function?? ? function blockingLabelResize(src,event)? ? ? ? scrollbarWidth = 15;? ? ? ? targetTextArea = findobj(src,'Tag','BlockedTextArea');? ? ? ? targetLabel = findobj(src,'Tag','BlockingLabel');? ? ? ? targetLabel.Position = targetTextArea.Position;? ? ? ? targetLabel.Position(3) = targetTextArea.Position(3) - scrollbarWidth;? ? end?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code is missing necessary data copy when using multiple handle classes that reference the same object
Description: Incorrect code generation might occur when creating references (aliases) with handle classes under the following conditions: The code contains at least two handle class variables that reference the same object. A property of one of the handle class objects is passed as an argument to a function which writes to its corresponding input argument. Following the function call from condition 2, the same property on one of the other handle class objects is read. For example, it could be read by passing it to another function which reads the value.  ?For example, consider the following MATLAB code:? function [y11,y12,y13,y14,y15,y16] = issue_1884256(zero,vec,mat,idxVec)  ? ? --SNIP--  ? ? handle_alias_mat = createMCOSAlias( handle_mat );  ? ? --SNIP--  ? ? [y13,y14] = fcn_1( handle_alias_mat.value, handle_alias_zero.value - 1, handle_alias_idxVec.value );  ? ? --SNIP--  ? ? [handle_alias_y15.value,y16] = fcn_2( handle_y15.value, handle_mat.value, handle_zero.value - 15, handle_idxVec.value );  end?  Condition 1 is satisfied? by: handle_alias_mat = createMCOSAlias( handle_mat ); . The function createMCOSAlias  assigns its input to its output (creating an alias). Condition 2 is satisfied by:? [y13,y14] =?fcn_1( handle_alias_mat.value, handle_alias_zero.value - 1, handle_alias_idxVec.value ); . The function?fcn_1?writes to its first input argument (which corresponds to the property of one of the handle classes passed as the first argument). Condition 3 is satisfied by:? [handle_alias_y15.value,y16] =?fcn_2( handle_y15.value, handle_mat.value, handle_zero.value - 15, handle_idxVec.value );  The function fcn_2  reads its second input argument (which corresponds to the property of the other handle class passed as the second argument). The generated code for this example might be as follows: void?issue_1884256(double zero, const double vec[12], const double mat[99],  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? const double idxVec[20], double *y11, double y12[99], double  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? *y13, double y14[99], double y15[12], double y16[99])  {  ? --SNIP--?  ? handle_alias_mat = createMCOSAlias(&handle_mat);  ? --SNIP--?  ?? fcn_1(handle_alias_mat->value, idxVec, y14);  ? --SNIP--  ? fcn_2(handle_mat.value, y16);  }  However, the correct generated code should be (notice the extra necessary data copy):? void? issue_1884256(double zero, const double vec[12], const double mat[99],  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? const double idxVec[20], double *y11, double y12[99], double  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? *y13, double y14[99], double y15[12], double y16[99])  {  ? --SNIP--  ? handle_alias_mat = createMCOSAlias(&handle_mat);  ? --SNIP--  ? for (i = 0; i < 99; i++) {  ? ? b_handle_mat[i] = handle_alias_mat->value[i];  ? }  ? fcn_1(b_handle_mat, idxVec, y14);  ? --SNIP--  ? fcn_2(handle_mat.value, y16);  }?
Workaround: For MATLAB code that uses this modeling pattern, a workaround is to avoid using handle classes which reference the same object.

Summary: Code generation in parallel build fails with error:?Model is already in a compiled state as a referenced model
Description: If you use parallel building for your Simulink model, Simulink Coder can produce this error: Model is already in a compiled state as a referenced model  This error can occur if a previous parallel build of the model failed with a different error, or was terminated prematurely.
Workaround: Before you start a parallel build, run this command:spmd bdclose('all'); endThe command closes (without saving) open models on the parallel build workers.

Summary: Subcompartments of a model are not visible in the SimBiology diagram
Description: When you open a model in the SimBiology desktop, some subcompartments might not be visible in the diagram. The model contains the subcompartments, but you are not able manipulate the model using the diagram anymore.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Execution order of disp function call and nested loop containing run-time error not correct in generated code
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a MATLAB function that meets these conditions: The function contains a for -loop with a disp ?statement followed by a nested for -loop The data used in the nested for -loop is invariant of the data used in the parent loop. The numeric output of the function does not change if the nested for -loop moves before its parent loop. A run-time error occurs in the nested loop code.  When these conditions are met, the generated code might not preserve the order of the disp ?function call and the nested loop because an optimization in the code generator moves the nested loop out of its parent loop. An example MATLAB function is below: function y = issue_1883778(inData,idx)    y = zeros(100,1);    for offset = 1:10       disp('Entered Loop');       disp(offset);       for k = 1:10           y(k) = inData(idx + k);       end    endend If this function executes with a value of idx  that attempts to access an out-of-bounds value of the? inData  array, ?there is a run-time error in the nested loop. Note that the disp? statements execute before the runtime error.>> issue_1883778(zeros(20,1),21)Entered Loop     1Index exceeds the number of array elements (20).Index exceeds array dimensions.  Index value 22 exceeds valid range [1-20] of array inData.Error in issue_1883778 (line 11)           y(k) = inData(idx + k);Error in issue_1883778 (line 11)           y(k) = inData(idx + k); When you generate a mex function for this function, the run-time error instead happens prior to the execution of the disp  statements>> cfg = coder.config('mex');>> cfg.ResponsivenessChecks = false;>> codegen -config cfg issue_1883778 -args {zeros(20,1),21}>> issue_1883778_mex(zeros(20,1),21)Index exceeds array dimensions.  Index value 22 exceeds valid range [1-20] of array inData.Error in issue_1883778 (line 11)           y(k) = inData(idx + k);

Summary: Error when simulating or generating code from model that contains multiple cross-release blocks
Description: For a model that contains multiple cross-release SIL/PIL blocks, simulation or code generation produces an error when all  of the following conditions apply:?? The model contains two or more cross-release blocks. The cross-release blocks reference code generated using the non-reusable top model code interface or the single-instance model reference code interface. The cross-release blocks all reference code from?different ?build folders. The model has been saved, closed, and then re-opened.  You see this error: Cross-release imported code 'pathToSourceFile ' does not support multiple instances, but multiple instances of the cross-release blocks were found:  firstBlock   secondBlock   ...?
Workaround: For each cross-release SIL/PIL block that is affected, disable the single-instance validation:set_param(block, 'XRelIsReusable', 'on')

Summary: Incorrect Simulation range checking warnings or errors for fixed-point type data
Description: W hen evaluating the Simulation range checking  diagnostic, Stateflow incorrectly compares the quantized integer of a fixed-point type data instead of its real-world value.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect reuse of?Function-Call Subsystem?containing State Writer block in generated code
Description: The reusable function generated?from a?Function-Call Subsystem containing a State Writer block might be incorrect if all of the following conditions are true:? A?model contains a Stateflow block or a non-virtual subsystem with a function-call output to a Function-Call Subsystem that contains a State Writer block. The parameter Function packaging  for the?Function-Call Subsystem?is set to Auto?  or using the?command ?set_param , the?parameter?  RTWSystemCode ?is set to 'Auto' . There are at least two identical instances of?the model with the?Function-Call Subsystem and a state owner block. After generating code for the top model, the code generation report shows that a reusable function has been generated for the?Function-Call Subsystem. To verify this problem, open the code generation report. In the left pane under Contents, select Subsystem Report. In the right pane?under the Code Mapping section, check the column Reuse Outcome for the?Function-Call Subsystem. If the reuse outcome is listed as?Reusable Function, your model is subject to this bug.  The code is incorrect because the reusable function only writes to the state of one particular instance of the state owner block. The intended behavior is that the state of the corresponding instance of the state owner block is passed into the reusable function to be accessed by the State Writer block.
Workaround: Set Function packaging to Inline or Nonreusable function for all?Function-Call Subsystems blocks?containing State Writer blocks.

Summary: MATLAB Crash while editing Simulink models
Description: In rare circumstances, MATLAB might crash when you add, copy, rename, or remove Simulink blocks, or during Simulation or Update-Diagram. The generated stack trace refers to: getBlocksWithName  or? removeBlockFromNameCaches?

Summary: External mode Connect error when building Simulink project model with custom code generation folder
Description: Suppose you create an XCP-based external mode target application by building a Simulink project model with a custom code generation folder. When you try to connect Simulink to the target application, you might see this error:  External Mode Open Protocol Connect command failed    Additionally,?an error?message dialog box shows this error message:   Executing the 'Connect' command produced the following error:  DWARF Parser internal error: Parser::initialize failed to open file   The error occurs because the internal DWARF parser is incorrectly trying to open the signal and parameter symbols file from the current folder instead of the custom code generation folder.

Summary: Simulink might crash when generating code for some System objects
Description: Simulink might crash?during code generation?for a model containing MATLAB System block when a System object implements the releaseImpl  method, or uses other System objects that implement the releaseImpl  method.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing calls for C charts with more than one call to rand()
Description: If you have a C action language chart that calls the rand()  C function more than once during the same time step, some of the calls might be incorrectly eliminated from the generated code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incremental rebuild ignored with normal MATLAB variant controls
Description: Incremental rebuild is ignored when a Variant block uses normal MATLAB variant control variables.  The incorrect behavior may cause numerical mismatch in code generation,f or example:  >> rtwdemo_preprocessor_subsys; >> VSSMODE = 1; >> rtwbuild(bdroot); >> VSSMODE = 0; //Change the Variant control value in the Base Workspace >> rtwbuild(bdroot); // Notice that code corresponding to the new value is not regenerated even though the value of variant control is changed.??  The incorrect behavior happens when all the below conditions are met: The Simulink model uses an ERT? based code generation target Variant block has Analyze all choices and generate pre-processor conditionals  selected Variant control variable is a normal MATLAB variant control and not a?Simulink.Parameter
Workaround: Use Simulink.Parameter instead of normal MATLAB variables. (OR)Remove slprj folder

Summary: MATLAB might crash when you open a?Bus Creator inside an inactive variant choice
Description: MATLAB might crash when you attempt to open a Bus Creator inside the inactive variant choice,if all the below conditions are TRUE:Model has a linked Variant Subsystem library block The linked Variant Subsystem block has an active choice
Workaround: Consider breaking the link to the library block.

Summary: An mdce service started in MATLAB R2018b using an mdce_def file copied from an earlier release cannot start workers
Description: The WORKER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY  property was added to the default mdce_def  file in MATLAB R2018b. If you start the mdce service using an mdce_def  file which is missing this new property, then the following error is issued when you start workers: Problem starting the MATLAB worker.?  The cause of the problem is:?============================================================================????timeout creating child process?????????This is causing:?????????Problem starting the worker group process.?????????This is causing:?????????Problem starting the worker group process.?============================================================================
Workaround: To resolve the problem, copy the missing line from the R2018b mdce_def file to the mdce_def file copied from a previous release.For a unix platform, add the following line to your mdce_def.sh file:WORKER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY="1024m"For a windows platform, add the following line to your mdce_def.bat file:?set WORKER_MAXIMUM_MEMORY=1024m?

Summary: Performance regression with imageDataAugmenter
Description: When training small deep learning networks on small images using imageDataAugmenter and trainNetwork, training times can be slightly longer compared with R2018a. This bug affects MATLAB Deep Learning Container for NVIDIA GPU Cloud.

Summary: Missing CONCRT140.dll error
Description: On Windows, if the Visual Studio 2017 redistributable runtime is not installed, Image Processing Toolbox throws an error when calling any function which depends on CONCRT140.dll (a Visual Studio 2017 C Runtime DLL). For example, >> strel('disk',10, 8);  Error using images.internal.morphmexInvalid MEX-file 'C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\toolbox\images\images\+images\+internal\morphmex.mexw64': Missing dependent shared libraries:'CONCRT140.dll' required by 'C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b/bin/win64\opencv_core340.dll->C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b/bin/win64\libmwmorphop_ocv.dll->C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\toolbox\images\images\+images\+internal\morphmex.mexw64'.

Summary: Incorrect incompatibility reported for Simulink Function with enumerated?interface?configured in data dictionary
Description: If your Simulink model satisfies all these conditions, Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check may incorrectly report the model as incompatible:  The model contains Simulink Functions  The parameters or arguments to a Simulink Function are Enumerated data types  The model uses a data-dictionary   The Diagnostic Viewer window displays this error:   Translation failed: Could not evaluate <Name of Enum Type> in data dictionary"

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated from a model containing Model blocks both with and without Initialize/Reset/Terminate Functions may fail to compile
Description: Code generated from a model may fail to compile due to mismatched signatures when all of the following conditions are true: The model contains one or more Model blocks. The top model or one of the submodels referenced by its Model blocks contains an Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block. At least one of the submodels referenced by a Model block does not contain an Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block. The submodel without?an Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block?uses only constant or triggered sample times.
Workaround: Place an empty Initialize Function, Reset Function, or Terminate Function block in the referenced model that does not have one.

Summary: Report API's customizeReporter method can generate an invalid subclass
Description: The Report API's customizeReporter  method can generate a reporter subclass that contains coding errors. This occurs if the method is used to generate a reporter that is a member of a? package of reporters.? For example, this command ?mlreportgen.report.TitlePage.customizeReporter("+my/@TitlePage")  generates a reporter named my.TitlePage  that contains coding errors. Adding an instance of this reporter to a report, for example, import mlreportgen.report.*rpt = Report('myreport');add(rpt, my.TitlePage);close(rpt);?  causes an error similar to? Undefined variable "TitlePage" or class "TitlePage.getClassFolder".?
Workaround: Replace occurrences of the unqualified class name (e.g., TitlePage) with the fully qualified name (e.g., my.TitlePage) in the following methods generated by the customizeReport method:getDefaultTemplatePathcreateTemplate

Summary: Error when executing Simulink Code Inspector qualification tests with MinGW compiler
Description: ?The following build error might occur when executing qualification tests for Simulink Code Inspector if you are using the MinGW compiler: Exception during eval : The model is configured for C++ code generation, but the C-only compiler, LCC, is the default compiler. To allow code generation, on the Code Generation pane:? 1. Select the 'Generate code only' check box.? 2. In the Toolchain field, select a toolchain from the drop-down list.? For information about C++ compiler support, see?  https://www.mathworks.com/support/compilers/current_release  .
Workaround: Follow these steps to make the model toolchain compliant: 1. Navigate to <matlabroot>/toolbox/qualkits/do/slci/tests.2. Open the model that encountered the error.3. Run the following command from the MATLAB command line: set_param(<modelName>,? 'RTWCompilerOptimization','off');4. Save the model.

Summary: Altitude and Julian date inputs to the WGS84 Gravity Model block are?limited to between 0?and 20,000?meters (altitude) and?before 2451545 (Jan 1, 2000)?(Julian date)
Description: Altitude and Julian date inputs to the WGS84 Gravity Model  block are?limited to between 0?and 20,000?meters (altitude) and?before 2451545  (Jan 1, 2000)?(Julian date), regardless of the Action for out-of-range input  setting.

Summary: Incorrect error on valid header file specified for imported Simulink Bus in data dictionary
Description: When?a model containing Stateflow and MATLAB Function blocks has a Simulink bus object in model associated data dictionary, Simulink may throw a false error on a Simulink Bus, claiming the imported bus doesn't have a valid 'HeaderFile'  specified. For example:  Custom code settings contain a structure type with the name 'A_BUS'. The bus type 'A_BUS' needs to have its 'HeaderFile' field specified with the header file name.
Workaround: In the?Configuration Parameters > Simulation Target pane, clear the Import custom code parameter.

Summary: Cannot start remote server from Polyspace Metrics and Remote Analysis Server Settings interface
Description: When an mdce service is started with the Start Server  button in the Polyspace Metrics and Remote Analysis Server Settings interface (polyspace-server-settings.exe ), the interface freezes and displays a timeout message:     Script startworker unable to complete successfully - exiting    The mdce service on the host     returned the following error:    Problem starting the MATLAB worker.
