Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation might ignore property validation code for a class in a package
Description: MATLAB classes can contain code to validate the type, size, and some invariants of a property value. In certain cases, MATLAB Coder and MATLAB Function Blocks in Simulink might fail to apply the property validation rules when generating C code for an assignment to an object property. Specifically, validation code is ignored if the class containing the validated property is located inside a package. For example, suppose that the class VAClass  is in a folder named +package  and that the function xValidate  creates an object of class VAClass . classdef VAClass  properties    Prop (1, 1) double  endend function out = xValidate  c2 = package.VAClass;  c2.Prop = 'a';  out = c2.Prop;end The generated C code for XValidate  computes a different value than MATLAB computes. Because property Prop  is constrained to be of type double, the assignment c2.Prop = 'a'  is supposed to convert 'a'  into a double. In code generation, if VAClass  is located inside a package, this assignment writes a character to the property without converting it to a double.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Conditionally called function with persistent system object incorrectly called unconditionally in generated code
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for MATLAB code that meets these conditions: ?An if-else statement calls a function in both branches of the if-else statement. The called function contains the use of a persistent system object.  In the case of the incorrect generated code, the code contains calls to the function before the if-else statement. The result of the calls are stored in temporary variables and used within the generated if-else statement. This code is incorrect if the persistent system object updates some form of internal state when used.  For an example of this issue see the model issue_model_1836686 in the attached file issue_example_1836686.zip. This MATLAB Function block in this model contains an if-else statement calling calledFcn if(in2 > 0)  y = calledFcn(in1);else  y = calledFcn(-in1);end? The function calledFcn uses a persistent system object, dsp.FIRFilter .? In the generated code, the function calls occur before the if-else statement.  issue_model_1836686_calledFcn(issue_model_1836686_P.Constant_Value, tmp);  for (i = 0; i < 9; i++) {    tmp_0[i] = -issue_model_1836686_P.Constant_Value[i];  }  issue_model_1836686_calledFcn(tmp_0, tmp_1);  tmp_2 = (issue_model_1836686_U.In1 > 0.0);  if (tmp_2) {    memcpy(&issue_model_1836686_Y.Out1[0], &tmp[0], 9U * sizeof(real_T));  } else {    memcpy(&issue_model_1836686_Y.Out1[0], &tmp_1[0], 9U * sizeof(real_T));  } This is incorrect because the function issue_model_1836686_calledFcn updates the internal state of a dsp.FIRFilter. Therefore it should only be called depending on the branch taken by the if-else statement. The correct generated code can be seen below:?   if (issue_model_1836686_U.In1 > 0.0) {    issue_model_1836686_calledFcn(issue_model_1836686_P.Constant_Value,      issue_model_1836686_Y.Out1);  } else {    for (i = 0; i < 9; i++) {      tmp[i] = -issue_model_1836686_P.Constant_Value[i];    }    issue_model_1836686_calledFcn(tmp, issue_model_1836686_Y.Out1);  } This issue might affect the following code generation workflows:Code generation using MATLAB Coder only Code generation from a Simulink model containing MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks Code generation from Stateflow charts containing MATLAB Functions

Summary: Symbols window might incorrectly show data as unused
Description: When a Stateflow chart that was saved with the Symbols window hidden is opened, data that is used in the chart might appear as unused after opening the Symbols window.
Workaround: Make any edit to the Stateflow chart to update the data in the Symbols window.

Summary: Slow model compilation for output signals nested in large virtual buses
Description: Update diagram, simulation, and code generation may be slow when an atomic subsystem or model has an output signal nested in a large virtual bus. These processes may display status "Compiling: Setting up implicit testpoints: Completed"  for longer than expected. These conditions may contribute to slow model compilation: Specifying?'inf'  for the sample time of a Constant block.  Having this Constant block connect directly?to an Outport block in a conditional subsystem.

Summary: Error about GLIBC_2.14 not found while attempting to perform cosimulation in MATLAB or Simulink
Description: When trying to perform cosimulation in MATLAB or Simulink, HDL Simulators (QuestaSim, Incisive, Xcelium), MATLAB may generate the following error: /lib64/libc.so.6: version `GLIBC_2.14' not found(required by /tmp/24736/ml_wXPs/matlab/toolbox/edalink/extensions/modelsim/linux64/liblfmhdlc_gcc450.so)

Summary: A Dataflow Subsystem inside aVariant Subsystem or Inline Variant may cause unexpected behavior.
Description: If a model contains a variantsubsystem in which one of the variant choices is a Dataflow Subsystem block andthe "Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessorconditionals"? parameter of the Variant Subsystem block is selected,simulation and code generation of the model might cause unexpected behavior,such as a crash. If a model contains a Dataflow Subsystem block as avariant choice of an inline variant (Variant Source or Variant Sinkblock)? and the "Analyze all choices during update diagram andgenerate preprocessor conditionals"? parameter of the Variant Sourceor Variant Sink block is selected, code generated from the model producescompilation errors.
Workaround: Clear the "Analyze all choices during update diagramand generate preprocessor conditionals" parameter of the Variant subsystemcontaining the Dataflow subsystem.

Summary: Code compilation failure for AUTOSAR model initialization function
Description: AUTOSAR model initialization functions might generate code that fails to compile. In a model initialization function, expressions that contain root I/O should be transformed into AUTOSAR access functions. Instead the code generator retains them as global variables without declarations. The resulting code fails to compile.

Summary: Simulink Test report generation fails when a parameter override in a test case contains an expression?that needs to be evaluated
Description: If a parameter override contains an expression in a test case, Simulink Test report generation can fail.? For example, if a parameter override value is a Simulink.Parameter , Simulink Test report generation can cause an error: Error using stm.internal.getTcprProperty? newParam?

Summary: Axis tick labels can be positioned incorrectly when using a reverse, log scale
Description: If you create an axis with a log scale and reverse the direction of increasing values, then the tick labels might appear in the wrong position. For example, this code results in an x-axis with tick labels that are not properly positioned. ax = axes; set(ax,'YScale','log','YDir','reverse')   ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a MATLAB Function block with in-place variables
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model that has a MATLAB Function block with the following conditions:  The block has at least one pair of in-place variables and at least one extra input or output variable. This extra variable shares the same data types and dimensions as the in-place variables.  This can result in variables being merged incorrectly in the generated code.? For example, consider a function  x?= foo(x,y)  in a? MATLAB Function block.?x  is a variable used in both input and output.?When this bug occurs,  y  and  x  could be wrongly merged in the generated code.

Summary: Error after converting a function-call subsystem to a referenced model
Description: When you convert a subsystem to a model and the top model uses a configuration reference, the generated model will use the same configuration reference. If you replace the subsystem with the generated model and the configuration reference does not work for referenced models, you receive an error.

Summary: Inconsistent tunable parameter behaviors between block dialog and command line in rapid accelerator mode and external mode
Description: If a block parameter's value is set to a workspace variable, it cannot be modified in the block dialog during simulation of a model in rapid accelerator mode or external mode. However, if you use?set_param ,?the value changes but it is reflected only on the Simulink canvas. The change does not affect the currently-running simulation results.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subscripted assignment of fi objects with non-zero bias may not match with Simulink, Embedded Coder, MATLAB Coder, and?fiaccel?
Description: When using subscripted assignment to convert scalar values with built-in integer type (int8, uint8, int16, uint16, int32, uint32, int64, uint64) to fi objects that use non-zero bias, the MATLAB interpreted execution may differ from Simulink, Embedded Coder, MATLAB Coder, and?fiaccel.  The following MATLAB function shows this issue.? function y = foo(u)  ? y = fi(1,1,32,7.3616,-1.9498);  ? y(1) = int32(u);  end?  In MATLAB, the function returns the following result.? >> foo(10)  ans =?  ? ? 12.7734  ? ? ? ? ? DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: slope and bias scaling  ? ? ? ? ? ? Signedness: Signed  ? ? ? ? ? ? WordLength: 32  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?Slope: 7.3616  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Bias: -1.9498  Using?fiaccel , the same function produces the following result (Simulink, Embedded Coder, and MATLAB Coder return the same result as fiaccel ).? >> fiaccel foo -args 10?  >> foo_mex(10)  ans =?  ? ? 5.4118  ? ? ? ? ? DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: slope and bias scaling  ? ? ? ? ? ? Signedness: Signed  ? ? ? ? ? ? WordLength: 32  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?Slope: 7.3616  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Bias: -1.9498

Summary: Returning a value from a fill hole method crashes MATLAB
Description: If a fill hole method returns a value, MATLAB crashes. For example, the following definition of a method to fill an Author hole unnecessarily returns its input argument, which crashes MATLAB when the method is executed: function obj = fillAuthor(obj)    append(obj, obj.Author);end
Workaround: Do not return a value from a fill hole method.

Summary: AUTOSAR receiver interface ports with data access mode QueuedExplicitReceive are flagged incorrectly for missing error ports
Description: When an AUTOSAR receiver interface port is configured using data access mode QueuedExplicitReceive , Model Advisor check "Check for missing error ports in AUTOSAR receiver interfaces" (check ID: mathworks.misra.AutosarReceiverInterface ) flags this port incorrectly. Receiver interface ports with data access mode QueuedExplicitReceive  are not allowed to have matching error ports. Code generation fails when you add an error port as recommended.

Summary: Linear interpolation in scatteredInterpolant might lose accuracy with degenerate sample points
Description: In rare circumstances, linear interpolation in?scatteredInterpolant? might lose accuracy when there are degenerate sample points, such as: 2-D sample points (x,y)  which almost lie on the same line. 3-D sample points (x,y,z)  which almost lie on the same plane.  For example, consider the following 2-D sample points (x,y)  which almost lie on the same line. The first two sample points (1,1)  and (16,4)  fall exactly on the line y = (x+4)/5 , while the third sample point (1001+1e-12,201)  almost lies on the same line (the 1e-12  term forces a very small deviation from this line). x = [1 16 1001+1e-12];y = [1 4 201];v = [1 2 3];F = scatteredInterpolant(x',y',v','linear');  Linear interpolation at the query point (xq,yq) = (6,2)  should return the interpolation result vq = 4/3:  xq = 6;yq = 2;vq = F(xq,yq)vq =? ? 1.3244  However, the result is 1.3244  instead of the expected 1.3333 .? Optionally, also visualize the degenerate 2-D sample points (x,y)  and the query point (xq,yq) : plot(x,y,'o-','DisplayName','Sample Points (x,y)')hold onplot(xq,yq,'r*','DisplayName','Query Point (xq,yq)')legend

Summary: Exporting a maximized figure causes the figure size to change and generates improperly sized output
Description: Maximizing a figure and then printing, saving, or opening the Print Preview dialog causes the figure to shrink to the previous size. The layout of the printed or saved figure matches the resized figure window instead of the maximized figure window.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing a While Iterator Subsystem block
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model containing a While Iterator Subsystem block. Inside the subsystem, the While Iterator block output port connects to a Unit Delay block. In the While Iterator Block Parameters dialog box, the Show iteration number port ? parameter is selected.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, set the?Optimize Global Data Access?parameter to?None?or at the MATLAB command prompt enter this command:??set_param(bdroot, 'GlobalVariableUsage', 'none')

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator can?produce wrong answers for reusable export-function model that includes reusable subsystem that uses shared local data store
Description: The code generator can generate incorrect code for a model?when all of these conditions are true: The model is an export-function model. If the model is a referenced model, model configuration parameter?Total number of instances allowed per top model  ?parameter is set to?Multiple.  If the model is a top model, model configuration parameter  Code interface packaging   is set to  Reusable function.  The model includes multiple instances of an atomic subsystem with block parameter Function packaging  set to Auto  or?Reusable function .  The atomic subsystem includes?a Data Store Memory block?with block parameter Share across model instances   selected . The generated subsystem function is called from multiple call sites.  When these conditions exist, multiple calls to the generated subsystem function all access the same shared local data store instance instead of accessing unique instances of the data store, which can result in wrong answers.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model?with a Unit Delay block inside an Enable subsystem
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Unit Delay block inside an Enable subsystem. The Unit Delay block connects to the output port of the Enable subsystem. A Reusable  custom storage class is specified on the input, output or internal state of the Unit Delay block or the the model parameter Optimize global data access? is set to Use global to hold temporary results .?
Workaround: set_param(bdroot, 'GlobalVariableUsage', 'none') and/or remove the Reusable custom storage class from the Unit Delay block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model with an in-place MATLAB Function block and customized code interface
Description: Incorrectly generated code is possible for a?model that meets these conditions:In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the System target file   is set to ert.tlc . The model contains a MATLAB Function block that connects to a pair of model-level root input and output ports. The MATLAB Function block input/output pair that connect to the root-level input and output ports contain an in-place specification (that is, they contain the same name.) In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Code interface packaging  parameter is set to Reusable function .? In the generated code, the root-level input/output pair that connect to the MATLAB Function block have different names.
Workaround: Remove the in-place specification from the MATLAB Function block or using function prototype control specify the same name for the root-level input and output ports that connect to the MATLAB Function block.

Summary: The Inherit sample rate from input?parameter on MIMO Fading Channel and SISO Fading Channel blocks does not work when checked
Description: When the Inherit sample rate from input  parameter is checked, the MIMO Fading Channel and SISO Fading Channel blocks erroneously get the signal sampling rate from the Sample rate (Hz)  parameter, instead of deriving it from the input signal. As the default value of the?Sample rate (Hz) ?parameter is 1, the channel may erroneously behave frequency-flat while it is expected to be frequency-selective for an input signal sampling rate of much larger than 1.?
Workaround: Uncheck the Inherit sample rate from input?parameter, calculate the signal sampling rate using the following script, and specify the?Sample rate (Hz)?parameter to the Rs variable value. To run the script, replace modelName by the name of the Simulink model where the channel block is and replace chanBlockName by the name of the channel block.?modelName([],[],[],'compile');inDim = getfield(get_param(chanBlockName, 'CompiledPortDimensions'), 'Inport');Ts = get_param(chanBlockName, 'CompiledSampleTime');Rs = inDim(2)/Ts(1);modelName([],[],[],'term');

Summary: Simulink may crash when a loop contains a nonvirtual bus nested in a virtual bus
Description: Updating the diagram, simulating, and generating code may crash Simulink when a?loop contains a nonvirtual bus nested in a virtual bus. Stack Trace (from fault):[  0] 0x000000012b587140    bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+05337408 SLGlue::WindowingService::~WindowingService+00174416[  1] 0x00000001456d3b5b    bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+08993627 slcomp::slCheckDimsForNDSignals+00001755[  2] 0x00000001456ce511    bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+08971537 slcomp::ReportDimsBlockMsg+00006081[  3] 0x00000001456cf558    bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+08975704 slcomp::ReportDimsBlockMsg+00010248[  4] 0x00000001456d0a8a    bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+08981130 slcomp::ReportDimsBlockMsg+00015674[  5] 0x00000001456cbe4f    bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+08961615 slcomp::GetVectorMatrixConvMsgStatus+00004815[  6] 0x00000001456ce6ed    bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+08972013 slcomp::ReportDimsBlockMsg+00006557[  7] 0x00000001456d485d    bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+08996957 slcomp::slePropagateArrayDimensionsAndFrameData+00001389[  8] 0x00000001450cd599    bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+02676121 SLCompPropagation+00000665

Summary: readtable detects trailing empty fields on the first line of the file as text
Description: If the first line of a text file ends in a delimiter followed by an empty field, then readtable? returns the variable after the delimiter as text. For example, if a file text.csv  contains the following lines: Var1,Var2,Var3  A,1,  B,3,4  Then,?readtable  reads the data as follows.?  T = readtable('test.csv')  T =  ? 2ˇÁ3 table  ? ? Var1? ? Var2? ? Var3  ? ? ____? ? ____? ? ____  ? ? 'A'? ? ? 1? ? ? ''??  ? ? 'B'? ? ? 3? ? ? '4'?
Workaround: Use detectImportOptions with readtable:>> opts = detectImportOptions(filename)>> T = readtable(filename,opts)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup Table might produce incorrect answers for fixed-point?Table data with non-trivial slope and Linear Lagrange interpolation method
Description: Lookup table blocks might produce mismatched results between simulation and generated code when all of the following conditions are true:  Table data ?is of a fixed-point type? The fixed-point type has a slope value not equal to 1 Interpolation method is set to Linear Lagrange?   The following blocks are affected: n-D Lookup Table 1-D Lookup Table 2-D Lookup Table Interpolation using Prelookup?  You are more likely to encounter this problem when the table dimension is greater than?3 or?the slope is?much smaller or much larger than 1.

Summary: Access violation sporadically happens on "locRegisterSampleTimeSpecAllBlk" in "slrootgraphtstable.cpp"
Description: Access violation sporadically happens related to bus expansion on a single rate block when sample time?to block information is cached. The problem could be reproduced by running?a test points on win64  about 100 times.

Summary: MAT-files resaved by Root Inport Mapper are larger than original MAT-files
Description: The Root Inport Mapper?resaves MAT-files?in 7.3 format,?resulting?in MAT-file?sizes that are much larger than the original MAT-file.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when using add_line to add a line between connection ports
Description: MATLAB might crash when you perform these steps: Load a model by using the?load_system ?function. Without opening the loaded model, add a line between two of its connection ports by using the add_line  function.
Workaround: Before using the?add_line?function,?use open_system to open the?specific subsystem?in which you want to add the line.

Summary: Extra header file (multiword_types.h) included in the generated code
Description: The generated code may contain the directive #inlcude "multiword_types.h"  when it is not required. This may happen when the following conditions are met: Model uses configuration reference. Configuration parameter Maximum word length  (ERTMultiwordLength ) is set to a value other than its default (256). Model is saved in R2016b or before and is loaded in R2017a or later.
Workaround: Manually set configuration parameter Maximum word length in the referenced configuration set to a desired value and then save the changes to the MAT-file or the data dictionary.

Summary: Models with Implicit ODE solvers may simulate slowly when the model Jacobian pattern is dense and 'Solver Jacobian Method' is set to 'auto'
Description: Simulink models that have specified an ODE solver that requires Jacobian matrix for integration may be impacted by a simulation slowdown compared to the previous releases when the model Jacobian matrix has a dense (full) pattern and the Solver Jacobian Method is set to 'auto '. The following solvers are affected by this issue: ode15s, ode23t, ode23tb, ode23s and ode14x .
Workaround: If you observe a slowdown, you can explicitly specify the Solver Jacobian Method as detailed below instead of using 'auto'.?In the Solver pane of the model Configuration Parameters, set the Solver Jacobian Method in the 'Solver details' section to 'Full perturbation'.Alternatively, you can also set Solver Jacobian Method parameter for a model named 'myModel' that is open or loaded using the set_param on the MATLAB command line as follows:set_param('myModel', 'SolverJacobianMethodControl', 'FullPerturbation');

Summary: svd can return imaginary values when called using the syntax sigma = svd(A)
Description: When you pass a vpa matrix to?svd ?and request only a vector of singular values as output, the algorithm can return imaginary values.
Workaround: Instead of requesting the vector of singular values as output, request the diagonal matrix of singular values (the second output) and take its diagonal entries. For example:  [~,S,~] = svd(A);  diag(S)where A is a vpa matrix.

Summary: Models with custom code might not error on the subsequent simulation if a change is made with source files after the first run
Description: When a Simulink model with valid custom code that is used in Stateflow or a MATLAB Function block runs with no issues, and a change is made with the source files and the model is simulated again, Simulink does not produce an error.?
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following steps:Close the model.Delete the slprj folder from the model working folder.Simulate the model again.

Summary: ImageDatastore? returns an error when the last file in the Files property is deleted
Description: If you delete the last file in the Files  property of ImageDatastore , this error occurs:? Error using audioDatastore/ subsasgn (line 364)    Index exceeds array bounds.   For example, this code will return an error: ds = imageDatastore({});?  ds.Files =??'corn.tif';  ds.Files(1) = [];?

Summary: The Integer-Output RS Decoder HDL Optimized wrongly marks input frames as uncorrectable when there is a transmission error in the last parity word of the frame.
Description: When a transmission error occurs on the last parity word in a frame, the Integer-Output RS Decoder HDL Optimized block is not able to correct the error.  The RS algorithm should be able to correct errors in up to (n-k)/2 words in any location within the message. The block asserts the errOut signal to indicate that the error was not corrected. This bug causes the overall uncorrectable error rate to be higher. If the system retransmits on errors, overall system throughput isreduced.

Summary: Error when running parsim on a non-local cluster for a model using Simulink Project
Description: Running parallel simulations using parsim on a non-local cluster for a model which is inside a Simulink Project but has file dependencies outside of the Simulink Project throws a java.lang.NullPointerException. Java exception occurred:?java.lang.NullPointerException?at?com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.archiving.exportfilters.FileExportFilter.getAllParentFoldersUpToRoot(FileExportFilter.java:33)?at?com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.archiving.exportfilters.FileExportFilter.modify(FileExportFilter.java:47)?at?com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.archiving.ProjectExport.applyProjectFilters(ProjectExport.java:100)?at?com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.archiving.ProjectExport.getProjectArchiver(ProjectExport.java:52)?at?com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.archiving.ProjectExport$1.archive(ProjectExport.java:44)?at?com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.archiving.ProjectExport$1.archive(ProjectExport.java:39)?at?com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.matlab.api.MatlabAPIProjectManagerFacade.export(MatlabAPIProjectManagerFacade.java:469)?at? com.mathworks.toolbox.slproject.project.matlab.api.MatlabAPIMatlabProjectManager.export(MatlabAPIMatlabProjectManager.java:235)??
Workaround: Add files?identified as a model dependency to the Simulink Project.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Output of disp may?be?different?between MATLAB?and generated MEX?when?disp is passed a property of an object that is conditionally defined as another object
Description: Generally, the code generator?produces an error?if your MATLAB code uses?a property that has not been previously defined along all potential execution paths.?However, the code generator?does not?produce an error if all of these conditions are true: An?object X had a property that is?defined along some, but not all, execution paths to be some other object?Y.? The?object X?is passed to a function?in which the?property Y?is directly used by disp .   In such case, the?behavior that occurs at the use of Y in the generated code is undefined. For example, consider this MATLAB function: function exampleFunction(in)obj = MyClass;if in > 0    obj.p1 = MyClass;endobj.displayIt();end Here the class MyClass  is defined as: classdef MyClass < handle    properties        p1    end    methods        function displayIt(obj)            disp(obj.p1);        end    endend Running exampleFunction ?in MATLAB produces?no output. However, if you run the generated?MEX?function, the disp  function displays this output: MyClass?with properties:  p1: []  This issue affects your code only if?disp ?directly uses the property that is an object rather than accessing one of its members.?So, for example, this issue?would not occur if disp  used?obj.p1.p1  in the above example code.

Summary: Very large CTF files when compiling applications that include features from Deep Learning Toolbox
Description: If you compile an application using MATLAB Compiler, and that application uses features from Deep Learning Toolbox, then the CTF file can be much larger than expected, e.g., it can be 300Mb rather than 50-70Mb.

Summary: Performance regression when large number of errors or warnings are reported in Simulation Manager when using parsim
Description: When running approximately greater than 500 simulations, if all or a large fraction of simulations complete with errors or warnings, additional time is spent updating the status of simulations in the Simulation Manager. This results in slowing down the task of executing simulations by up to three times slower than when the Simulation Manager is not used.? The status of the simulations as shown on the Simulation Manager is still accurate. The results and performance of the simulations themselves are not affected.?

Summary: Test harnesses for a model might not load correctly after the associated harnessInfo file is merged
Description: Test harnesses stored externally?might fail to load when all of these conditions occur: Multiple test harnesses are present in a model Two or more test harnesses are edited by different users and submitted to source control The <modelname>_harnessInfo.xml  file, which maintains metadata about the test harnesses, is manually merged?  Subsequent harness operations, including opening or updating metadata, can fail with warnings similar to the following: The test harness <test harness name> was saved externally but the owner block?<path to the block that owns the harness> was not found in <model name>. This  test harness will not be created. Re-save <model name> to update the harnessInfo metadata file <harness info file name>.

Summary: Mac OSX MATLAB startup might show warning about?psn_0_xxxxxx
Description: When starting MATLAB on Mac OSX, you might see a warning similar to " Warning: Unrecognized command line option: psn_0_69649 ." on the MATLAB Command Window. ?The number psn_0_xxxxxx  varies.
Workaround: The warning is harmless and may be ignored.

Summary: Warning when configuration set is loaded from MAT-file and?CodeProfilingInstrumentation?is 'on'
Description: When you load a configuration set from a MAT-file that was saved by using an earlier MATLAB release, and CodeProfilingInstrumentation  is 'on' , you see this warning: The value 'on' is not supported for 'CodeProfilingInstrumentation'. 'on' is replaced by 'Detailed'. If you want to profile only atomic subsystems and referenced models, ?set 'CodeProfilingInstrumentation' to 'Coarse'.

Summary: Memory leak in imrotate?for rotation angles ~= 90 when the IPP library is used
Description: For rotation angles ~= 90, and when the IPP library is used, the MEX implementation of imrotate  does not de-allocate dynamically allocated memory, thus?causing a memory leak.

Summary: Variant Subsystem can generate unused zero initialization for local variables
Description: Embedded Coder can generate unused zeroinitialization when all these conditions are true for the Variant Subsystem: Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals  is enabled.  Variant subsystem is in dead code path.  This results in some dead code in the generated code.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when executing a script from command line that has graphics functionality, such as plot.
Description: MATLAB can crash when executing a script from the Linux or macOS command line that contains graphics functionality. ?Two conditions are necessary to trigger this crash: Automating MATLAB in a way that runs code and then automatically quits, without displaying a MATLAB desktop environment. Having an open figure at the time MATLAB tries to shut down. For example: ? echo "figure;" | xargs matlab -r  If the script does not have any graphics functionality it does not crash, and works as expected. ?For example, you can just have one line in a script, such as rand(4) .?
Workaround: To work around this issue, ensure there are no open figures before the script exits MATLAB.For example, modify the above simplified reproduction steps as follows:  echo "figure;close" | xargs matlab -r

Summary: Blank page follows last section in a report
Description: A blank page, created by Report API?mlreportgen.report.Section  reporter,?follows the last section in a Word report.
Fixed In: R2017b Update 8, R2018a Update 4, R2018b, R2019a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect behavior for Rate Transition block
Description: The Rate Transition block behaves incorrectly when the following?conditions are all true: The block?Ensure deterministic data transfer  check box is selected. The block input rate is faster than its output rate. The block output port?is directly connected to the input port of another Rate Transition block that does not have the Ensure data integrity during data transfer  check box selected and whose output rate is the same rate as?the input rate of the first block. The configuration parameter Treat each discrete rate as a separate task  is selected.  ?The behavior of this block is consistent yet incorrect both in simulation and in the generated code.?
Workaround: Select the?Ensure data integrity during data transfer?check box for all Rate Transition blocks in the model.

Summary: When a Function-Call Subsystem's?Outport block specifies a different sample time than its function-call sample time, Simulink will have wrong answers in both Simulation and code generation.
Description: If the Outport block of a Function-Call Subsystem specifies a sample time that is different from its function-call sample time, Simulink should not allow this case and error out. But Simulink?does not error out, and therefore leads to wrong answers in both Simulation and code generation.  Specify the same sample time for both the Outport block and its function-call, or leave it as -1.

Summary: Out of memory error when plotting test and simulation outputs in the generated report
Description: If you select a large number of simulation outputs to plot in a report, report generation can cause an out of memory error.

Summary: MATLAB System block may generate uninitialized variables for System objects inheriting from matlab.mixin.Nondirect
Description: MATLAB System block may generate uninitialized variables for System objects inheriting from matlab.mixin.Nondirect.  This happens when the System object uses nondirect feedthrough has no direct feed-through and both the OutputImpl? and UpdateImpl methods are implemented.? When this happens, the generated code may contain uninitialized variables and there is a warning message link at the bottom of the model. When you click the link, the message is?displayed on the Diagnostics Viewer. However, there are no visual warnings on the MATLAB command window.?

Summary: Wrong simulation results when blocks combining output and update methods are in an algebraic loop.
Description: Blocks that combine their output and update methods such as function-call subsystems will lead to incorrect simulation results when they are part of an algebraic loop.?

Summary: Duplicate coverage results from Simulink test iterations run with MATLAB Unit Test
Description: If you have a test case in Simulink Test that:  Is configured with iterations, and Is set to collect coverage (using Simulink Coverage)  And you run the test case as part of a test suite in MATLAB Unit Test, duplicate coverage results are returned for the same model. The number of coverage results equals the number of test iterations for the parent test case. This behavior may also occur for results at the test suite or test file level in Simulink Test. The expected behavior is that coverage is aggregated across test iterations for previous results in the test hierarchy.?

Summary: Model Advisor reports a warning and recommends a change to?a read-only configuration parameter
Description: While running the Check safety-related optimization settings for data initialization  check on a model, Model Advisor reports a warning, and recommends a change to?the model?configuration?parameter Remove root level I/O zero initialization.  This parameter is grayed-out because it is?a read-only parameter.This bug occurs?for a?model?in which the?System target file? is set to autosar.tlc .

Summary: importdata function returns a warning when reading multimedia or text files
Description: When you use the importdata  function to read multimedia files or text files,? MATLAB? issues warnings.   For example, when you read a video file:  data = importdata('myvideofile.mp4') ? The following warning is generated: Warning: A value of class "matlab.internal.video.PluginManager" was indexed with no subscripts?specified. Currently the result of this operation is the indexed value itself, but in a future release, it will be an error.?> In matlab.internal.VideoReader/createChannel (line 495)? In matlab.internal.VideoReader (line 192)? In VideoReader/init (line 598)? In VideoReader (line 171)? In finfo>getVideoInfo (line 205)? In finfo>openAsMultimediaFile (line 184)? In finfo (line 79)? In importdata (line 133)?  Warning: A value of class "matlab.internal.video.PluginManager" was indexed with no subscripts?specified. Currently the result of this operation is the indexed value itself, but in a future?release, it will be an error.?> In matlab.internal.VideoReader/createChannel (line 495)? In matlab.internal.VideoReader (line 192)? In VideoReader/init (line 598)? In VideoReader (line 171)? In importdata (line 191)
Workaround: To work around this issue, suppress the warning using:warning off MATLAB:subscripting:noSubscriptsSpecified?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Variant conditions with arithmetic and/or relational operations generate incorrect code
Description: A Simulink model with Variant blocks may generate incorrect code in any of the following scenarios:   Variant conditions with arithmetic operations   Consider a block with Variant condition? A + B > 255, where?A = uint8(255), B = uint8(2).The expression is evaluated to false during simulation, while the C preprocessor evaluates the same to TRUE.  Variant conditions with relational operations   Consider a block with Variant condition A > B, where?A = uint8(1), B =? int8(-1). The expression is evaluated to true during simulation, while the C preprocessor evaluates the same to FALSE.  Note: This behavior is specific to the scenario where the condition has a?mix of signed/unsigned types.?

Summary: Unexpected error occurs when atomic subchart data mapping results in size mismatch.
Description: When you map atomic subchart data to an expression in the main chart, and the mapping results in a size mismatch, Stateflow might throw the following error: Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: b:\matlab\src\cgir_xform\dom_core\optimization\smartinliner.cpp line 600: Function inlining failed. ". Please report this to MathWorks if you can cause it to recur Component:Simulink | Category:Blockdiagram error
Workaround: Change the size of atomic subchart data to match the size of the expression in the main chart.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect value during simulation of Stateflow chart containing enumeration data or outputs
Description: Stateflow might use the incorrect value for enumeration data or outputs during simulation of a model that meets all of the following conditions: The enumeration is of a built-in integer type other than int32 The enumeration is set to an Exported DataScope The enumeration is used in both a Stateflow Chart and a MATLAB Function Block Variables or signals utilizing the enumeration are NOT explicitly written in all states within the Stateflow Chart  For example, see issue_model_1799390.slx  in within the attached issue_example_1799390.zip . At both time step = 0 and time step = 1 the output of the Stateflow Chart should be SomeThing.ModeB . At time step = 1 there is an incorrect output value of SomeThing.ModeA .

Summary: Content of live script lost when opened in MATLAB Online using Internet Explorer.
Description: If a live script containing code examples is opened in MATLAB Online using Internet Explorer, the content of the live script is lost.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Discrete FIR Filter block with Adaptive Pipelining causes mismatch in validation model
Description: When using the Discrete FIR Filter block, a numeric mismatch can occur after HDL code generation if Adaptive Pipelining  is enabled and non-zero values are set on the?MultiplierInputPipeline  or MultiplierOutputPipeline  HDL block property options. The block then reports more latency than is actually introduced during optimizations (i.e block reports 8 cycles but actually only has 6 pipeline stages in the generated code).
Workaround: MultiplierInputPipeline or MultiplierOutputPipeline options need not be specified when using Adaptive Pipelining. Adaptive pipelining introduces the appropriate pipelines around multipliers for proper DSP mapping and frequency. Set these options to 0 if using Adaptive pipelining.

Summary: Simstruct macros related to data-type properties do not work in Rapid Accelerator mode.
Description: Prior to R2018b, user-written S-Functions that contain calls to the following simstruct macros were not compatible with Rapid Accelerator mode: ssRegisterDataType ssGetDataTypeSize ssGetNumDataTypes ssSetDataTypeZero ssGetDataTypeZero ssGetDataTypeId ssGetDataTypeName  In releases prior to R2018b, use of the macros will cause build failures or run-time errors in Rapid Accelerator mode.?

Summary: Missing VHDL library name in Xilinx? Vivado??IP core generated using IP Core Generation?workflow
Description: When you run the IP Core Generation  workflow for your Simulink model with Xilinx? Vivado? as the Synthesis tool and the VHDL library name specified as a value other than the default value work, the IP core definition file component.xml  for the generated Vivado?IP core does not contain the top-level VHDL library name. The custom library name is missing because:  For example, if ledgroup  is the library name, the generated component.xml  file must have a line containing the tag logicalName  corresponding to the ? VHDL library name. <spirit:file><spirit:name>hdl/vhdl/led_count_ip_addr_decoder.vhd</spirit:name><spirit:fileType>vhdlSource</spirit:fileType><spirit:logicalName>ledgroup</spirit:logicalName></spirit:file> The custom library name is missing because the ?logicalName? is not included in the  component.xml  file of the generated IP core .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect data logging for certain fixed point types
Description: Chart data in Simulation Data Inspector might be incorrectly logged with the wrong type when all of the following conditions are true: The data is of fixed point type and has slope and bias scaling The total slope of the fixed point type is in the range of 1 to 2

Summary: Setting an FMU Import block to direct debug logs to file might crash Simulink in multithreaded simulation.
Description: Setting an FMU Import block to to direct debug logs to file?and running the simulation might crash Simulink if the simulation is multithreaded. The crash log may include the following:? MATLAB: management.cpp:723: find: Assertion `find: no active context for type 'matrix::ErrorMode'? [ 5] 0x00007fe62a267798 bin/glnxa64/libmx.so+00976792 _ZN6matrix12getErrorModeEv+00000024[ 6] 0x00007fe40e1ee0ed bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_services.so+02142445?[ 7] 0x00007fe40e1ef38a bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_services.so+02147210 _ZN14CMatlabCommand7executeEbN6matrix9ErrorModeE+00000058?[ 8] 0x00007fe40e1efa95 bin/glnxa64/libmwsl_services.so+02149013 _Z17slsvFEVAL_NoThrowiPP11mxArray_tagiS1_PKcPvbN6matrix9ErrorModeEPSt10unique_ptrIS_15TMxArrayDeleterE+00000181? ?[ 14] 0x00007fe25c606f87 bin/glnxa64/libmwfmubase.so+01019783 _ZN15FMUBasePrmDescs13getFMULogFileB5cxx11EPK7SLBlocki+00000087??
Workaround: Use one of the following workarounds:Turn off file logging for the FMU import block:>> set_param(gcb, 'FMUDebugLogging', 'off')?Turn off multithreaded simulation from the command line:>> set_param(gcs, 'MultiThreadCoSim', 'off')?

Summary: A Function Caller block inside a Dataflow subsystem may cause unexpected behavior.
Description: If a model contains a Function Caller block inside a Dataflow subsystem, update diagram might cause unexpected behavior, such as a crash.
Workaround: Move the Function Caller block outside of the Dataflow subsystem.

Summary: Unexpected vehicle motion at stopped conditions?
Description: If you configure the vehicle with the Combined Slip Wheel 2DOF  block, the Magic Formula tire force calculations at low speeds can result in unexpected vehicle motion at stopped conditions.

Summary: Standardization of edge case behavior for rem and mod
Description: In R2019a , the?rem ?and?mod ?functions consistently handle finite values in the first argument and infinity in the second argument. In previous releases, these input combinations produced?NaN ?values. The new behavior provides continuity in the computed result as the second argument tends to infinity.? For example:? >> format short e >> a = [1 -1 1 -1 1 -1 1 -1]; >> b = [Inf Inf -Inf -Inf 1e300 1e300 -1e300 -1e300]; >> rem(a,b) ans =  ???? 1??? -1???? 1??? -1???? 1??? -1???? 1??? -1  >> mod(a,b) ans = ?? 1.0000e+00????????? Inf???????? -Inf? -1.0000e+00?? 1.0000e+00? 1.0000e+300 -1.0000e+300? -1.0000e+00  In R2018b or earlier releases , the behavior was: >> rem(a,b)ans =??  ? ? ?NaN?? NaN?? NaN?? NaN???? 1??? -1???? 1??? -1 >> mod(a,b)ans = ????????? NaN????????? NaN????????? NaN????????? NaN?? 1.0000e+00? 1.0000e+300 -1.0000e+300? -1.0000e+00

Summary: Default hole styles can fail in Word header and footer templates
Description: A Word report may ignore default header or footer? styles specified by holes in the template used to generate the report. This happens when a specified style name contains? a non-alphanumeric character, such as _. For example, a generated report ignores a default header hole style named Body_1.
Workaround: Use alphanumeric characters for default style names, e.g., Body1, specified by header and footer template holes.

Summary: Library link is broken when add_block is used within?mask initialization
Description: When you use the?add_block  function in mask initialization code for masks on certain blocks, the library link might be broken when you insert the masked block into your model. This happens for :  MATLAB Function Block? Stateflow Chart State Transition Table Chart Truth Table chart? Discrete Event Chart

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answers can occur when a Model block referencing a multi-instance export-function model is in SIL mode
Description: Software-in-the-loop (SIL) mode simulation of a Model block?can result in wrong answers if all of the following conditions are true: The Model block references an export-function model whose?Total number of instances allowed per top model ?parameter is set to ?Multiple  or whose   Code interface packaging    parameter is set to  Reusable function . The Model block has a model initialize port that receives an initialize function call. The referenced model contains at least one block whose initial condition is zero.

Summary: Exporting a clock from a session that contains channels from multiple devices might generate an error
Description: When using a session containing channels from multiple NI-DAQ devices, exporting a clock using?addClockConnection ?and then starting a clocked operation with either?startForeground  or?startBackground , might generate the following error message: NI Error -89120: Source terminal to be routed could not be found on the device. Make sure the terminal name is valid for the specified device. Refer to Measurement & Automation Explorer for valid terminal names. Property: DAQmx_SampClk_Src Property: DAQmx_SampClk_ActiveEdge Source Device: Dev4 Source Terminal: Default Task Name: _unnamedTask<1F7> Status Code: -89120  For example, if a session contains channels from two different devices Dev1 and Dev2, the following code results in the error.   s = daq.createSession('ni');   addAnalogInputChannel(s,'Dev1','ai0','Voltage');   addAnalogInputChannel(s,'Dev2','ai0','Voltage');    addClockConnection(s,'Dev1/PFI0','External','ScanClock');   startForeground(s)
Workaround: To work around this issue, explicitly add a clock connection between the devices in the session. For example, modify the sample code from the description to this:  s = daq.createSession('ni');   addAnalogInputChannel(s,'Dev1','ai0','Voltage');   addAnalogInputChannel(s,'Dev2','ai0','Voltage');    addClockConnection(s,'Dev1/PFI0','External','ScanClock');   addClockConnection(s,'Dev1/PFI0','Dev2/PFI0','ScanClock');  startForeground(s)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing a For Iterator Subsystem block
Description: The generated code may be incorrect for a model containing a For Iterator Subsystem block. Inside this subsystem, a?For Iterator block connects to a Unit Delay block. In the For Iterator Block Parameters dialog box, the?Show iteration variable ?is selected.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, set the Optimize Global Data Access parameter to None or at the MATLAB command prompt enter this command:set_param(bdroot, 'GlobalVariableUsage', 'none')

Summary: Code generator produces uncompilable C++ code for model that uses a Rate Transition block with multitasking execution
Description: The code generator produces C++ code?that does not compile for a model when all of the following conditions exist: The model includes a Rate Transition block that is configured with block parameter Ensure data integrity during data transfer? selected and Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)  cleared.  The input signal of the Rate Transition block is of an aggregate type (for example, a complex or nonvirtual bus type). Model configuration parameter Treat each discrete rate as a separate task  is selected and Language  is set to C++ .?  The compiler returns an error message?similar to this: file-path \file-name .cpp(61): error C2679: binary '=': no operator found which takes a right-hand operand of type 'volatile ParamBus' (or there is no acceptable conversion)  \
Workaround: Do one of the following:Change the data type of the input signal of the Rate Transition block to a virtual bus or another non-aggregate type.For the Rate Transition block, select block parameter Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay).If you cannot?select?block parameter?Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)?block, clear block parameter Ensure data integrity during data transfer.If model configuration parameter Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer is selected, set parameter Deterministic data transfer to Always.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Fixed-point arithmetic driven by constant blocks results in HDL code mismatches
Description: For certain operations driven by constant fixed-point values, HDL Coder attempts to optimize away the arithmetic with new constant values. In some cases, the resulting constant value has a quantization error which can result in a mismatch of the validation model and/or HDL code. In R2019a, HDL Coder avoids the quantization error by disabling the optimization in cases where a mismatch would occur.
Workaround: Follow the workaround listed in this?bug report?to avoid the issue in affected releases.

Summary: Model checksums in the System Design Description report (SDD) can differ from the checksums provided in the Tool QualificationReport for Simulink Report Generator
Description: When a Simulink model contains? MATLAB function blocks,?Stateflow charts, or?Truth Table blocks, the model checksums in the System Design Description report (SDD) can differ from the checksums provided in the Tool Qualification Report for Simulink Report Generator. The content of SDD is not affected.

Summary: SIL/PIL simulation produces incorrect error message when data type replacement provides a Boolean mapping for boolean
Description: Software-in-the-loop (SIL) and processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulations do not support the provision of a Boolean alias type for a data type that has a boolean  base type. The Embedded Coder documentation topic "SIL and PIL Limitations" provides a subsection "Data Type Replacement". However, the subsection does not cover the mapping of Boolean to boolean . The subsection is as follows: "The software does not support replacement data type names that you define for the built-in data type boolean if these names map to the int or uint built-in data type."

Summary: Code inspection might return "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" for models that require the CodeWarrior compiler
Description: Code inspection returns "Failed to verify" for a component that should return "Verified" for models that contain custom storage classes and that require the CodeWarrior  compiler for a FreeScale PowerPC target.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Product block might generate wrong?answer for complex integer?0 divided by 0
Description: The Product block might generate wrong answers when these?conditions are all true: There?are one or more division operations, as specified by the Number of Inputs  parameter, for example,?/*/.  The Multiplication  parameter is set?to Element-wise(.*).  The output of the Product block is complex.?  For division operationsAll inputs have the same built-in integer type, such?as int8 , uint8 , int16 , uint16 , int32 , and so forth. The denominator is complex and has the value 0 for its real and imaginary parts.    This issue affects both simulation and code generation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In a Moore chart, assignments to output variables might be incorrectly eliminated
Description: In a Moore chart, when an output to a variable is assigned twice in the same time step, and the value of the first assignment is used to update a local variable, the second assignment may be ignored in both simulation and in generated code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Coder incorrectly reuses the code for subsystems
Description: Simulink Coder might incorrectly reuse the code for subsystems under the following circumstances: The subsystems are graphically identical and the blocks inside to the subsystems have identical port data types, sizes and complexities. The subsystems contain virtual Bus Selector blocks. The virtual Bus Selector blocks do not select identical elements form virtual busses that are inputs to the subsystems. When the model is placed in compiled mode, the above mentioned subsystems incorrectly have identical checksums.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Top model SIL and normal mode results do not match if initialize function in referenced model writes to Data Store Memory
Description: A top model software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation does not support an intialize function?that writes?to data stores defined by Data Store Memory (DSM)?blocks: If the writing occurs at the top level, the SIL simulation produces an error. If?the writing occurs?in a referenced model, the SIL simulation does not produce an error but the results do not match normal mode simulation results.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect row-major code for a model containing State Writer block that writes states to Delay block
Description: Embedded Coder can generate incorrect code?when all these conditions are true for a model: Model configuration parameter Array layout  set to 'Row-Major'. ? Model contains a State Writer block which writes states to a Delay block with Delay length  parameter greater than 1. N-Delay block has a nonscalar state value.

Summary: Accessing structure fields can lead to a false red check when the structure is initialized by a stubbed function
Description: In some code-dependent situations, if a structure is initialized by a stubbed function, a red Non-initialized variable  check might be observed on a field of the structure. The access to the first field is orange, but accessing the other fields can lead to a red check. Because the function potentially initializes all structure fields, the accesses should all be orange.
Products: Polyspace Code Prover Server, Polyspace Code Prover

Summary: Logging for verify statements in Simulink Test Real-Time test case may skip samples
Description: When running a Real-Time Test Case in Simulink Test Manager, results for verify statements might skip some samples if data are produced at a high rate on the real-time target. However, the first failure point is still logged.

Summary: Closing certain windows within MATLAB causes MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash after closing certain windows such as App Designer, UIFigures, the Add-On Explorer, the Add-On Manager, and some others. The generated stack trace contains these lines: Stack Trace (from fault):?[??0] 0x00007ffa09004ea4???????????????????????????????????<unknown-module>+00000000?[??1] 0x00007ffa3166ddc5??????????????????????C:\WINDOWS\SYSTEM32\ntdll.dll+00056773

Summary: MATLAB may crash when running models in External mode
Description: A MATLAB crash may occur when running models in External mode. The crash is more likely to happen for models configured with smaller time steps or that run for long time. The issue occurs only on the Windows operating system with certain newer CPU types, and is related to an incompatibility between the real-time binary code generated for these CPUs and the real-time kernel.
Workaround: Follow these steps to work around this issue:Open the compiler configuration file? ? ?edit(fullfile(toolboxdir('sldrt'),'rtw','sldrtclang.mk'))Navigate to line 34, which starts with?LLC :=.?Add the compiler option -mattr=-avx to the end of the line.Save the file. You need administrator privileges to save the file.Rebuild all models affected by this issue before running them.

Summary: Code generated for?Ideal Airspeed Correction block not optimized to exclude unused lookup table data
Description: The Ideal Airspeed Correction block generates code that always contains lookup table data for all airspeeds less than Mach 5, resulting in unnecessarily large compiled executables when one of the following is true: * The block is used for subsonic applications* Method  is set to Equation?

Summary: Mismatch between page boundaries in the output and the Simulink Editor when using tiled printing
Description: When tiled printing is enabled, PDF and paper prints might?use different page boundaries than displayed in the Simulink Editor. The contents of one tile in the Simulink Editor might be split across multiple pages in the print.

Summary: Simulink models with subsystems that call replace_block function fail to compile
Description: Simulink models with subsystems that call the replace_block  function from their mask initialization functions may fail to compile. This occurs if the model is compiled using the Generate S-Function  option. The "Model has compile errors" error message is displayed. This message incorrectly indicates that one or more subsystem output ports are not connected.
Workaround: You can?restore the output connections by extending the mask initialization function?to call?the add_line function.

Summary: Error 'The given key was not present in the dictionary'?occurs when working with instrumentation panels in Simulink Real-Time Explorer
Description: The instrument label error?'The given key was not present in the dictionary' ?can occur when working with instrumentation panels in Simulink Real-Time Explorer.  This error indicates that one or more labels added to instrument panels in Simulink Real-Time Explorer could have a corrupted name.  This label issue causes errors when opening and closing the instrument panel.
Workaround: Resolve the errors for corrupted labels by renaming instruments. Simulink Real-Time Explorer names labels the form "Label<num>", where <num>? is an integer. The numbering starts at "1" and increments by 1 each time a new label is added.? Occasionally, a label may receive a name with a very large value for?<num>? (for example, "Label2147483648").? Use Simulink Real-Time Explorer to find these labels with large values for?<num>?and rename them to avoid errors.

Summary: Propagated signal labels might be incorrect when a referenced model has virtual bus outputs
Description: When a referenced model outputs a virtual bus, the propagated signal labels from the Model block might be incorrect.  These incorrect signal labels might result in errors. For example, if those signals are inputs to a Bus Creator block that requires specific signal names, the mismatch produces an error.?
Workaround: Insert Signal Conversion blocks after the Model block and rename the output signal copies.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model containing Enabled Synchronous Subsystem or Resettable Synchronous Subsystem and?Single output/update function parameter set can generate incorrect C code
Description: Embedded Coder can generate numerically incorrect code when a model meets all the following conditions: Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Interface >?Single output/update function  parameter is selected.  ANDThe model contains an Enabled Synchronous Subsystem, OR? The model contains a Resettable Synchronous Subsystem,?  The results obtained from running this C code can be inaccurate and differ from simulation results.??

Summary: Baselines for iterations not captured programmatically
Description: Baselines for iterations are not being captured or saved when you use Simulink Test's programmatic interface.
Workaround: To capture baselines for iterations, select "Capture Baselines for Iterations" in the Test Manager.

Summary: Compatibility checks fail when Remove reset function parameter is not selected even though code inspection passes
Description: If the model configuration parameter Remove reset function  is not selected, the compatibility checks fail and recommend that you select this parameter even though code inspection passes. Starting in R2018b, verification does not depend on the Remove reset function  parameter setting, so the compatibility checks should not recommend that you select this parameter.

Summary: Initial output values of conditionally executed subsystems are ignored
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may ignore the initial output value of a conditionally executed?subsystem, if the initial output value is inherited from a signal object and the model is set to Simplified initialization mode.
Workaround: Specify the required initial output values of conditionally executed subsystems by using the dialog parameters of Outport blocks.

Summary: Simplifying polyshape?objects does not always remove coincident points or edges
Description: When simplifying a polyshape  object containing exactly coincident points or edges, sometimes these points or edges are not removed as expected. This behavior can occur when creating a polyshape  object with the polyshape  function, or using polyshape  object functions such as?union  or simplify . For example, consider the following code: P = polyshape([0 0; 1 0;?1 1; 0 1]);? % Create unit square?Q =?translate(P,[0 -1]);              % Create square sharing one edge with ProtatedSquares = rotate([P; Q],60);   % Rotate P and Q clockwise 60 degreesR = union(rotatedSquares)             % Compute union of rotated squares The resulting polyshape  object R  has two regions sharing an edge instead of a single region with the common edge removed.?

Summary: Chaining calls to MWArray Get or GetData function might return incorrect results
Description: When an MWArray object is nested within another MWArray object, the Get()  and GetData()  functions might incorrectly act on the parent object when they should be acting on the nested object instead.? A similar problem occurs if the C++ copy constructor is used to create an MWArray object returned by Get() .
Workaround: Each call to Get() and GetData() should be executed separately.? Any MWArray objects returned by Get() should be stored in a local variable using a C++ assignment operator.? The assignment operator does not have the same issue as the copy constructor.The following pseudo-code illustrates the workaround:  mwArray local_object;  local_object = mw_array_object.Get("nested_object",1,1);  // Get() used with C++ assignment operator  local_object.GetData(local_array,10);

Summary: Compilation might fail with the error: too many recursive substitutions of function template signatures
Description: When there are some recursions on function templates, the Polyspace compilation phase of a file can fail with the following error message:   Verifying my_file.cpp (X/Y)    line X: error: too many recursive substitutions of function template signatures   |  template <typename c, typename> auto b() -> decltype(b<c>());
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: GraphicEQ can output signal with wrong gains if SampleRate is tuned after the object was locked
Description: GraphicEQ uses a gain correction matrix to improve the frequency response of the output when the EQ order is the default value of 2. This matrix is not being recomputed when the sampling rate is tuned after the object is locked. For example, generated audio plugins might lock the object with the default sampling rate (44100), so their subsequent attempts to tune the SampleRate  property will not recompute the gain correction matrix.

Summary: Error when a virtual bus or an element of a virtual bus passes through a nonvirtual subsystem
Description: Simulink may throw an error when you simulate your model if either of the following conditions is present:? A virtual bus input to a nonvirtual subsystem connects directly to an Outport block in the subsystem. An element of a virtual bus input to a nonvirtual subsystem connects to an Outport block in the subsystem directly or through one or more Bus Selector and/or Bus Creator blocks.?  ?The error message may reference mixed numeric types. Specific errors include the following: Input port cannot accept mixed numeric types. Input port 1 of 'model/Subsystem' expects a real or complex signal. However, it is driven by a signal that includes both real and complex elements. This can be usually fixed by inserting a Signal Conversion block with the 'Signal copy' option selected, at the output of block 'model/Subsystem/In1'.? Alternatively, if this input port is within a function-call subsystem, consider checking the 'Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs' option.   An error occurred while propagating mixed numeric type from 'model/Bus Creator', output port 1.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block after the Inport block that receives the virtual bus input to the nonvirtual subsystem, and set the Output parameter of the Signal Conversion block to Signal Copy.

Summary: Calling?exit or quit from a timer callback may leak MATLAB process or crash MATLAB on exit
Description: On Unix-based systems, the following command leaves MATLAB? running: t = timer('TimerFcn','quit','StartDelay',5) start(t)% code to execute    On Windows??systems,?calling?exit  or quit  after starting a timer will cause the?MATLAB session to crash.?For example, the following code may lead to a crash: t = timer('TimerFcn','quit','StartDelay',5) start(t)% code to execute    ?
Workaround: Avoid terminating MATLAB in a timer callback function. To exit MATLAB after a period of time use the timer wait?function:t = timer('StartDelay',5,'TimerFcn',@(~,~){});start(t)% execute code herewait(t)?exitNote that this workaround will not interrupt running MATLAB code.

Summary: Example for?coder.replace?function incorrectly sets?code replacement table entry parameter?ArrayLayout to?ROW_MAJOR
Description: On the?reference page for the coder.replace ? function, https://www.mathworks.com/help/ecoder/ref/coder.replace.html , the example file crl_table_calculate.m ??incorrectly sets the?code replacement table?entry parameter ArrayLayout  to ROW_MAJOR . The correct setting for the parameter is?COLUMN_MAJOR . If you?follow this example as documented, no replacement happens.
Workaround: To produce correct?code for the example, set parameter?ArrayLayout to COLUMN_MAJOR.

Summary: Converting datetime or duration array to categorical can create categories with duplicate names
Description: If you convert a datetime  or duration  array to a?categorical  array, then the categorical  array might have duplicate category names. This result can occur if the display format of the datetime  or duration  array does not display enough information to distinguish?unique values. Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Zero-crossings are not detected for the enable port signal connected to an Enabled and Triggered Subsystem block.
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when it meets the following conditions: Model uses a variable step solver. Model contains an Enabled and Triggered Subsystem block. Input signal to the enable port is pure continuous time, not fixed in minor time step. Input signal to the trigger port is continuous time but fixed in minor time step.?  In this case, zero-crossings are not detected for the enable port, resulting in incorrect simulation results in accelerator and rapid accelerator mode.
